A cold 4-bet from the Big Blind is a powerful move that represents a narrow, premium range (QQ+, AK). This action should force Minni out of the pot and put Elon in a difficult spot with hands like 88, 99, or AJ/AQ. If I am shoved on, I will have to evaluate based on the size, but a 4-bet to 2500 defines the strength of my hand and takes control of the pot.

I opened to 300 from the button and now face a 3‑bet to 900 and a 4‑bet to 2,500. The pot is 3,700 and it costs me 2,200 to call (min‑raise to 4,100). T♠5♠ is a marginal, often‑dominated hand versus the likely 3/4‑bet ranges (QQ+/AK) and my raw equity is well below the ~37% needed to justify a call here. Flash is short‑stacked and can easily shove, which would force a difficult and costly decision. A tiny 4‑bet bluff is thin and likely to be met by a shove or a call from stronger hands, so the +EV play is to conserve chips and fold.

Facing a large 4-bet shove from Flash in the BB (2500 into 3700 pot, leaving himself ~2800 behind from 5300 stack), with Ah8s OOP from SB. While pot odds are favorable (~3.3:1, needing ~23% equity), my hand has marginal equity vs Flash's polarized 4-bet range (likely QQ+, AK, some bluffs like suited broadways or blockers). Ah8s blocks AA/AK but plays poorly postflop OOP against a committed opponent who will often barrel. Flash has shown aggression but this sizing suggests strength more than bluff. Calling risks a multi-street investment with reverse implied odds if behind, and 5-betting all-in is overplay with 137BB deep. Folding preserves stack for better spots in tournament; this is a standard pro fold to avoid tough OOP situations.

3-handed on the button acting first preflop, wide opening range of ~60-70% applies. 84o is playable with position, live cards, and potential for straight draws. Standard open-raise to 3x BB (300) to steal dead money from blinds or play profitably postflop in position. Opponents frequently fold to button aggression based on hand history. Deep stacks allow for postflop flexibility.

In a 3-handed game, Ace-Three offsuit (A3o) is a strong enough hand to defend against a standard Button open. While playing out of position is not ideal, folding is too conservative given the wide range the button typically raises with. I will call the 250 to see a flop, keeping the pot manageable while I evaluate the board, though I must be cautious of the Big Blind (Minni) squeezing behind me.

I’m OOP in the big blind with 4♥3♥ facing a 300 open from the button and the SB called. This is a cheap, speculative suited connector with good post‑flop implied odds, and calling 200 more (300 total) gets me decent pot odds (~22%) to see a flop multiway. Raising to 500 here has low fold equity and risks bloating the pot with a weak holding; folding is too tight given the price and stack depth. I’ll call and see the flop to realize equity and play position/texture post‑flop.

On this flop of 7s-Td-3d, I have bottom pair with an Ace kicker. As the Small Blind, I am out of position against both the pre-flop aggressor (Elon) and the Big Blind (Minni). Standard play here is to check to the raiser to see how they define their hand. My hand has some showdown value and can improve to two pair or trips, but it is not strong enough to lead out into two opponents. Checking allows me to control the pot size and re-evaluate based on the action behind me.

I have 3♥4♥ — bottom pair with a weak kicker in a three‑way pot and I’m out of position. The flop (T♦‑7♠‑3♦) is fairly dry but with two diamonds, so there are some diamond draws and many hands that already beat me (tens, sevens, and most pocket pairs). Betting OOP with a small pair into two players bloats the pot and gets me in tough spots against continuation bets, check‑raises or callers with overcards and draws. I’ll check for pot control and to let the preflop raiser take the initiative — I can call small probe bets to realize equity but will fold to large aggression. If checked to on the turn I’ll re‑evaluate for thin value or a delayed bluff depending on turn texture and opponents’ actions.

Flop is 7s Td 3d, which hits some straight and flush draws but is relatively dry. I raised preflop from the button with a wide range (84o is a standard open), so opponents likely assign me strength. Both blinds checked, indicating weakness or traps, but given hand histories where they call light but fold to aggression often. My hand 8d4c has zero equity: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot absent), only a weak backdoor diamond flush draw. No showdown value. Deep stacks, pot control is key—checking realizes any tiny equity on turn/river without bloating pot OOP for opponents. C-betting here would be a pure bluff with poor blockers (no 7/T/3), and while my range hits some floats, folding to a check-raise or calls is fine long-term. Prioritize not spewing chips with trash; play post-turn if improves or bluff there with better board texture.

The turn card is the Queen of Diamonds, which completes a possible flush. I currently hold bottom pair (threes) and the Ace of Diamonds, which gives me the nut flush draw. Since the flop was checked around, the ranges remain wide. Checking here is the best play to see how my opponents react to the flush-completing card. If a bet occurs, my hand has significant equity with the nut flush draw and a small pair, making it a comfortable call to see the river.

I have 4♥3♥ and only a small pair of threes on a Qd‑Td‑3d‑7s board with two opponents. The turn Qd creates three diamonds on board and adds multiple overcards that beat me (Q, T, 7, many pocket pairs, and any made diamond flush). I’m out of position and the pot is small relative to stacks, so I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand or face a raise that’s hard to continue against. Checking controls the pot, lets me see the river for free, and keeps weaker hands and missed draws in the pot so I can realize equity. Plan: check the river as well if action remains passive; if opponents lead I’ll fold to large aggression and only call small probe bets to see a cheap river.

Turn board Qd completes diamond flushes (three diamonds now: Td,3d,Qd) and adds straight draw possibilities (e.g., J9 for 9-T-Q-J, or KJT). My 8d4c has no pair, no straight draw, and only a weak diamond holding (8d gives minor backdoor but board-heavy). Preflop raise range checked flop, opponents checked twice showing likely capped weak ranges or traps, but history shows they call light yet fold to aggression. Pot 900 deep stacks (my 13400). Betting pure air bluff here risks spewing to calls from medium strength, draws, or floats; poor blockers (no Q/T/7/3). Check realizes thin equity for free river card, maintains pot control, and sets up potential river bluff on blank or check-behind if improves minimally. Superior to donk betting OOP ranges or thin value.

The hand has been checked through on every street. I have bottom pair (threes) with an Ace kicker. Given the extreme passivity shown by both Elon and Minni, it is likely that their ranges are weak. However, a pair of threes is rarely strong enough to bet for value here, as most hands that would call likely have me beat (like a 7 or a 10). By checking, I can reach a showdown for free and potentially win against missed straight draws or lower high-card hands. If someone bets now, I will have to evaluate based on the sizing, but for now, checking is the most prudent play to realize my showdown value.

I have 4♥3♥ — bottom pair (a 3) with a weak 4 kicker on a T♦‑7♠‑3♦‑Q♦‑2♣ board. There are three diamonds on board, so any diamond in an opponent’s hand is a flush; Q/T/7 and most pocket pairs also beat me. The hand has been checked down every street and the pot is small (900) relative to everyone’s stacks. Betting for thin value is unlikely to get called by worse hands, and bluffing has poor fold equity in a multi‑way, passive pot (I have no diamond blockers). I’ll control the pot and take the showdown — check and fold to significant river aggression if faced with a bet.

River board completes obvious diamond flushes (Td, 3d, Qd) and has possible two-pair combos or straights. Both opponents checked flop, turn, and river, indicating capped ranges likely consisting of weak pairs (like 7x, Tx), missed draws, or ace-high floats without showdown value against my perceived strong range. As preflop raiser from button who checked back twice, my range is polarized: nutted hands (flushes, QT, T7 sets/straights) or bluffs. 8d4c is a perfect bluff—8d blocks some flush combos (e.g., K8d, J8d), 4c irrelevant; zero showdown value, so pure semi-bluff with fold equity. Pot 900, stacks deep (mine 13400, Flash 8700, Minni 17000). Betting 600 (2/3 pot) exploits their weakness, gets folds from medium strength (pairs below top, missed draws), thin value from worse, and minimizes loss if raised (easy fold). History shows opponents check-call light early but fold rivers to aggression (e.g., Hands 12, 17, 36). Checking realizes 0 equity and gifts pot to their possible better hands; betting maximizes EV by denying equity and stealing ~66% of time.

The board is 7s-Td-3d-Qd-2c and I have bottom pair (threes) with an Ace kicker. The action was checked through on the flop and turn, which usually indicates weakness across the board. However, Elon has now bet 600 into a 900 pot (2/3 pot sizing). While this could be a 'stab' at a pot no one seems to want, a pair of threes is a very weak bluff-catcher. My hand loses to any 7, 10, Queen, or even a deuce that may have been checked. Furthermore, Minni is still behind me and has yet to act on the river. If I call, I risk her over-calling or raising with a superior hand. Given the 3-way nature of the pot and the fact that almost any made hand beats me, the most disciplined play is to fold and preserve my stack.

Elon (BTN) fires a small 600 into a checked‑down pot on a 7♠ T♦ 3♦ Q♦ 2♣ board. I have 4♥3♥ — bottom pair with a weak kicker and no diamond blockers. Many hands beat me (Qx/T x/7x/pocket pairs/flushes), but this is a small probe bet heads‑up and folding surrenders good pot odds. Calling 600 to win ~2,700 requires ~22% equity — a cheap showdown given my hand can still beat bluffs and missed overcards. Raising would be reckless (no fold equity and poor blockers). I’ll call the 600 to see showdown and avoid bloating the pot with a marginal hand.

I am on the button with Jack-Seven offsuit. In a 3-handed game, this hand is slightly below the standard opening range, especially against active opponents like Minni and Elon who have shown they are willing to defend their blinds and 3-bet. With a stack of 87 big blinds, there is no need to force a play with a marginal hand. Folding is the safest and most disciplined choice here.

Button folded — heads-up vs the Big Blind (Elon). Q♣5♦ is a marginal offsuit hand and I'm out of position. With deep stacks (~184 BB) there's no need to force the action or bloat the pot with a weak holding; a min-raise to 200 would risk a 3-bet and commit chips with poor equity. I'll complete the blind by calling the extra 50 (100 total committed) to see a flop. Plan: fold to heavy aggression on dry misses, or continue only when I hit a queen/strong draw or get a favorable runout/odds.

Preflop in the big blind facing a small blind limp after button folds, resulting in a heads-up pot of 200. My hand Qd6h is offsuit queen-high with poor playability, marginal at best against a likely wide limping range from Minni, who has shown a tendency to limp/call frequently in past hands. Raising risks committing to a tough spot out of position postflop with a weak hand, while checking allows me to realize equity for free in position postflop (acting second) with implied odds potential on suited or connected boards. No need to bluff-raise this weak holding; better to keep the pot small and play postflop.

Flop is very dry (4♥ 8♣ 2♠) and I completely missed with Q♣5♦ — no pair, no real draw (only 6 clean outs: three queens and three fives → ~24% to hit by river). I’m out of position against Elon with deep stacks, so I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand. Default plan is to control pot size: check and fold to heavy aggression, but consider calling small-to-medium continuation bets if the price is right (pot odds + implied odds). If checked to me on the turn I’ll re-evaluate — only continue aggressively if I pair the Q/5 or get a very favorable fold-equity spot.

Flop is 8c-4h-2s, a dry, low, uncoordinated board with no flush draws or straight draws obvious. Qd6h is queen-high with backdoor heart draw but no immediate pair, draw, or strong blockers. Minni limped preflop (wide range including low cards, suited junk) and checked flop, showing weakness. With position (acting last postflop), checking back realizes equity cheaply against her likely checking range, keeps the pot small (200 into 12.7k stack, deep), and preserves betting range for better spots like turns that improve me or allow bluffs with equity. No fold equity justifies a bluff here; villain calls light due to history of limp/calling stations. Pot control optimal with marginal hand.

I paired my 5 on the turn (Qc5d => second pair) on a very dry board (4h 8c 2s 5c). Both players have been passive so far (checked through), so this is a good spot to take the initiative and extract thin value while keeping the pot small with deep stacks behind. A small lead bet (~half pot) charges any worse made hands and backdoor draws, denies a free river, and still allows me to fold to real strength. I’ll bet 100 (half the pot) to get value from worse pairs/overcards that will call small and to price out bluffs; if I face a significant raise I’ll treat my hand as suspect and fold rather than inflate the pot with a vulnerable second pair. If called I’ll re-evaluate on the river.

Facing a small 100 bet into 300 pot on a low, uncoordinated board (4h-8c-2s-5c), getting over 4:1 odds to call with queen-high. My Qd6h has decent showdown value against Minni's wide limping/checking range, which includes many weak pairs, ace-high, and draws she might bet lightly. No strong draws completed for her on this turn (5c adds minor gutshots like A6 or 36 for wheel, but unlikely given her flop check). Raising a bluff lacks fold equity against her calling station tendencies seen in history (frequent limp/call, check/call large bets). Folding is too tight preflop limped pot OOP; realize equity cheaply deepstacked (12k effective), control pot, and re-evaluate river in position (acting second postflop). Plan to check/call or check/fold river depending on her line.

I have Qc5d — second pair (fives) with a Q kicker. The river (7c) makes the board 4♥-8♣-2♠-5♣-7♣, which is now more dangerous: it completes possible two-club flushes (board has three clubs) and several straight combinations (3/6 type straights). Elon has been passive (check–call), so his range includes medium/weak made hands and missed draws — many of which now beat me. With deep stacks and a vulnerable second pair, my priority is pot control; betting risks getting raised off my hand or bloating the pot when I’m behind. I’ll check and go to showdown rather than bluff or thin-value bet a marginal holding. If Elon bets, I’ll fold to a sizable river raise and only consider calling a very small probe bet.

River board completes a club flush (8c-5c-7c) and adds straight possibilities (e.g., 3-6 for 3-4-5-6-7, 6-9 for 5-6-7-8-9 gutshot earlier), making it draw-heavy. My Qd6h is queen-high showdown value with backdoor heart draw that missed, no pair/draw. Minni's line (SB limp wide range, check flop weak, small turn bet probe/value thin with weak pairs like 44/22/55/Jx or club draws, check river suggests missed draw, weak made hand, or trap but unlikely given history). Her frequent limp/call/check-call station tendencies (e.g., Hands 1,3,14,18,20,23,27,29,35,39,41) mean she showdowns worse often (Ax, Kx, weak pairs). Position allows cheap realization of equity in 400 pot deepstacked (~12k eff). Betting thin value unlikely (beats only bluffs/A-high she checks), bluff lacks fold equity vs her calling station profile. Check-behind optimal to pot control, deny her free cards if weak, and win at showdown vs her wide checking range including worse high cards/pairs that don't bet river.

K♦Q♣ on the button in a 3-handed pot is a strong, positionally advantaged hand — worth opening for value and fold equity. Table history shows 3x opens (to 300) are the norm, so I’ll raise to 300 to isolate the blinds, take the initiative, and keep the pot a reasonable size with deep stacks. If called I’ll C‑bet in position on favorable boards; if I face a 3‑bet I’ll evaluate sizing and opponent — call smaller 3‑bets to play postflop, fold or re-evaluate to large/offensive 3‑bets.

Preflop from the SB facing a button raise to 300 (3x). Qd5d is a suited queen-high hand with flush and straight potential. Button range is wide (likely 35-50% of hands), giving Q5s approximately 38-42% equity, sufficient for the pot odds (calling 250 more into 450 pot requires ~36% equity). Suited playability provides implied odds for strong draws postflop. Deep stacks (mine 129k BBs equiv) favor seeing flops with speculative hands OOP. Folding is too tight in modern play vs button; 3-betting lacks strong blockers or value. Calling balances defense and realizes equity well.

I am in the Big Blind with K6s. Minni opened to 3bb from the button, and Elon called from the Small Blind. I am getting very favorable pot odds (200 to call into a 700 pot) to see a flop. King-high suited has decent playability and realization of equity in a multi-way pot, especially given the stack depths and the price provided. Closing the action here is the most standard and profitable play.

Flop of 5h-3c-9d gives me middle pair (fives) with a diamond flush draw, a very strong combination with significant showdown value, nut flush draw potential, and straight draw blockers (holds 4x, 6x somewhat). Acting first OOP vs. button raiser (wide range including Ax suited, broadway, pairs, suited connectors) and BB defender (wide calling range). Board is coordinated with diamond flush draw, gutshots (4-6, 7-8 somewhat), so betting denies equity to draws while extracting value from weaker pairs (9x, Ax high), overpairs sometimes, and floats. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or multiway pot control issues with two opponents. Optimal to lead ~60% pot (540) for balance: thin value, protection, and fold equity vs. air. Deep effective stacks (mine 127 BBs) support realized equity if called. Preflop call was correct; now transition to aggression with improved hand.

I have King-high with a weak backdoor heart draw on a board of 9-5-3. Elon has led out (donk bet) for 550 into two players, which typically indicates a made hand like a 9 or a set, or at least a strong draw. Given that I have completely missed the flop and Minni (the pre-flop aggressor) is still left to act behind me, there is no reason to continue with this hand. King-high is unlikely to be the best hand, and I have very few ways to improve.

I raised preflop and I’m on the button with K♦Q♣ on a very dry 5♥-3♣-9♦ flop. Elon’s small bet (550 into a 1450 pot) looks like a standard, wide c-bet. Calling 550 gives me ~27.5% pot odds but only ~24% to pair K or Q by showdown, so a flat call is marginal. I’ll leverage position and apply pressure — raise to the minimum (total 1100) to pick up folds from weaker one-pair/overcard hands and to take the initiative. If Elon 3-bets big I’ll fold (this is largely a bluff/semi-bluff); if he just calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and continue barreling on many blanks or slow down versus strong action.

Facing a raise to 1100 total (additional 550 to call) from the button after my flop donk bet of 550 into 900 pot. Pot now 2550, excellent odds of 550 into 2550 (~4.6:1 direct, ~5.6:1 effective post-call pot of 3100, needing ~15-18% equity). Hand strength: middle pair (5s) with strong Queen-high kicker and nut flush draw (Qd5d vs 9d board, 9 outs ~19% equity standalone, total ~35%+ vs value-heavy raising range). Minni (button raiser preflop, wide ~40% range) raises donk OOP likely polarized: value (99, sets 33/55 unlikely, A9s/K9s/JTs, overpairs TT+, AQo+), strong draws (AdXd flush draws, some gutshots like 46), bluffs (missed broadways, weak Ax). Crushes bluffs/draws (>60% equity), solid vs value (20-40% w/ outs). Raising risks bloating pot OOP (SPR ~4 post-call, her 169BBs could stack off lighter), folding too tight preflop call + donk committed us reasonably. Calling realizes equity, controls pot size, high turn playability (flush ~19%, pair improves ~8% to trips/TW, holds often). Balances ranges, exploits her aggression shown in prior hands (frequent postflop leads/calls big). Deep stacks favor multi-street play with combo draw.

Turn board pairs the 9s, improving some hands like 99 to trips or two-pair combos (e.g., 95s, 93s), but my hand remains very strong: middle pair of 5s with Queen kicker (beats worse 9x or Ax without 9/5), plus nut diamond flush draw (9 outs, ~19% to river) for ~35-40% total equity vs. her polarized raising range (value: 99, 9x strong, overpairs TT+, sets; draws: AdXd, straight draws like 46s/7xs; bluffs: missed Ax/broadway). Pot 3100 OOP heads-up after I donk-called flop raise, SPR ~3.7 (my 11600). Checking risks free card to her 17 club outs (~19%), gutshot straights (4x ~4%), or pure air that improves; induces her bluffs/value but pot control suboptimal with huge equity. Betting extracts value from worse pairs/overpairs (JJ-QQ call often, weaker 9x), protects vs. draws (~33% pot denial equity), fold equity vs. bluffs (she aggressive per history: Hand 1 big river bet/call, Hand 14 river jam, Hand 23 turn raise). Size 2/3 pot (2100) balances thin value/protection (smaller risks overcall), exploits her wide button range and postflop aggression (frequent raises postflop in Hands 3,12,14,21,24,28,32,34,35,38,39), deep stacks favor getting value multiway earlier. Preflop/flop lines committed us profitably; continue aggression to realize/maximize EV.

Elon called my flop raise and now leads turn (board 5♥-3♣-9♦-9♣) for 2,100. That line (call flop raise → bet turn after the board pairs) heavily favors a 9x or other made hand. I have K♦Q♣ with zero pair and only 6 clean outs (3 Ks + 3 Qs) to hit on the river. Pot math: calling 2,100 into the current 5,200 pot would make the final pot ~7,300, so I need roughly 28.8% equity to break even. My one-card equity is ~6/46 ≈ 13% (and is lower if Elon holds any Ks/Qs). Raising as a bluff is possible but unlikely to fold 9x or other value hands that called the flop; it would just risk more of my deep stack for little fold equity. Given the poor odds and the line he took, the +EV play is to release the hand and wait for a better spot.

Preflop on the button with 5d7d, suited one-gapper connectors, deep effective stacks (147BB). Position is excellent for realizing equity multiway or heads-up. Pot 150, blinds posted. Standard play is to raise to ~3x BB to steal blinds or play postflop in position with initiative. Minni and Flash defend wide based on history (frequent calls pre/post), but button equity favors aggression. Raising to 300 total balances range, applies pressure, and sets up continuation bets. Calling/limping weaker as it invites multiway pots without initiative; folding too tight.

I have Ace-Four suited in the Small Blind. In a three-handed game, this hand is well ahead of a standard button opening range. I will 3-bet to 1000 to exert pressure on Elon and potentially take down the pot immediately. Ace-Four suited also has great playability due to its nut-flush potential and blocker value, making it an ideal candidate for a 3-bet from the blinds.

I’m in the big blind with 9♦3♠ — a weak, unsuited, disconnected hand. Elon opened to 300 and Flash 3‑bet to 1000; I’m facing a 900 call (min‑raise to 1700 is available) and likely a multiway or at least a strong 3‑bet range. Calling 900 into a ~1400 pot requires roughly ~39% equity to break even, which 9‑3o very rarely has versus these ranges, and playing this hand out of position will be difficult. A shove/raise bluff here would be too risky with two opponents and no blockers. With a healthy 16.8k stack and better spots to pick, folding is the +EV play.

Facing a 3-bet to 1000 (total) from Flash in the SB after my button open to 300. Pot 1400, to call 700 getting over 2:1 (33% equity). 5d7d is a suited one-gapper with great playability, position on SB heads-up, deep stacks (74BB eff). Flash is aggressive preflop (frequent raises/3-bets from history), likely wide 3-bet range including Ax suited, broadways, suited connectors for isolation. Folding too tight preflop with position and playability; 4-bet bluff not ideal without better blockers. Call to realize equity postflop OOP for opponent, control pot, use position to outplay on flops that connect (gaps, flush/straight draws). Plan to c-bet favorable flops, float/raise draws.

I was the pre-flop aggressor with a 3-bet, and this King-high board (Ks 2d 6d) is very favorable for my perceived range, which includes strong hands like AA, KK, and AK. Despite only having Ace-high, I have the initiative and an overcard. A continuation bet here puts pressure on Elon's middle pairs and non-King hands. I will bet around 35% of the pot to maintain my lead in the hand and potentially take it down immediately.

Flop board Ks2d6d is great for my 7d5d: nut flush draw (9 outs) plus double gutshot straight draw (4x and 8x, 8 outs total, minus 2 overlap for ~15 outs or 32% equity). Pot odds excellent at 750 into 2850 (~21% needed, plus massive implied with 66BB effective stacks and position). Flash's aggressive history suggests wide 3-bet range (AK, KQ, suited broadways, Ax draws, some bluffs); his 35% pot bet could be value or semi-bluff, but my equity crushes many hands. Calling in position realizes equity, controls pot size OOP for opponent, sets up turn aggression (c/r if improves or check-raise air), or check back floats. Raising draw risks fold equity loss if he has showdown value, but calling maximizes EV long-term. Plan: call, play turn based on board/texture.

I will check this turn. After my 3-bet pre-flop and continuation bet on the flop were both called, it is likely Elon has a piece of the board, most likely a King or a medium pocket pair. The 5c on the turn gives me a gutshot straight draw to the wheel, but a second barrel here is risky against a range that has already shown resistance. Checking allows me to control the pot size and potentially realize my equity if Elon checks behind.

Heads-up on turn vs Flash (SB) after he checked. Board Ks-2d-6d-5c, pot 3600, effective stacks ~66BB (his 6650). My 7d5d gives bottom pair (5s), nut diamond flush draw (7-high, 9 outs), double gutshot straight draw (4 or 8x for straights 2-3-4-5-6 or 5-6-7-8, ~8 outs minus overlaps), ~35-40% equity vs his polarized range. Flop action: his 3-bet pre, 750 bet into ~2900 (26% pot, aggressive continuation), my call with monster draw. His check-turn after flop bet suggests possible medium strength (top pair weak kicker, missed draw, or trap with set/overpair), but history shows aggression (frequent c-bets, some check-backs). Checking risks giving free card to his equity (e.g., clubs, spades draws, gutshots) or letting him realize showdown cheaply. Betting builds pot with huge equity/implied odds, gets value from worse pairs/draws (Kx weak, 77-JJ trapped), protects vs runner-runner, and denies equity. Size 2/3 pot (2400) balances value/semi-bluff, < half his stack to keep fold equity low while charging max. Position allows controlling river. EV+ bet over check given deep stacks and my nutted potential.

On the turn, Elon has bet 2400 into a 3600 pot (66% pot) after I checked. My hand, Ace-Four of Spades, currently only has Ace-high and a gutshot straight draw (needing a Three). While I have 3-bet pre-flop and c-bet the flop, Elon's call on the King-high flop suggests he has a piece of the board, likely a King or a middle pocket pair. To call 2400, I need approximately 28.5% equity. With only 4 outs for the straight and potentially 3 outs for an Ace (which might still be behind a hand like AK or KQ), my equity is roughly 14%. Given the pot odds and the likelihood that Elon is holding at least a pair of Kings, calling is mathematically incorrect. Folding allows me to preserve my remaining 6650 chips.

I am on the button with King-Ten offsuit in a 3-handed game. This is a strong opening hand in this position. Standard strategy dictates an open-raise to take control of the pot and potentially pick up the blinds. Given the stack sizes and the 50/100 blind structure, a 3x raise to 300 is a standard and effective sizing.

8♦2♥ is the bottom of the deck — no high card, unsuited, no real straight/flush potential. I’m out of position (SB) facing a button open to 3BB; calling 250 into a 450 pot requires ~36% equity to break even, which 8-2o doesn’t have against a normal open-raise range, and playing multiway/OOP will be very difficult. A 3-bet bluff is unnecessary with my ~168BB stack and two opponents (BB has shown willingness to defend); better to preserve chips and wait for a real spot. Fold.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot is 450, need to call 200 more for pot odds of about 3.25:1, requiring roughly 23% equity, which Q2s suited achieves against a typical wide button opening range (30-50% hands). Q2s has good implied odds due to suitedness for flush/straight potential and queen blocker value. Folding is too tight in BB vs BTN; 3-betting as a bluff is possible but unnecessary with direct playability postflop where we have position advantage over button OOP. Opponent Flash is active on button from history (frequent opens), so defend wide and realize equity. Calling to see flop.

Heads-up flop vs aggressive Button who opened wide preflop and checked to me as IP. Pot 650, stacks deep (my 16k, villain 6k). My Qc2c is queen-high with a weak backdoor club flush draw (one club on board) and negligible straight potential on J♥5♥7♣ board, which has heart flush draw possibilities and some straight outs but misses me entirely. No pair, gutshot, or strong draw—pure air with poor showdown value. Villain's check suggests weakness (missed J/5/7, Ax, low pairs, draws), but betting as a bluff is risky: low fold equity after check (villain can have floats/calls), thin value range is capped (no sets likely), and I block few calling hands (KQ/AQ but not strongly). Checking back realizes equity for free, controls pot, and allows villain to bluff turn/river where I can value own weak hands or catch up. Folding unnecessary as checked to; prefer check to pot control and multiway potential later if we check down. Consistent with wide BB defense realizing equity postflop IP.

As the pre-flop aggressor, I am in a good position to continuation bet this board. The flop of J-7-5 is relatively unconnected, though it does favor a calling range slightly. With King-Ten, I have two overcards to the middle cards and one overcard to the Jack. A standard-sized C-bet of roughly half the pot should be enough to fold out his weaker hands and air while allowing me to realize equity if called and a King or Ten falls on the turn.

Facing a half-pot bet of 350 into 1000 on a J♥5♥7♣ flop as Q♣2♣ (suited queen-high with weak backdoor club draw). Board favors villain's wide button opening range: likely hits like Jx, 5x/7x pairs, overcards, or heart flush draws (two hearts on board). My hand has no pair, no made draw, minimal backdoors, and poor showdown value—queen-high loses to any pair or likely better high card. Pot odds require ~26% equity (350/1350), but Q2s realizes <20% against polarized betting range (value + semibluffs that crush me). Calling bloats pot OOP for villain on turns I miss (most), leading to difficult spots. Raising as pure bluff risky: low fold equity vs aggressive Flash (history of continuation betting flops), blocks few value hands (no Q), exposes to shoves. Folding preserves stack (16k deep), consistent with preflop wide defend but disciplined postflop—realize equity selectively, avoid spew. Long-term ICM/tournament EV prioritizes survival over marginal floats.

Button with K7s in a three-handed game — strong spot to apply pressure. Suited King has good blocking value and postflop playability; in position I want to open a wide range to steal blinds and take initiative. Standard 3x open (to 300) is size I’ve used throughout this tournament and keeps the pot manageable while building value. If I face a large 3-bet I’ll re-evaluate and fold most K7s lines vs heavy aggression.

8d3c is a complete trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card strength. Facing a button raise from Minni, who opens wide but OOP defense requires playable hands. Pot odds are 250 to call into 450 (pot will be 700 after call), but postflop OOP with this hand is disastrous - low equity, no bluff value, dominated often. Flash may 3bet or call behind, but folding preserves deep stack (162BB) for premium spots in tournament with 104 hands left. Standard fold.

I am in the Big Blind with Ac 3d. Minni raised from the button to 300. Elon folded the small blind. The pot is currently 450, and it costs me 200 more to call. Given the pot odds (2.25 to 1) and the fact that I am closing the action pre-flop, defending with an Ace is a standard play here, even if it is a weak one. I will call to see the flop.

I have bottom pair (threes) with an Ace kicker on a dry flop of 10-6-3. I am out of position against Minni, who was the pre-flop aggressor. Checking is the standard play here to see how she reacts. If she makes a continuation bet, I will likely call a reasonable sizing to see if I can improve or reach showdown, as my hand has some value but isn't strong enough to lead out.

I opened from the button and got called by the big blind, so I have position and range advantage. Flop 3♠ T♦ 6♣ largely misses a big portion of the BB’s calling range (many broadways, Axs, small pairs and suited connectors). I have K♠7♠ — a high-card with a two-card backdoor spade draw and a strong blocker (the K). Standard play here is a continuation bet to take the initiative, fold out many unpaired hands and small pairs, and charge any genuine draws. I size ~half-pot to keep fold equity but not overcommit: pot is 650 so I bet 350. If he folds I take it; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and only continue aggressively with improvement or a favorable turn texture; if he raises big I’ll usually fold K7s and re-evaluate.

I have bottom pair with an Ace kicker on a dry rainbow board. Minni, the pre-flop aggressor, has made a standard continuation bet of 350 into a 650 pot. With pot odds of approximately 3.86 to 1, calling is the most appropriate play to see a turn card and re-evaluate, as my hand has enough showdown value and potential to improve to justify continuing against a wide button range.

On the turn, the Ace of Spades improves my hand to two pair (Aces and Threes). While this is a strong hand, the Ace is also very much in the range of the pre-flop aggressor (Minni). By checking, I allow her to continue betting with her bluffs or weaker holdings that she might have been barreling with on the flop. If she also has an Ace, I am in a strong position but need to be cautious of better two pairs (like AT) or sets. Checking is the most flexible play here to see her reaction to the scare card.

Flop c-bet succeeded but the turn A♠ is a nasty card for my range — it helps any A-x and checks some pairs. I currently have K♠7♠ with a four‑to‑a‑flush (9 outs) but no pair. I want pot control and to keep decision flexibility: checking denies free turns to worse hands and induces bluffs, while avoiding building a big pot out of position vs a likely Ace or a made pair. Plan: check now; call a small bet to realize my ~19% river flush equity and fold to a large raise/shove.

I have Two Pair (Aces and Threes) on a board of 3s-Td-6c-As-7c. After calling a continuation bet on the flop, the turn went check-check when the Ace appeared. This suggests Minni likely has a marginal made hand such as a Ten or a weak Ace that she was pot-controlling, or perhaps a missed draw. Since I am out of position, I should lead out on the river to extract value from her medium-strength hands (like Tx or Ax) which she might check back if I check. A bet of 800 (approximately 60% of the pot) is a standard value sizing in this spot.

River analysis: I now have a pair of sevens (K♠7♠) after the 7♣. Flash (BB) checked the turn and then led the river for 800 into a 1,350 pot (2150 total) — I must call 800 to see showdown. His line (call preflop, call my flop c-bet, check turn, small river lead) is consistent with thin value (Ax, some Tx or 6x, occasional slow-played sets) but also contains missed two-spade combos and other bluffs. Pot odds on the call are ~37% (800/2150), and the call is cheap relative to my stack (~5% of 16,100). Raising here would be thin — it will get called by most A/T value hands and only fold some bluffs — while folding surrenders value from bluffs and weak hands. Given my showdown value and the price, the +EV play is to call and realize equity/showdown.

K7o is a standard raising hand from the button in an unopened pot. With deep stacks and a pot of 150 already from blinds, a standard raise to 300 (3x BB) has good equity realization: steals blinds frequently or plays well post-flop with king-high and backdoor potential. Opponents have shown aggression but also fold to raises often in history (e.g., many pre-flop folds). Limping is too passive, folding too tight on button. Aim to build stack by stealing effectively over tournament.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Five offsuit, which is a very weak starting hand. Elon has raised from the Button. Defending such a weak hand out of position against a button open is generally a losing play, especially with an active player in the Big Blind yet to act. Given my stack size of 84.5 big blinds, there is no need to take unnecessary risks with junk hands. I will fold and wait for a more favorable situation.

Call (total 300). Heads-up to a BTN open, 3♠8♠ suited is a playable defend — it has backdoor flush/straight potential and decent equity vs a wide opening range. The pot is 450 and I have to call 200 more (total committed 300) so I need ~31% equity to break even; 3♠8♠ vs a typical BTN open has about that or better and the ~15k effective stacks give good implied odds. A min-raise to 500 is possible, but it’s thin with a marginal hand and risks bloating the pot or getting 3-bet. Plan: see the flop cheaply, continue aggressively when I hit spades/pairs/draws, otherwise control the pot and fold to heavy pressure on later streets.

I have middle pair (8s) with a weak 3 kicker on 8c‑7c‑Qd. Villain (BTN) checked — his range includes a lot of Qx and overcards plus some draws; my hand is too marginal and I’m out of position to start bloating the pot. With ~15k effective stacks I’ll control the pot: check to see how he reacts. Plan is to call reasonable bets on the turn to realize equity and fold to large pressure unless I improve (pair/8x or a very favorable runout), or to become more aggressive if I pick up equity (backdoor/spade or trips).

Middle pair top kicker (7s pairing 7c) with king overcard on a Q-8-7 flop with two clubs (flush draw) and connected cards (straight draws possible like J-10, 9-10, J-9). Preflop raiser in position vs one opponent who checked (weak range likely). Continuation bet for thin value, protection against draws, deny equity, and build pot. History shows Minni calls stations but folds to aggression often. Deep stacks allow flexibility. Sizing ~60% pot (400) balances value vs bluffs/draws.

I have middle pair (8s) with a very weak 3‑kicker on 8c‑7c‑Qd. BTN opened wide preflop so his c‑bet range contains a lot of Qx/overcards, some pocket pairs and some club draws — not a polarized, crushing range. He bet 400 into the pot (I need ~28% equity to call). My pair plus backdoor spade/straight equity and the deep stacks justify a call OOP to realize equity and keep the pot under control. Raising to 800 is thin with this kicker (it bloats the pot out of position and invites 3‑bets or big turn pressure). Plan: call now; on the turn I’ll re‑evaluate — fold to heavy aggression unless I improve to two‑pair/trips or pick up a clear draw (or face a very small continuation bet), and be ready to extract value if villain shows weakness.

I hold 3♠8♠ on 8c‑7c‑Qd‑A♠ — middle pair (pair of eights) with a very weak 3‑kicker. Pot is 1,450 and stacks are deep (~15k each). The turn Ace is a scary card: BTN opened wide preflop and c‑bet the flop, so his range contains lots of Qx/overcards and some Ax — many of which now beat me. I only have 5 outs to improve to two‑pair/trips on the river (two remaining 8s + three 3s) ≈ 11%, and I cannot make a flush anymore (only one card left).

Elon checked the turn (no bet to call), which shows some pot control/weakness but doesn’t eliminate Ax from his range. Betting here as a bluff has limited fold equity against a wide calling range and needlessly bloats a small pot into a dangerous river decision against a deeper stack. Betting for value is thin because many hands that would call (Ax, Qx, better pairs) already beat me.

Plan: control the pot and realize equity — check back the turn. If the river misses me I’ll fold to sizable aggression and only call reasonable thin value bets; if I hit two‑pair/trips I will extract value. Checking preserves stack and keeps options open with only ~11% to improve.

Turn card As is a dangerous card that likely improves Minni's range significantly, potentially giving her top pair or better with an ace, while our middle pair (sevens) with king kicker remains vulnerable to aces, queens, better sevens, and draws. Minni has checked twice, suggesting weakness or trapping, but her flop call indicates some strength. Pot is 1450 with deep stacks (15500/14600), and position allows us to realize equity profitably at showdown. Betting risks folding out worse hands or getting called/raised by better; checking controls the pot, denies villain cheap river cards, and lets showdown value our hand against her wide BB defending range, which often includes marginal holdings that called the flop. History shows Minni calls but folds to heavy aggression; checking balances our range here after cbeting flop.

Check. I have a marginal one‑pair (8s) with a terrible 3‑kicker on 8c‑7c‑Qd‑As‑2c. Villain’s line (BTN open, c‑bet flop, check turn) is consistent with a wide range that contains lots of Qx, some Ax and pocket pairs — many hands that already beat me. I only had ~5 outs (two remaining 8s + three 3s) on the river (~11% to improve) and no backdoor flush; the river 2c is a blank for me. Betting for value is thin because stronger hands will call and worse hands that might fold are rare; bluffing has limited fold equity versus a wide calling range and would only bloat the pot OOP with ~15k stacks. Preserve the stack and take the showdown: check and fold to significant river aggression (only consider calling very small, non‑committal bets).

River 2c completes the club flush draw from flop (8c7c), pairs nothing relevant, and adds minor straight possibilities but board remains uncoordinated for straights beyond broadways. We hold middle pair (7s on 7c) with K kicker, which has showdown value against Minni's BB defending range that includes worse pairs (6x, 5x, small pairs), weak Qx, busted draws, and some Ax without improvement. She called flop cbet (likely with medium strength/draws), checked turn after scary As (which hits her Ax but also strong hands), and checks river again, representing weakness or traps but history shows her as a passive caller who doesn't barrel draws often. Pot is 1450 with deep stacks (15.5k/14.6k); betting risks folding out worse (our value) while getting called/raised by flushes, Ax, Qx (beats us), 88/77 better. Thin value bet or bluff unprofitable given her calling station tendencies (wins less when called, loses EV to calls from betters). Checking realizes equity profitably at showdown, controls pot, and balances our checking range (missed draws, medium pairs) after cbeting flop and checking turn. Consistent with prior turn check logic, exploits her passivity while denying unnecessary risk mid-tournament.

I am on the button in a 3-handed game with 89o. While 89o can be an opening hand in some 3-handed situations, both Minni and Elon have significantly larger stacks and have shown a willingness to defend their blinds and play aggressively post-flop. Given my stack size of 84.5 BB and the tendency of the blinds to contest pots, I will fold this marginal hand to avoid a difficult post-flop situation against deeper stacks.

I have K♠4♠ in the small blind. The pot is 150 and it costs me 50 more to call (bringing my total in the pot to 100). This is a marginal, often dominated hand and I'll be out of position post‑flop vs Elon. With deep stacks I don't want to open‑raise and face a 3‑bet or play a larger pot OOP with a weak holding. Calling is cheap, preserves stack, keeps the pot manageable, and lets me realize equity (backdoor spade + chance to hit K). I will call to see a flop and proceed cautiously — folding to heavy aggression on unfavorable boards and continuing only with clear equity or good reads.

Preflop in the big blind facing a small blind limp after button fold. Pot is 200 with no money to call. Qc5s is offsuit queen-high, weak hand with poor playability and low equity against a typical limping range. SB limps are often weak or speculative, but raising risks playing OOP post-flop with a marginal hand. Checking realizes equity, maintains pot control, and leverages position advantage to play post-flop profitably if we hit or bluff. Folding is unnecessary with free play option. No strong reason to raise a trash hand here in a tournament with deep stacks.

I hold K♠4♠ on Td-4h-6c — a small pair with a good kicker in a heads‑up pot. Elon (BB) checked, so his range is wide. This is a dry board with little coordinated draw potential; betting extracts thin value from worse hands (unpaired broadways, smaller pairs) and charges any overcards/draws while keeping the pot controlled. I don't want to inflate the pot OOP with a vulnerable one‑pair hand, so I make a controlled half‑pot bet (100 into 200). Plan: if Elon folds I take it down; if he calls I re‑evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously (check back on dangerous cards, value/turn small on safe cards). If he check‑raises substantially, I will assume strong holdings and fold rather than bloat the pot OOP with a marginal pair.

Preflop check was correct with Qc5s, a speculative hand suited for multiway pots but weak OOP. Flop Td4h6c completely misses: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot to 3-7 unlikely), no flush draw, queen-high overcard at best. Minni's SB limp-bet (50% pot) suggests a polarized range: value (pairs like 66-TT, A-high, sets) or bluffs/draws (suited connectors like 53, 75, J5). My hand has ~20-30% equity against her range at best but realizes poorly OOP, where check-calls/reraises are awkward without equity. Pot odds 3:1 require ~25% equity, but calling builds pot OOP with air, vulnerable to turn aggression. Folding preserves deep stack (154x BB), avoids spew, and exploits Minni's loose limping tendencies by waiting for stronger hands. Tournament stage favors discipline over marginal defense.

Three-handed and on the button I should be opening a wide range. Q♦5♠ is marginal but worth raising to take the initiative, fold out blinds, and play postflop in position. A standard 3x BB open to 300 is consistent with my image and reduces the chance of a multiway limp pot. Plan: fold to a large 3‑bet from either opponent (Q5o plays poorly vs 3‑bet ranges); if called, C-bet on dry flops and proceed cautiously on ace‑high or heavily coordinated boards where I’m likely dominated.

Preflop in SB facing button min-raise to 300 (3x BB). Pot 450, calling 250 more for great implied odds (pot will be ~700). A6o (offsuit ace-high) blocks AA/AK/AQ, has ~38-42% equity vs typical button open range (25-40% hands). History shows Minni aggressive on button, often raising wide and continuing postflop, but I can realize equity OOP with ace blocker and potential domination. BB (Flash) shorter stack may defend wide, but pot odds justify call regardless. Folding too tight deepstacked (150BB); 3-betting offsuit A6 OOP risks dominated calls/4bets. Plan to play aggressively postflop if I hit or as bluff.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Five of clubs. Minni raised from the button and Elon called from the small blind. I am getting excellent pot odds (200 to call into a pot of 700), and my hand is a strong suited ace that plays well multi-way. While a squeeze is possible, calling allows me to see a flop with a hand that has good potential without bloating the pot unnecessarily against two larger stacks.

Nut flush draw (As) with ace-high on a spade-heavy board (Qs4sJc), giving me 11 immediate outs to the nuts (~22% to hit flush by river) plus potential runner-runner straights or backdoors. 3-way pot of 900 OOP first to act; betting builds the pot when ahead vs. worse draws/pairs (e.g., Minni's wide button range includes Qx, Jx, suited connectors; Flash's BB defends include similar). Charges draws (e.g., lower flush draws, gutshots), denies equity, and leverages my blocker to AA/AK/AQ. Checking risks free cards to flush/straight draws or getting check-raised; betting 2/3 pot (600) balances value/protection/bluffing my SB calling range. History shows Minni aggressive postflop but folds to pressure often (e.g., Hands 12,21,28); Flash shorter and passive. Deep effective stack (81bb min), great spot to apply pressure.

I have Ace-high with a backdoor flush draw on a flop of Jack-Four-Queen. Elon has led out for 600 into a 900 pot, which is a strong bet into the pre-flop raiser (Minni) and myself. Given the board texture and the fact that I have no pair and no significant draw, calling 600 is not profitable. Folding is the most prudent move to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Flop: Jc 4s Qs — I hold Qd5s: top pair but a very weak kicker and only a backdoor spade. Elon leads for 600 into 1500 (~40% pot). In position with a marginal but likely‑good hand, I prefer to call rather than inflate the pot with a raise. Calling keeps worse Qx and Jx/bluffs in, controls pot size, and lets me re‑evaluate on the turn based on whether I improve or the board gets dangerous (ace/king or another spade). If the turn is a blank I can value bet; if it’s a spade or high card and Elon shows heavy aggression I’ll be prepared to fold — my kicker is too weak to build a big pot without improvement. Call 600 to realize equity and keep options open.

Turn card 5d is a relatively blank card that doesn't complete the nut flush (spades still live) or major straights beyond what was possible pre-turn. Board remains draw-heavy (Qs4sJc5d: flush draws, open-enders like 23o/KTs, pairs like QJ). I hold nut flush draw (As) with 11 outs (~22% equity to river) and ace-high blocker, OOP vs Minni's button calling range post-flop bet (likely Qx, Jx, suited connectors, pocket pairs, weaker Ax). Pot 2100 deep-stacked (90+BB effective). Checking risks her check-back (no fold equity, drawing thin) or bet where I must call OOP; semi-bluff betting ~55% pot (1200) applies max pressure: gets value from Qx/Jx/top-pair, charges her draws (e.g., KsJs, lower spades), denies equity to floats, and sets up river shove if spade hits. History shows Minni calls wide but folds to continued aggression (e.g., Hands 12,21,28,36); my range advantage as preflop caller + flop bettor includes strong hands. Favorable if called (pot-odds/SDV), fold-equity ~30-40% vs her range. Optimal continuation to realize equity/protect.

The turn 5d gives me two pair (Q5) — my hand just went from a marginal top-pair to a strong value hand. Elon’s small turn bet (1,200 into ~3,300) looks like a probing/continuation-size bet that will be made with a wide range: single-pair Jx/Qx, some weak made hands, and occasional bluffs; only sets/trips or QJ two‑pair really beat me. I want to extract value and charge hands that will call (weaker Qx, Jx, KQ etc.) while keeping the raise size small enough to induce calls rather than folding out all my worse hands — and I don’t need heavy protection (few realistic draws left). I’ll raise to the minimum (to 2,400 total) to get value from worse pairs and to fold out some air; if Elon jams/3‑bets big I’ll shrink back (he’s likely got trips/full house), and if he just calls I’ll plan a value line on a blank river and re-evaluate versus large river aggression.

Facing a turn raise to 2400 total (1200 into 4500 pot pre-my action) from Minni on a draw-heavy board (Jc4sQs5d) with nut spade flush draw (As6h, 11 outs ~23% to nuts) and ace-high blocker. Effective stacks deep (~128BB). Pot odds to call: 1200 into 5700 (~17% equity needed), and I have that plus implied odds if spade hits (nuts, river jam). However, calling OOP is passive: if miss river, tough spot with ace-high vs her strong raising range (Qx/Jx top pairs/two-pair, QQ/JJ sets, higher spades like KJs/QJs/Ts9s for combo draws/flushes). History shows Minni calls wide pre/flop but folds to turn aggression (e.g., Hands 12,21,28,36 where she folds to continued bets; aggressive but not sticky). Shoving all-in (13300 total) leverages fold equity (~40% vs her capped range: gets value from worse like QJ/weak Qx/weak draws, folds out KsJs/Ts9s combo draws/scares off floats), realizes max equity (~35-45% if called vs polarixed range), denies river equity, and if called still live (outs + blocker reduces her AA/AK nuts). Superior to call (no fold eq, pot control issues) or fold (spew equity). Tournament stage (hand 49/150), stack preservation secondary to +EV spot with range advantage as preflop defender/flop+turn bettor.

Elon’s shove to 13,300 after I raised to 2,400 is highly polarizing and heavily weighted to value. I have Q♦5♠ — two pair (Qs & 5s) — which is strong vs lots of single‑pair hands but loses to sets (JJ, 44), QJ/two‑pair and any full house. I only have 4 clean outs to make a full house (2 queens + 2 fives ≈ 8–9% on the river) while the pot (17,800) requires ~38% equity to justify a 10,900 call. Calling would commit almost my entire stack (leaving ~1,950) for a small chance to improve and against a range that very plausibly has me crushed. I planned to shrink back to a large 3‑bet, and this spot meets that criterion — fold and preserve chips for better spots.

Preflop on the button with 9h3d offsuit, a speculative trash hand with minimal equity against any competent raising range. Pot is 150 with 100 to call, implying a standard raise to 200 (min-raise 200 confirms). Poor implied odds due to hand's lack of playability postflop—no straight potential without gaps filled ideally, no flush draw potential, dominated often. Opponents have shown aggression but check-calling tendencies; still, folding preserves stack for superior spots in a long tournament (50/150 hands). Position helps but insufficient for this marginal call.

I am in the Small Blind with 7h 4d, which is a very weak hand. Elon has folded on the button. While I could attempt a steal against Minni in the Big Blind, this specific hand realizes equity poorly and Minni has been quite active in defending her blinds or raising pre-flop. Given that I have 8,100 chips and we are only 1/3 of the way through the tournament, there is no need to commit chips with such a marginal holding. Folding is the most disciplined and standard play here.

I am on the button with a strong starting hand, Queen-Ten of hearts. In a 3-handed game, this is a premium hand that plays very well in position. Standard strategy is to open-raise to 3x the big blind to exert pressure on the blinds and build a pot with a range advantage. My stack is healthy enough at 81 BBs to play a standard game.

Hand: J♠6♣ in the small blind facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, to call 250). This is a marginal, unsuited hand that is frequently dominated by a button’s wide 3-handed opening range, and I don’t have the blockers or strength to profitably 3-bet as a bluff. However the call is cheap (250 into 450 ≈ 36% pot-odds) and the stacks are deep, so defending some portion of my range here to see a flop is reasonable. I’ll call and see a flop, play cautiously out of position, and fold to heavy aggression unless I connect well. Avoid bloating the pot with a weak hand — calling preserves stack and keeps options postflop.

Preflop in BB with TsJd offsuit facing a button min-raise to 300 (3BB) and SB call. Pot is 700, calling 200 more for ~3.5:1 direct odds, and implied odds are excellent with deep effective stacks (opponents ~78BB and 126BB, mine 189BB). Button range is wide (suited connectors, broadways, Ax, pairs), SB calling range also loose multiway. JTo has good playability, straight potential, and decent equity (~35-40% vs wide ranges). Folding is too tight OOP multiway; 3-betting offsuit is risky without blockers or suitedness. Calling to realize equity postflop, especially for set-mining or straight draws. Plan to play aggressively if flop hits well.

I called preflop with J♠6♣ to see a flop but completely missed: 4♥ 2♠ 8♣. I only have J‑high with a weak backdoor club (6♣) and almost no showdown equity. This is a relatively dry board and there are two opponents — bluffing or c‑betting multiway is generally unprofitable and I lack blockers to make a bluff credible. With deep stacks and out of position, the prudent line is to check and fold to meaningful pressure, preserve my stack, and only continue if I pick up real equity on the turn or face a very small probe bet that gives correct pot odds to call.

Flop of 4h-2s-8c completely whiffs my TsJd offsuit. No pair, no straight or flush draw (only backdoor spade), just overcards with marginal showdown value. Preflop called multiway with position postflop as BB last to act after SB check. Pot 900 multiway (SB and Button), deep stacks but my hand has poor playability if raised. Betting air here OOP risks folding out better and getting check-raised off equity; better to check for pot control, realize thin equity on later streets if checked through, or fold cheaply to c-bets from Button's wide range. No strong history indicating aggressive bluffs needed yet.

I have two overcards (Queen and Ten) and a backdoor flush draw on a dry flop of 4-2-8. As the pre-flop aggressor, it's standard to follow up with a continuation bet here. The board is unlikely to have connected strongly with the blinds' calling ranges, which often consist of middle-to-low connectors or suited hands. By betting around half the pot, I can fold out many hands that have equity against me (like Ace-high or King-high) or take the pot down immediately. If called, I still have six outs to top pair and potential for a flush draw on the turn.

Board is 4♥-2♠-8♣ and I hold J♠6♣ — I completely missed. Flash (button) c-bet 450 into a 1,350 pot. I have no pair and essentially no viable draws (a club backdoor flush is impossible from this spot), only a few thin pairing outs that will often still be behind. Calling 450 for ~25% pot odds requires meaningful equity I don’t have, and raising as a bluff out of position multiway is unlikely to be profitable. With deep stacks and many hands left, preserving chips for better spots is correct. I fold.

Facing a half-pot bet of 450 into 900 on a 4h2s8c rainbow flop with TsJd offsuit, which completely misses my holding—no pair, no draws, just worthless overcards and backdoor spade potential. Minni has folded, leaving us heads-up OOP vs Button, whose wide preflop raising range (suited connectors, broadways, Ax, pairs) continues frequently here with top pair, sets, overpairs, or draws. My equity is poor (~20-25% at best vs his betting range), and pot odds of ~3:1 don't justify calling without fold equity or implied odds, which are limited deep but irrelevant OOP with no hand. Bluff-catching with air is -EV long-term; folding preserves stack for premium spots in this tournament phase (hand 51/150, deep effective stacks). Consistent with preflop plan for pot control and folding to aggression when whiffed.

I hold 2♠6♦ — a very weak, unsuited hand with almost no playability. Even though I'm on the button, this is outside my profitable opening range in a 3‑handed spot because both blinds defend and 3‑bet here fairly often. Calling 100 to see a flop will too frequently leave me in dominated or marginal post‑flop spots and a small raise invites a reraise that I don't want to play out of position. My stack is healthy so there's no need to gamble with junk; the correct, lowest-variance play is to fold and wait for a much better opportunity to apply pressure.

Preflop in SB with T9o vs BB only (button folded). T9o has good playability with straight potential and can flop strong hands or draws. Excellent pot odds: pot 150 to call additional 50 (3.5:1 implied). Folding SB dead money is -EV long-term. Raising OOP risks vs wide BB defending range, better to realize equity postflop OOP by limping into a small pot. Opponent Flash defends BB sometimes per history but limps keep pot small for implied odds with deep stacks (18k/8.6k). Standard SB limp here to maximize EV over 150 hands.

In the Big Blind with Ace-Five offsuit, I am facing a limp from Elon in the Small Blind after Minni folded the Button. While raising is a valid option to take control of the hand and potentially win the pot immediately, checking to see a free flop is a solid, low-variance play that realizes the equity of my Ace-high without bloating the pot with a marginal hand. I will check and play the flop in position.

Flopped top pair (9s) with Ten kicker on a dry board (2d 9c 6h). No strong draws apparent: no flush draws completed, limited straight draws possible (e.g., T7, 75 for gutshots, wheel draws unlikely). Flash's BB checking range after SB limp is wide, including Ax, worse pairs (77-33, 55, TT?), overcards, suited connectors. As OOP aggressor preflop via limp, c-bet for thin value against worse hands (Ax, smaller pairs), protection vs overcards/live cards, and to charge draws if any. Half-pot sizing (125 into 200) builds pot while denying equity to floats/improvers. Deep stacks allow postflop flexibility if raised. Checking risks giving free cards or facing tough river spots OOP.

In this hand, I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Five offsuit. After the Small Blind (Elon) completed and I checked, the flop came 2-9-6 rainbow. Elon led out for 125 into a pot of 200. This is a fairly dry board, and while I have missed, Ace-high still has significant equity against a Small Blind's completion range, which can include many weaker hands and air. Since I am in position and the bet size is relatively small, I will call to see how the turn develops. Raising here is an option but risks bloating the pot with a weak hand, whereas calling allows me to potentially win at showdown if the board runs out favorably or if Elon stops betting.

Turn pairs the 9s, giving me trip nines (9s Th) with a Ten kicker on a 2d-9c-6h-9d board. This is a monster hand, far ahead of Flash's wide BB defending range after my SB limp and flop c-bet call (Ax, smaller pairs like 77-88, T-high, 87 for str8 draw, weak diamonds). Board is dry with limited draws: possible 78 str8, diamond flush draw (but only two diamonds), no wheel threats strong. Paired board kills some floats/improvers and doesn't heavily help his range. Deep effective stacks (his 8475 vs pot 450), so value bet to extract from worse pairs/top pair/draws that call. Half-to-2/3 pot sizing (300 into 450) builds pot without scaring off Ax/Tx/smaller pairs, denies equity to draws, and sets up river jam if he floats. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards/draws or facing free showdown. Betting maximizes EV in tournament with 98 hands left.

Elon led the flop and is now firing a second barrel on a paired board. While my Ace-high had some merit on the flop, the nine pairing on the turn makes it less likely I can win at showdown without improving. I have no draws, and calling here likely commits me to a difficult decision on the river against an opponent who is showing strength. I will fold and preserve my chips for a more favorable situation.

Preflop on the button with Jc7s offsuit, SB folded, facing only BB Minni's 100. Pot 150, to call 100. J7o is a profitable open-raise from button vs BB in a deepstack tournament setting (top ~45% of hands). Minni's history shows wide calling ranges preflop but passive check-calling postflop tendencies, making a raise + position advantageous for value/thin value and steals. Standard sizing is 3x BB (300 total) given frequent 300 raises in history. Builds pot heads-up OOP for villain, initiative gained. Folding too tight, limping too passive/multiway invite risk unnecessary.

I am in the Small Blind with 9c 2s, which is a very weak hand. The Button (Elon) has opened with a raise to 300. Given the poor quality of my cards and being out of position for the rest of the hand, the most prudent and standard play is to fold.

Elon (button) opened to 300. I’m in the BB with A♠8♠ and ~125BB effective. A8s is a hand I want to leverage: it blocks a lot of Ax combinations and has good post‑flop playability, but calling and playing passively OOP is unattractive. I’ll 3‑bet to apply pressure and isolate his wide button open — a raise to 1000 total gives meaningful fold equity without bloating the pot given deep stacks. If he folds I take the pot; if he calls I still have good equity and can proceed cautiously OOP; if he 4‑bets big I’ll fold A8s to avoid playing dominated vs a polarized range (I may consider a call to a small 4‑bet depending on sizing).

Facing a 3bet to 1000 (3.3x my BTN open to 300) from Minni in BB, with Jc7s offsuit. J7o is a standard BTN open but bottom-tier in my range (~45% hands), with poor postflop playability, dominated equity vs typical BB 3bet range (15-25% hands like pairs, broadways, suited aces/connectors), and weak blockers for bluffing. Calling 700 OOP for me? No, I'm IP vs BB heads-up, but SPR ~9 deep, reverse implied odds bad if I hit marginal hands. 4bet bluff possible but J7o not ideal (no strong blockers), and Minni calls/5bets light sometimes per history (e.g., Hand 5 3bet shove-fold spots). Her preflop aggression noted (frequent opens/3bets), but folding weak opens to 3bets preserves stack (186BB deep) for premium spots in 3-handed tournament. Pot odds ~2:1 insufficient for equity needed (~33%). Best play: fold.

I am on the button with Jh9h, which is a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game. My goal is to open for a standard raise of 3x the big blind to exert pressure and take advantage of my position. Both opponents in the blinds are active, but this hand has great post-flop playability even if called.

Facing a button open to 300 from Flash while I’m in the small blind with 3♠6♠. This is a very weak, low suited connector that will be dominated by a large portion of the opener’s range and has poor playability out of position. Calling 250 into a 450 pot gives me ~35.7% immediate pot odds, but my realistic equity vs the Button’s opening range (and the risk of a BB 3-bet from Elon) is well below that. A light 3-bet isn’t attractive either — 3♠6♠ lacks good blockers and will struggle postflop if called. With deep stacks I can wait for a better spot; fold and preserve chips for higher equity situations.

A7s is a strong suited ace from the big blind facing a button raise after the small blind folds. Button opening ranges are wide (likely 30-50% of hands), and A7s has excellent equity against that range (~45-50% vs random broadway/gappers/suited), blocks AA/AKs, and plays well postflop with flush/straight potential. 3-betting isolates the button, denies equity to weaker hands, builds a pot when ahead, and our deep stacks (18k effective vs 8k) allow flexibility. Folding is too tight, calling risks multiway if somehow, but SB folded. Sizing to 900 (3x their raise) is standard, balanced, and gets value/call from worse like Axs, KQs, suited connectors. If they 4bet, we have fold equity and can call profitably sometimes.

I opened from the button with Jh9h, and Elon 3-bet from the big blind. Jh9h is a strong suited connector that plays very well in position, especially in a 3-handed game where ranges are wider. The pot odds are favorable (calling 600 to win a total pot of 1850), and I have the benefit of acting last on all subsequent streets. Folding would be too conservative, and 4-betting is unnecessary as I can realize my equity better by seeing a flop in position.

Preflop 3-bet with A7s was standard as a strong blocker hand with playability, isolating the button's wide range. Flop Ad-4c-2h hits my ace for top pair (As pairing the board's Ad), weak 7 kicker, but strong vs button's calling range which includes weaker Ax (A10-A5), broadway, suited connectors/pairs that missed or have lower pair/draws. Board is relatively dry (few strong draws: gutshots with 3/5, no flush draws completed), ace-high favors my range. As preflop aggressor acting first heads-up, c-betting for value/thin value from worse Ax/pairs, protection against gutshots/improvers, and to deny equity. Pot control less ideal with TPTK on coordinated board. Sizing ~60% pot (1100 into 1850) balances value from Ax/draws while keeping SPR manageable (~5:1 effective post-bet), allows room for turns/rivers. Fold equity if he has air, but primarily value-oriented. Vs his 7525 stack, pressure without overcommitting.

On a flop of Ad-4c-2h, my J-high hand with a backdoor heart draw has almost no equity against Elon's range, which significantly favors the pre-flop 3-better. Elon has many Aces in his range (AK, AQ, AJ, ATs) as well as pocket pairs. Given that I have no pair and no direct draw, and the board is very static, there is no reason to float or bluff here. Folding is the correct decision to preserve my stack.

3-handed on the button with K♣2♦. Pot is 150 and it’s a standard spot to open a wide stealing range — I have position on both blinds and can fold to big aggression if needed. K2o is marginal but has a king-blocker and is fine as a button open to pick up the blinds or play a small pot in position. I’ll raise to a standard 3x-ish size (300 total) to apply pressure while keeping plenty of stack behind. Plan: fold to a sizable 3-bet (hand is poorly placed versus 3-bet ranges); if called, c-bet favorable flops and play cautiously on ace-high/connected boards unless I improve.

Preflop in SB with A9o facing a button raise to 300 from Minni, who is aggressive and raises frequently from late position based on hand history. A9o is a strong hand here: it blocks AA, dominates broadway hands like KQo/AJo that Minni might open wide, and has good postflop playability. Position is poor if I call, so 3-betting isolates vs button and puts pressure on BB Flash. Standard 3-bet size is ~3x the open (900). Pot will be attractive, and with deep stacks (my 19.5k, Minni 12.6k, Flash 7.4k), this builds the pot when ahead. Folding too tight, calling invites multiway pot OOP.

I am holding Ten-Eight of spades in the Big Blind. Minni opened from the Button, and Elon 3-bet from the Small Blind. This 3-bet from the SB into a BU open typically signifies a strong range. While 8sTs is a decent speculative hand, calling 800 more (representing about 11% of my current stack) to play a bloated pot out of position against one or two opponents is not ideal. The hand does not have enough equity against a standard 3-betting range to justify a call, and 4-betting as a bluff is too risky given the stack sizes and the strength shown by Elon. I will fold and wait for a more favorable spot.

I opened the button to 300 with Kc2d as a standard steal. Elon (SB) 3-bet to 900 — a sizable, standard 3‑bet size. K2o is poorly placed vs a 3‑bet range (often dominated by Kx and crushed by Ax/pairs) and has terrible playability post‑flop despite having position. Calling 600 to see a flop gives very little equity and invites difficult decisions or larger bets on later streets; a light 4‑bet here (min to 1500) is too thin and can be punished by a call or a shove. With a healthy stack (12,600) and lots of hands left, preserving chips and folding to a sizable 3‑bet is the +EV line. I’ll fold and wait for a better spot to apply pressure.

3-handed on the button in an unraised pot (SB limped, BB checked). Qd4c is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity multiway. Even in position, it's below the stealing range against limpers who have shown tendency to call wide (especially Minni). Folding preserves stack for premium spots in this tournament phase, avoiding speculative situations with marginal equity.

I am in the Small Blind with Ten-Five offsuit. The Button (Elon) has folded, leaving just me and Minni in the Big Blind. T5o is a very weak hand to play out of position. While completing the blind is cheap, playing such a weak hand against an aggressive opponent like Minni post-flop is generally a losing strategy. I will choose to fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with 87o. This is a marginal hand, but in a three-handed game, it's often worth a standard raise to steal the blinds or play in position. However, looking at the table dynamics, Minni and Elon have been quite active and aggressive recently. Given the 150-hand limit and my current stack size of 7,375, I want to avoid unnecessary losses with weak hands. I will choose to fold this hand and wait for a better spot to utilize my chips.

Heads-up from the small blind with Q8s (suited) — a fairly strong, playable hand in this spot. Deep stacks and a single opponent make stealing/playing for initiative profitable. A 2x big-blind raise to 200 gets the pot rolling, gains fold equity, and lets me see a flop with position/initiative; if Elon 3-bets large I can re-evaluate without committing a big portion of my stack. Calling here is too passive and surrenders initiative. I raise to the minimum standard size to pressure the big blind and keep postflop options wide.

Preflop in the big blind with 2c7h offsuit, one of the worst possible hands with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. Facing a raise to 200 from Minni in the small blind after button folds, the pot is 300 and I need to call 100 more for ~3.3:1 odds. However, my hand has zero equity against any reasonable raising range from SB, which includes many strong hands, broadways, pairs, and suited connectors. Minni has shown aggression in prior hands but folding trash here preserves stack for better spots in a 3-handed tournament with deep stacks (mine 19875, hers 12450). No fold equity if raising, and calling bloats pot OOP with a hand dominated postflop. Clear fold to maximize long-term EV.

I’m on the button with 2♦3♠, blinds 50/100, pot 150 and it would cost 100 to see the flop. This is a very weak, unsuited, low-connected hand with poor post‑flop equity and little chance to realize value against the SB/BB defending ranges. Calling 100 commits me to a multiway pot where I’ll be dominated or face aggression; raising as a steal is possible but both opponents have shown willingness to defend/3‑bet and this hand is not a profitable 3‑bet bluff candidate. With a healthy 12.7k stack (≈127 BB) I’ll preserve chips and wait for a better spot. Fold.

Preflop in SB vs BB after BTN folds. Hand 4c3h is trash: disconnected, offsuit, low cards with minimal equity and terrible postflop playability OOP. Pot 150, to call 50 (33% pot odds), but range too weak vs BB defense. Raising has low fold equity vs likely wide BB range and poor playability if called. Folding preserves stack for better spots in tournament with 198 BB effective.

3h7d is a trash hand: low, offsuit, gapped connector at best but poor playability. In 3-handed play on the button first to act preflop, opening ranges are wide but 73o is still a clear fold. Limping invites multiway pots where this hand performs poorly postflop. Deep stacks (200BB effective), no need to risk chips on a hand with poor equity and reverse implied odds. Wait for better spots to maximize EV over remaining 91 hands.

I am in the Small Blind with 5s 2h, which is one of the weakest starting hands in poker. The Button has folded, leaving only me and Minni (the Big Blind). While it's tempting to try and steal the blinds, 52o is too weak to defend post-flop out of position, and Minni has shown enough aggression in previous hands that a fold is the most prudent way to preserve my stack.

I am on the button with KTo (King of Spades, Ten of Clubs) in a three-handed game. This is a strong opening hand from this position. I'll make a standard 3x raise to 300 to take control of the pot and potentially win the blinds or play post-flop with position.

Call to 300 total (250 more). 4♦8♦s is a speculative, suited connector with decent multi-way equity and flush potential; with 127 BB effective and a wide button open in a 3-handed game it's worth defending. Folding is too tight versus a standard 300 open, and 3-betting as a bluff OOP with this hand is overly ambitious — I don't want to build a big pot out of position or face a shove/3-bet from Elon. The pot is 450, so calling 250 more gives attractive immediate pot odds (~1.8:1). Plan: fold to a large 3-bet, otherwise see the flop and proceed cautiously — pursue diamonds/connected flops, otherwise keep the pot small and concede to heavy aggression.

2d7c is a complete trash hand with no playability, suitedness, or high card strength. Facing a button raise from Flash and a call from Minni in the SB, their ranges are wide but still crush this hand. Defending wide in BB OOP is possible deepstacked, but this specific hand has negligible equity, poor postflop playability, and reverse implied odds. Pot odds are about 3:1 (700:200), but equity vs even wide calling range is under 30%, not justifying a call. Folding preserves stack for better spots.

Flop K♣ 7♦ 2♠ with 4♦8♦ in the small blind: I have no pair and only a backdoor diamond (I hold two diamonds and the flop has one) — I need both turn+river diamonds to make a flush (≈(10/47)*(9/46) ≈ 4.3%). My chance to make at least a pair by the river is ≈24%, but any pair I make will often be second-best against the button’s opening range (many Kx, 7x, and overcards). Flash opened preflop from the button and will continue with a wide but often-connected range; out of position I don’t want to barrel off or build a big pot with essentially air. Pot is 700 and stacks are deep, so priority is pot control and realizing equity. Plan: check and see what the button does — call small continuation bets to see the turn if the price is right (I’ll defend versus small probes, fold to large/committing bets), and only become aggressive if I pick up a diamond or pair on the turn. For now: check.

I have top pair with a decent kicker on a very dry board (Kc 7d 2s). After Minni checks, I'll place a standard continuation bet for value and protection. A bet of 350 (50% of the pot) should get called by weaker kings, mid-pairs, or Ace-high hands while making it expensive for her to float.

Call 350. I have 4♦8♦ on K♣7♦2♠ — no pair and only a backdoor diamond draw (need two more diamonds on turn+river, ≈4.3%). Flash’s bet is a small half‑pot probe (350 into the current pot), so calling keeps the pot controlled and lets me realize equity against a wide c‑betting range.
