I’ll size to ~50% pot (300) to deny good odds to spade draws, extract from Kx/Qx/Tx and low pairs, but keep the pot controllable. Plan: if he folds I take it down; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously (check‑back or block-bet unless I improve or the turn is very safe). If he raises large, I’ll fold — A7o is not worth war with heavy aggression from a deep stack.

Qh3h completely whiffs the 4c8s2s flop: queen-high with no pair, no straight draw (gutshot to 5 is negligible), only backdoor heart flush draw. Board favors pairs (sets/two-pair rare but possible), spade flush draws, wheel straight draws (A5, 35 suited), and overcards in Minni's wide button range. Her small 300 bet into 600 pot (now 900) is a standard weak c-bet or probe from an aggressive player like Minni (frequent raises/calls historically). Pot odds ~3:1 require ~25% equity to call, but Q3h has minimal equity (~10-15% at best vs her polarized range including Ax, pairs, draws). Raising has poor fold equity given her stack (79BB) and history of calling stations postflop; she'd continue wide with draws/top pairs. No showdown value, deep stacks (my 311xBB vs her 79BB) favor folding trash to preserve stack for better spots over 49 hands left. Discipline over speculative calling.

Heads-up on the button/SB with J♠4♥ offsuit. This is a speculative hand with some straight potential, but weak overall. However, position is excellent, pot odds are great (150 pot, calling 50 gives 3:1), and dead money from blinds makes it profitable to raise wide. SB raise-first-in range heads-up is ~45-50%, and J4o is playable for thin value and steal equity. Minni's stack is 87BB, mine deep at 311BB. Standard sizing ~3x BB, raise to 300 to build pot or take it down pre. Folding too nitty, limping invites trouble postflop.

Holding 3♠2♥ in the big blind facing an open to 300 from the button is a very weak, unsuited hand with poor playability OOP. The pot is 400 and it costs 200 to call, so I need ≈33% equity to break even. 3‑2 offsuit will be significantly disadvantaged vs a normal button opening range and will be dominated postflop a large portion of the time. With deep stacks the button can apply heavy pressure postflop; a light 3‑bet bluff is possible but 3‑2o is too weak to do it profitably. Preserving my 8,725 chip stack and waiting for a better spot is the correct long‑term play, so I fold here.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the small blind) with 5♣3♥ — a weak hand, but in heads-up play the button should be opening a wide range to apply pressure. Pot is 150, to call costs me 50 more (total committed 100). With deep-ish stacks (I have ~86.7 BB) I want to keep SPR reasonable while taking the initiative and using position postflop. A min open to 2x BB (200 total) achieves that: it buys fold equity for cheap, preserves stack depth if called, and lets me play postflop with the positional advantage. Plan: fold 5‑3o to any sizable 3‑bet (too weak to continue as a 4‑bet bluff), but if called I'll c-bet favorable, dry flops and play cautiously on wet/connected boards — check/control or fold to heavy aggression. Therefore raise to 200 to try to steal the blind and keep options postflop.

Heads-up against Minni on the button (SB position). She min-raised to 200 preflop, which is a standard small raise from this position with a very wide range (likely 50-70% of hands). My K6o has a king blocker, blocking some of her strong hands like AK/AQ, and provides decent equity (around 40-45%) against her opening range. Pot odds are excellent: 100 to call into a pot of 300 (3:1 immediate odds), and implied odds are good given deep effective stacks (her 8.5k). OOP defense is necessary in BB vs BTN/SB, and K6o is comfortably in the calling range rather than folding (too tight) or 3-betting (mediocre blocker/suitless). Plan to play postflop carefully, using position disadvantage by check-raising strong hands or floating selectively.

Preflop: Defended BB wide vs Minni's minraise from BTN/SB with K6o, which has good playability, positionally disadvantaged but solid blocker and equity (~40% vs wide range). Flop 5d9s6h hits me perfectly for middle pair (6s) with top kicker (Kc), strong showdown value and protection against her polarized wide range (overpairs like JJ+, sets like 99/66/55 rare; draws like 78 OESD, spade flush draws like AsXs, gutshots). Board is draw-heavy (78 straight draw ~15% equity, spades ~9% to nuts), so must bet to deny equity and build pot OOP. Pot 400, her 8525 effective stack allows multi-street value. Bet sizing: ~60% pot (250) balances value from worse pairs/draws (77-TT, A-high, 87 suited), protection, and fold equity vs air/overcards while keeping SPR manageable (~25:1 post-bet). Avoid check (gives free cards to draws/overpairs check-raise strong); no raise/oversize needed. Plan: If called, barrel turn strength/draw-heavy; if shoved, good price to call lighter.

I flopped a pair of fives (5h) on a 9s-6h-5d board after opening preflop — a decent heads-up hand but with a weak 3 kicker. Pot is 650 and Elon’s small lead of 250 is likely a wide c-bet (overcards, floats, some weak pairs); calling 250 gives me ~27.8% pot odds, and my pair is ahead of a large portion of his range. Raising would bloat the pot and put me in tough spots against 9x or bigger hands; folding surrenders value unnecessarily. I’ll call to keep worse hands and bluffs in, control the pot size, and re-evaluate on the turn: if I improve I’ll value-bet, if a scary card comes I’ll pot-control or fold to heavy aggression.

Turn board 2c is a low, disconnected card that doesn't significantly change the board texture—still draw-heavy from flop (straights like 47/78 possible but incomplete; spade draw bricked; minor club draw introduced but low equity). I hold middle pair top kicker (6s with Kc), which remains very strong against Minni's wide BTN minraise/call range: ahead of her floats, missed draws (e.g., A-high spade draws ~30% equity now), weaker pairs (77-88, JTs), and some Ax like A7/A8. Vulnerable to sets (99/66/55 rare <5%), two-pairs (e.g., 95/96 unlikely), or trips (97s possible but thin). OOP with ~9bb effective pot (900 pot, her 8275), betting continues value extraction from worse (Ax/draws call ~40% time) while charging protection vs straight draws/improved hands and denying free rivers. Sizing ~2/3 pot (600) balances value/protection/fold equity, keeps SPR ~11:1 post-bet for river flexibility (can barrel or check-call strong). Checking risks giving free cards to draws/overcards or check-raise setups; multi-street value best vs her sticky IP calling tendencies seen in history (e.g., Hands 14,20,35,46,73,78,99). Plan: Value bet thin; if called, river bet bigger vs draws or check strong; fold to raise.

Call 600. I have top pair (5♣5♦) with a very weak 3 kicker on 9♠-6♥-5♦-2♣. Pot is 1500 and Elon’s 600 is a relatively small turn bet (~40% pot) — in heads-up play this sizing is consistent with a wide continuing range (overcards, floats, bluffs and some 9x/6x or two-pair combos). Calling requires ~28.6% equity and keeps worse hands and bluffs in the pot while avoiding bloating the pot or polarizing my range by raising with a marginal hand. I’ll call to control pot size, extract value from his weaker holdings or bluffs, and then re-evaluate on the river — bet for value if he shows weakness or I improve, but concede to clear strength.

River 10h completes some straight draws like 78 (now 6-7-8-9-10) and 87 (5-6-7-8-9), and improves 10x hands to top pair, but my middle pair top kicker (6s Kc) remains strong against Minni's capped calling range after two calls IP to my OOP bets. Her minraise pre and calls suggest overpairs (TT+ somewhat unlikely given board), weaker pairs (77/88/JT), Ax floats that missed spades/hearts, or two-pair combos like 95/56 which are narrow. Vulnerable to completed straights/sets/two-pair (~20-30% of her range?), but ahead of ~40-50% (missed draws, weaker pairs like 77/A-high). Betting extracts value from worse pairs (10x with bad kicker, Jx/9x missed, 5x) and overpairs that call thin, while charging any missed draws for protection. Check risks checking down worse or facing a value raise; multi-street aggression continues thin value line vs her history of calling stations (e.g., Hands 14,35,46,73,78,99 where she called multiple bets light). Sizing ~60% pot (1300 into 2100) offers good odds for calls from 70% equity hands, keeps SPR ~5:1 post-bet for her shove range manageable (fold to raise if overbet). Plan: Bet-call all-in lighter or fold to raise; maximizes EV long-term in deep tournament.

I have top pair (5♥) with a very weak 3 kicker on 9♠‑6♥‑5♦‑2♣‑T♥. Pot is 3400 and calling 1300 requires ~27.7% equity (1300/(3400+1300)). Elon’s line — calling pre, small flop c‑bet (250), small turn bet (600) and another relatively small river bet (1300) — is consistent with a wide heads‑up continuing range (overcards/floaters, some 9x/Tx/6x, and bluffs). My pair beats most floats and missed overcards and likely has >27–28% showdown equity versus his range, so the pot odds justify a call. Raising to 2600 would polarize and commit more chips with a marginal one‑pair hand; it will often fold the hands I want to extract value from and get called by the hands that beat me, so it’s thin and unnecessary. I’ll call to realize equity and see the showdown.

Heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks (my 335BB vs Minni's 63BB). Standard strategy is to raise a very wide range pre-flop from SB to apply pressure and steal the dead money in the pot (150 total). 75o is a marginal hand but within the profitable raising range due to high fold equity from BB defense ranges. Even if called, I have position pre-flop and can play post-flop OOP with initiative. Raising to 3x (300) is a standard sizing for value and bluffs. Folding or limping is too passive and misses EV.

Call 300. Elon opened to 300 from the button (3bb). I’m in the big blind with Q♠9♣ — a marginal but playable hand heads‑up. Pot is 400 and I need 200 more to call, giving me ~33% immediate pot odds; versus a wide button open my hand has enough equity and postflop playability to defend. I don’t want to 3‑bet OOP with a hand that’s often dominated and hard to play if 4‑bet; calling preserves stack, keeps the pot manageable, and lets me see a flop where I can continue on Q‑ or 9‑high or spade cards or fold to heavy aggression on truly unfavorable boards.

I have Q♠9♣ on A♥4♣9♠ — second pair (9s) with a decent kicker, and I'm first to act on the flop (out of position for the rest of the hand). Pot is 600, my stack 6,075 (effective stack ≈ 6k) so SPR is ~10. Versus the button's wide HU opening range he will c‑bet a lot but also has many Ax and better 9x that beat me. Betting out here bloats the pot out of position and is easier for a deep opponent to exploit (by calling with better or floating), so I prefer pot control and information. Checking keeps weaker hands and bluffs in his range (I can call reasonable c‑bets and fold to extreme overbets), and lets me realize equity cheaply if he checks back. Plan: check — call a normal c‑bet and re‑evaluate on the turn; bet/raise only if I improve or pick up a clear fold/raise opportunity.

Flop Ah4c9s is a good c-bet spot for my button/SB raising range. It has an Ace, which blocks some of Minni's calling hands, and the board is somewhat disconnected, favoring my range advantage. 75o has no showdown value but decent playability with position and backdoor draws. Minni checked OOP after calling pre, likely weak or trapping rarely; betting for fold equity takes down the pot often, maintaining initiative. Sizing 2/3 pot (400) balances value (overpairs, Ax) and bluffs. Checking gives up too much equity and lets Minni realize equity cheaply.

I have Q♠9♣ on A♥4♣9♠ — second pair with a decent kicker. Elon (button) c‑bet 400 into a ~600 pot (pot now ~1000); calling 400 gives me ~28.6% immediate pot odds and my hand has solid showdown value vs a wide HU c‑betting range. Raising OOP here is risky because many of his combos (Ax, A9, better 9x) dominate and I don't want to bloat the pot out of position with a marginal hand. Plan: call to control pot size, keep his bluffs and worse pairs in, then re‑evaluate on the turn — I’ll value/raise if I improve (Q/9) or proceed cautiously/fold to heavy turn aggression if the board gets worse (an Ace or dangerous coordinated card).

I have Q♠9♣ and the turn Q♥ pairs my queen — I now have two pair (Q9). Heads‑up this is a very strong hand: only combos that currently beat me are A9/AQ and sets/full houses (99/44/AA/QQ) which are a minority of the button’s range. Elon opened wide preflop and c‑bet the flop, so his calling range contains lots of A‑x, single Q’s and medium pairs that will pay to see another street. Pot is 1,400 and my stack is 5,675 (SPR ≈4), so I should extract value but keep the pot reasonably sized. I’ll bet for value to charge worse Ax, Qx and many 9x/medium pairs and deny a free river card. A half‑to‑two‑thirds pot sizing hits that sweet spot — big enough to get calls from worse hands, small enough to avoid committing the stack to only a few beating combos. Bet 800. Plan: if called, re‑evaluate on the river and target thin value on safe runouts; if raised strongly, respect the raise (he could have A9/AQ/sets) and re‑assess (call smaller raises, fold to extreme aggression).

Facing a turn donk-lead of 800 into 2200 after calling my flop c-bet with complete air (75o misses the Ah4c9sQh board entirely: no pair, no straight draw, only weak backdoor heart). Minni's range for calling pre and flop OOP includes Ax, pairs (99,44,94s), draws, and now turn Qh improves Qx, gutshots (TJo), or flush draws. Her lead OOP shows strength or semi-bluff, and while I have position and deep stack (329BB vs her ~49BB effective 4875), raising bluff lacks fold equity given her history of calling stations (frequent calls on flops/turns/rivers with marginal hands). Pot odds 2200:800 ~2.75:1 insufficient for 0 equity hand; folding preserves chips for superior spots where I can realize equity or bluff profitably. Consistent with GTO: fold missed c-bet bluffs to resistance.

Heads-up on the button (I act last) with Qc5s — a marginal hand but position is a big advantage. Pot is 150 and I only need to put 50 more to see a flop, but opening a wide range from the button is standard in heads-up play to seize initiative. A min-raise to 200 (2x BB) is a small, standard sizing: it pressures the big blind, keeps the pot manageable with a speculative hand, and lets me play postflop with position. Plan: if Elon cold-calls I’ll C-bet and proceed cautiously; if he 3-bets large I’ll fold Q5o (it’s often dominated), re-evaluating only against small squeezes where pot odds/implied odds make a call reasonable. Calling the 50 would be passive and folding surrenders too much; raising to 200 gives the best mix of aggression and risk management here.

Preflop in the big blind facing a small raise to 200 from Minni on the button (her stack 6875 effective). Pot is 300, calling 100 more for pot odds of about 4:1 (25% equity needed). A3o is offsuit but has a strong ace blocker, live cards, and decent playability postflop. Minni's history shows frequent loose-aggressive opens from late position. Defending wide here is standard to realize equity and use position effectively postflop. Folding too tight; raising lacks value or fold equity justification. Call and see flop.

Heads-up on a dry 8-6-3 rainbow flop, I have bottom pair with ace kicker (Ac3s pairing the 3d), which is strong on this board given the low coordinated but uncoordinated texture. Few draws (straight draws like 45, 57, but rainbow reduces flush potential). Minni's button min-raise preflop and check-flop suggests weakness or a trap, but her history shows loose-aggressive play and frequent folds to postflop aggression (e.g., Hands 2,12,14,23,32, etc.). With position (BB acts last in heads-up), effective stack 6875 (~17BB for her, but deep for pot), and pot 400, a value bet extracts from her worse pairs (e.g., A-high, weaker pairs) or floats. Betting ~2/3 pot (275) builds pot while denying equity to draws or floats. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards or draws. Folding not applicable. Raising N/A.

Call 275. Qc5s on 6♥3♦8♣ is a marginal holding (queen-high) with only backdoor possibilities, but the bet is relatively small. The pot is 675 and calling 275 to win ~950 requires ~29% equity; my chance to pair by the river or hit the backdoor flush is roughly 24% + ~5% (overlap adjusted ≈28%), so this is a razor‑thin decision. I choose to realize equity in position rather than folding away equity or risking a bluff-raise that will be costly if called or 3-bet. Plan: call and reassess on the turn — if I pair or pick up a meaningful draw I can proceed for value or pressure; if the turn bricks and Elon fires a substantive barrel I’ll fold.

Heads-up pot of 950 on the turn board 6h-3d-8c-2h, holding Ac3s for bottom pair top kicker OOP. Hand is well-disguised and strong against Minni's loose-aggressive calling range from button min-raise pre (Ax, smaller pairs like 77-99/44-55, gutshots like 45/57, overcards). Flop bet polarized my range, her call narrows her to having equity or medium strength. Turn 2h adds a weak heart draw (backdoorish) and possible gutshots (e.g., 45), but doesn't complete strong draws and improves my relative strength slightly vs pure air. History shows Minni calls stations multi-street (e.g., Hands 14,35,73,78,99) but folds to aggression at times. Checking risks free cards to overcards/draws and induces bluffs I can call; however, value betting thin extracts from her worse pairs/A-high/floats while building pot for river. Bet sizing ~2/3 pot (600) denies equity, gets value from ~30-40% of her range, keeps fold equity vs bluffs, and pot-commits her lightly (6600 stack). Raising/folding irrelevant. Deep effective stack but her shorter; maximize EV by betting.

On the turn I have Qc5s on 6♥-3♦-8♣-2♥. Elon fired a second barrel (600 into a 1550 pot). To call 600 I need ≈21.8% equity (600 / 2750). My realistic outs on the river are: 3 queens, 3 fives and 4 fours = 10 outs → ~21.7% raw, but several of those outs are often “dirty” (they can still lose to hands Elon is polar to betting here: pairs, two-pair, sets). I have no backdoor flush and only one card to come, so my real equity is at or below break-even. Raising as a bluff would commit more chips with very limited fold equity versus his line (pot-building on flop and turn suggests at least a pair). Given the math, his line, and preserving my 6600 stack in a long tournament, folding is the correct, least costly option.

6h4d is offsuit with a gap, very weak hand lacking high card strength, suitedness, or straight potential in heads-up. Preflop from SB/Button, standard ranges include it as a limp or small raise candidate to steal or play position, but Minni has been sticky in history (frequent calls with marginal hands). With deep effective stack (her 65BB), risking chips OOP postflop with poor equity (~30-35% vs typical BB defending range) and bad implied odds. Better to fold and wait for stronger spot; 44 hands left to build stack selectively without spew. Pot odds not compelling (150 pot, 50 to call marginal, but raise preferred if playing; folding tightens image for later steals.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the SB) with A♣7♥ and 66 BB effective. This is a clear open-raise spot — A7o is well ahead of a BB calling range and has good playability postflop. I want the initiative and fold equity, so I’ll raise rather than just calling the extra 50. I’ll size to 3xBB (300 total): big enough to pressure many worse hands and extract value from weaker aces/pairs, but not so large that I commit too much of my stack. If Elon 3-bets big I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold versus a large 3-bet, call versus a small–medium one depending on sizing); calling now would be too passive and folding is too tight. Raise to 300 total (250 more chips).

Facing a button min-raise to 300 from Minni (63.5BB stack) with Jd9s in BB (33k stack). J9s is a strong suited connector with good playability, positionally disadvantaged OOP but excellent equity vs. her wide button opening range (~40-50% hands). Pot odds are favorable (200 to call into 400 pot, ~33% equity needed; J9s realizes much more). Implied odds strong if we flop draws or pairs. Her aggression from history suggests she CBets often, allowing check-raise bluffs or value. No fold equity concern preflop. Calling balances defense range, extracts value from worse. 3bet too strong without blockers; flat preserves pot OOP for multi-street play.

Preflop called button minraise OOP with J9s suited connector for its playability, multiway potential, and equity realization against wide button range. Flop 7s Kh 7c completely misses: no pair, no flush draw (backdoor spades at best), no straight draw, pure air with 9-high. Paired board reduces her bluffing somewhat but still favors her cbets with Kx, 7x, draws. OOP first to act, donk betting air is spewy and blocks value; check preserves fold equity and induces bluffs/value to fold behind if checked through. Pot 600 small vs stacks (my 330BB, her 63BB); check-fold standard to pot control and avoid tough rivers OOP with no equity. Her history shows aggression but also calls stations; no spot to hero here. Maximize EV by folding bad flops.

I have trip 7s with the A kicker on 7s Kh 7c — a very strong, near-nut hand on a dry, paired board. Elon checked, so with position I should be extracting value rather than checking behind and giving free cards. Villain’s calling range from the BB includes many Kx, overpairs and Ax hands that will call a decent flop bet. I’ll size to ~2/3 pot (400 into 600) — big enough to charge top pairs and overpairs but not so large as to fold out most worse hands. If he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and continue extracting; if he check-raises big I’ll assume a very narrow full-house-heavy range and re-evaluate (likely fold to an enormous shove, call smaller/medium pressure depending on sizing).

Facing a 400 bet into 1000 pot (~28% pot odds) OOP with complete air on a paired K77 board. Jd9s has zero equity (no pair, no flush draw beyond backdoor spade, no straight draw). Minni's button minraise pre and small flop cbet fits her aggressive history but likely strong on this texture (Kx, sets, 7x overpairs). Calling commits to tough OOP rivers with no improvement needed from villain; raising as bluff overplays air without blockers or fold equity vs her range. Check-fold maximizes EV by avoiding realization issues and tough spots OOP. Preserve stack for better situations.

Heads-up on the button/SB with KJo, a strong hand in this spot. Standard play is to raise for initiative, to steal the blinds or play postflop in position if called. Minni's stack is 6850 (~68BB), mine is deep at 33000. Raising 3x the BB (to 300 total) is standard sizing preflop heads-up. No reason to limp or fold this hand given wide ranges.

Heads-up vs a standard 3x open from the button, Ad6h is a defensible Big Blind holding. Calling the extra 200 (making my total 300 in) keeps the pot controlled and lets me realize equity — the call is roughly 3:1 pot odds (~25% break-even), which A6o clears against a wide opening range. A tiny 3-bet to 500 has little fold equity and simply bloats a pot I’ll be out of position in; a larger 3-bet would commit too much with a hand often dominated by better Aces. I'll call to see a flop and proceed based on texture (look to extract value when I hit an Ace, fold or exercise pot control when I miss).

Heads‑up vs a wide button opening range — Ad6o has decent showdown value on 3♣‑5♠‑3♠ and an Ace blocker to some strong A3/Ax combos, but it’s out of position and currently just Ace‑high. Donk‑betting here would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and get called or raised by many hands that already beat me (3x, pocket pairs, 5x). I’ll check to control the pot, induce continuation bets from his wide range, and realize equity. Plan: check; call small–medium c‑bets to see a safe turn and fold to heavy pressure or a shove. If he checks back I can take a free card and consider betting the turn if I pick up an Ace or the runout is safe.

Preflop raiser with KJo heads-up in position on a paired 3c5s3s flop. Board is relatively dry but features a spade flush draw and pairs the trey, hitting some of villain's wide BB calling range (e.g., 3x, 53s, suited connectors) but missing many Ax, broadways, and overcards. My king-high has decent blockers (Js to spade draws, Kc to clubs somewhat), decent showdown value against bluffs/missed draws, and 6 outs to top pair if called. Minni checked, indicating weakness or trapping (unlikely with her history of calling stations). With deep stack (327BB effective vs her 66BB), standard to c-bet ~50% pot (300) for initiative, value from worse (worse Kx, Qx, draws), protection against spade draw/implied straight draws, and frequent folds from air. Checking risks giving free card to draws and loses value from worse hands. If called, re-eval turn with position.

Elon fires a small flop c-bet (300 into a 900 pot, ~1/3 pot). I checked and Ad6o has decent showdown value plus an important Ace blocker to A3/A5 combos — the bet only prices me in to continue (~25% break-even). Raising OOP with a marginal hand that’s often dominated by better Aces or pocket pairs would bloat the pot and give little fold equity; folding is too tight given the cheap price to see a turn. I’ll call the 300 to realize equity and re-evaluate on the turn: if I hit an A I can extract value; if he barrels big on a scary turn I can fold; if he checks back I can take a free card or bet thin depending on the card.

I have top pair (A6) on 3c-5s-3s-As after Elon checked the turn. He opened wide preflop and c-bet small on the flop, so his range is broad (many worse A-x, small pairs, and bluffs). My Ace blocks the worst disguised full-house combos (A3/A5), and checking lets him take a free river or bluff-catch me cheaply. I should charge his wide checking range and protect my hand from any spade/gutshot holdings. I’ll bet a medium size (~2/3 pot) to get called by worse A‑x and smaller pairs while folding out pure air and some draws. If he calls I’ll re-evaluate the river; if he produces a large check‑raise I’ll heavily discount my kicker and fold to avoid getting bloated against sets/strong Aces.

Heads-up in position on Button with KcJs facing a turn lead of 800 into 2000 pot after I c-bet flop and got called. Board: 3c5s3sAs - paired low cards, completed spade flush draw (I have the Js for 9-out flush draw ~19.5% equity standalone, plus K overcard with showdown value). Minni's BB calling range vs my BTN raise includes suited connectors, 3x, Ax/spade draws, pairs. Her check-call flop suggests trips, draw, or float; turn As lead could be strong (flush, ATrips, Ax), trap, or thin value/protection. However, my hand has excellent equity vs her range (~45%+ vs value-heavy, higher vs bluffy), position for river control, and implied odds given her 55BB stack (5550). Pot odds: 800 to win 2800 (~28%), far better than needed equity. Raising risks bloating pot OOP with draw (if she shoves flush), folding discards too much equity/FD value; calling keeps pot manageable, realizes equity, and allows river steal/check-fold/value bet. Consistent with deep-stack aggression and her history as a calling station who folds to river pressure.

The river 5 pairs the board and dramatically increases the frequency of full‑houses in Elon’s calling range (any 3 or 5 in his hand, pocket 33/55, A3/A5, etc.). He called my turn bet, which is consistent both with a wide range of Ax/small pairs and with slow‑played sets that now beat my two‑pair. I hold Ad which removes some A3/A5 combos, but plenty of full‑house/strong combos remain and Elon is very deep — betting risks getting check‑raised and committed for my tournament life for thin value against hands that will either tie or crush me. Checking is the safer, higher EV line: I avoid turning a marginal value bet into a bluff‑catcher against a depth that can trap me, and I can call a small river stab but fold to a large raise. Check and take showdown (or call a small sizing; fold to a big raise).

River board 3c5s3sAs5d pairs the fives, creating full house possibilities (53 boat, A5 boat) alongside existing trips/flush. My KcJs is king-high with a blocked spade (Js), decent bluff-catcher/SDV vs her BB calling range that check-called flop (3x, 5x, suited connectors/draws) and led turn (Ax, flush, trips value/protect). Her check after turn lead suggests possible medium strength (Ax no spade, trips wary of boats), trap (unlikely OOP), or weakness. Pot 2800, her 5550 (~56BB effective vs my deep 316BB), position allows thin value bet targeting worse Ax/Jx/OTD/weak pairs that call flop/turn but check river, plus fold equity vs air/busted draws. Checking misses value from worse hands with SDV and risks her having nothing. Bet ~40% pot (1100) balances value/protection/fold equity; if raised/shoved, fold to nuts. Consistent with deep-stack play, her calling station tendencies per history (calls light but folds river pressure sometimes), maximizes EV long-term.

Call 1100. Pot is 3900 and his river stab is 1100; calling 1100 wins a pot of 6100 so my break‑even equity is ~1100/6100 ≈ 18.0%. My Ad6 has become two pair (Aces and Fives) which loses to any 3 or 5 in his hand (those make full houses). Unconditional combinatorics (given the known cards) show roughly 170/990 ≈ 17.2% of two‑card combos contain a 3 or 5, so the bare frequency of hands that crush me is right around, or slightly below, the break‑even threshold. Elon’s tiny sizing is also consistent with thin value and bluffs (missed spade draws, weak pairs, floats), and my Ad blocks many A3/A5 combos, so calling the small bet to take it to showdown is the higher‑EV line. Raising would have almost no fold equity and would commit a large portion of my stack to a range that contains many full houses, while folding is too tight given the cheap price to see showdown. I’ll call the 1100 and re‑evaluate only if he produces a large raise.

Heads-up on the button with A♣8♠ — a strong, playable hand. I already have 50 in as the SB and can either call 50 or raise. I want the initiative and fold equity against the BB's wide range, and 2x the big blind (raise to 200 total) is a standard, low-variance aggressive open. It builds the pot when I'm likely ahead, keeps positional advantage post-flop, and protects against giving free cards. If Elon 3-bets large I'll re-evaluate (likely fold to extreme sizing, call smaller 3-bets), but open-raising to 200 is the +EV play here.

2h7s is one of the worst hands in poker, with terrible connectivity, no suitedness, and dominated by any raising range. Facing a raise to 200 from Minni on the button/SB in a heads-up pot, with pot 300 and to-call 100, the hand has negligible equity and playability post-flop. Even with fold equity on a steal, the EV of calling or raising is massively negative given the deep stacks (my 30k vs her 9k) and history showing Minni raises light but still beats trash like this. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots in BB defense.

Heads-up from the SB (button) with K7o, a playable hand with a strong king blocker and decent equity against BB defending range. Minni has defended wide in past hands and is aggressive but shorter-stacked at ~94BB while I have ~304BB. Raising takes initiative, builds the pot, and applies pressure preflop. Folding is too tight heads-up; limping is too passive. Standard raise size of 3x BB to 300 total.

Heads-up in the big blind vs a 3x SB open to 300. I have Q♠6♥ — marginal but acceptable to defend in heads-up. Pot is 400 and a call costs 200 more (total 300), giving me ~33% break-even equity; versus a wide SB opening range Q6o has reasonable equity and good showdown value. With deep stacks (~95 BB effective), I don’t want to inflate the pot out of position with a marginal hand by 3-betting; calling keeps the pot controlled and lets me realize equity and play post‑flop. Plan: call to see the flop, proceed aggressively if I hit a queen or good draw, otherwise fold to heavy aggression on dry boards.

I defended preflop with Q♠6♥ but the K♦-2♣-5♠ flop is very dry and misses me — I only have queen-high and a one-card backdoor spade. Out of position heads-up vs a wide opening range, the button c-bets a large portion of his range and many of those hands (Kx, 5x, 2x) dominate me. I’ll check to keep the pot small and avoid inflating the pot with a marginal hand. If Elon checks back I can see a free turn and re-evaluate (look for a Q or a real draw); if he bets a normal c-bet (≈1/3 pot or larger, ≳200) I will fold most of the time. I would only consider calling a very small probe (<~200) as a bluff-catcher against an extremely wide player.

Heads-up on the button with top pair top kicker? Wait, Kh pairs Kd for top pair, but 7c kicker is mediocre. Board is relatively dry: Kd-2c-5s has few strong draws (spade flush draw possible, gutshots like 34 or A34 unlikely). Preflop I raised K7o and got called by Minni's wide BB defending range, which includes Ax, pairs, suited connectors. She checked flop, which is weak and invites a c-bet. I should c-bet for value against her worse pairs/draws (e.g., worse Kx, 55/22/set mining value, backdoor draws) and protection against any live cards. Checking back risks giving free cards to overcards/draws and loses value from floats. Bet sizing: ~2/3 pot (400) balances value/thin value/protection while keeping pot manageable vs her 92BB stack. My deep stack (300BB+) allows aggression without risk.

Fold. I have Q♠6♥ on K♦-2♣-5♠ — only queen-high with no meaningful draws. The pot is 1000 and Elon bets 400; calling 400 would require ~28.6% equity (400 to win 1400) to break even. Against his likely continuation range here (lots of Kx, 5x, pocket pairs and other value hands) my actual equity is well below that. Raising would be a thin out‑of‑position bluff with poor fold equity and too much risk given the stack sizes. Preserve chips and wait for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button (SB) I should be opening very wide to seize initiative — 4♦8♠ is weak, but in position it’s playable and worth a standard heads-up open. A min-raise to 200 (2x BB) costs me only 150 more chips (small portion of my 9,200) and gives good fold equity against Elon’s wide defending range while keeping the pot manageable deep-stacks. If Elon 3-bets big I’ll fold this hand; if he calls I’ll use position to play postflop (c-bet appropriate flops, check back troublesome textures). Raise to 200 is the +EV, aggressive line here to build stack and avoid giving Elon a free flop.

Heads-up pre-flop in BB vs SB button raise to 200 (to call 100 into pot of 300, making 400). Hand Ad6h is ace-high offsuit, but strong enough to defend wide against a loose button range from Minni, who has been aggressive in prior hands (frequent raises and calls). Position advantage post-flop, deep stacks (my 30k vs her 9k), pot odds excellent (3:1). Calling is standard; folding too tight, 3-betting marginal with this hand. Plan to play post-flop aggressively if it hits or as a bluff catcher.

Heads-up flop OOP with Ad6h on 5d9dTh board. I have the nut diamond flush draw (Ad blocks her likely draws/pairs) plus ace-high. Pot odds great pre-flop call was correct. Minni's SB raise range is wide and aggressive per history (frequent limps/calls/raises with marginal hands). This board hits her range (pairs like 99/TT/55, draws like 67,78,89,JdQd, KdQd etc.) but I have equity dominance with nut FD. Betting semi-bluffs for fold equity from her worse draws/ace-highs, protection, and pot building if she calls with worse. Size 2/3 pot (300 into 400) denies equity, gets value from weaker pairs/draws, and keeps pot manageable with her 9k stack. Check would invite free cards or tough spots. Raising later possible if checked to. EV+ bet maximizes stack long-term.

Elon defended a wide range from the BB and his 300 into a 700 pot is a standard, medium-sized c‑bet. My 4♦8♠ has essentially no showdown value here but it does have a backdoor diamond and some slim runner‑runner straight outs. Calling 300 gives me ~30% pot odds to continue and keeps the pot manageable while I retain position — I can float and take a free look at the turn or barrel if he shows weakness. A raise would be an ambitious bluff with little fold equity vs a deep-stacked opponent and folding surrenders too much frequency in heads‑up play. I’ll call, see the turn, and re-evaluate: continue on a favourable card (diamond or blank that misses his range), fold to significant turn aggression when I have no improvement.

Turn board pairs the 10c, which is somewhat neutral but favors hands that improved like T-x or sets. I have the nut diamond flush draw (9 outs ~18% equity + fold equity), ace-high showdown value, and positionally disadvantaged OOP. Minni's flop call range includes pairs (99/TT/55), two pairs, weaker diamond draws (non-nut), straight draws (67/78/89), and floats. Her SB aggression history suggests she calls wide, but continued betting applies pressure on worse draws/pairs while denying equity and building pot for flush. Check risks free river cards to her draws or check-behind with medium strength. Bet sizing ~2/3 pot (700) balances value/protection/fold equity without overcommitting vs her 8750 stack; pot-committal on river if called/flushed. EV+ aggression maximizes stack growth.

I called the flop to use position and see a turn, but Elon's turn bet of 700 into a 1700 pot is clear turn aggression. My hand (4♦8♠) has no pair and no realistic draw now — the turn (Tc) kills the backdoor diamond possibility. I only have 6 river outs to make a single pair (6/46 ≈ 13% equity), while calling 700 would require ~29% equity to be profitable (700/(1700+700) ≈ 29%). A raise here would be an ambitious bluff with poor fold equity versus a deep-stacked, aggressive opponent and could cost a lot if re-raised. Given the math and Elon's line, folding and preserving my 8,750 stack is the correct play.

Heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB with K7s, a strong suited king-high hand with good playability. Minni in BB has defended wide in past hands. Standard play is to raise for value, steal blinds, and build pot in position. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is appropriate sizing given pot 150 and my deep stack vs her effective ~86BB. Folding or limping is too passive with this hand.

Heads-up vs Button open to 300. I hold Ac-3s — a decent heads-up hand but vulnerable (weak kicker). With ~86BB effective, I prefer to defend the big blind by calling rather than 3-betting out of position: a call costs 200 more (300 total) and gives good pot odds to see a flop and realize equity (chance at top pair with an Ace or backdoor draws). A min-raise to 500 is too small and a larger 3-bet would commit me OOP with a marginal hand. Plan: call, evaluate the flop — fold to heavy aggression if I miss, and extract value or apply pressure if I hit top pair or a favorable board.

Flop is 6c Js 3h and I hold Ac 3s — bottom pair (treys) with a useful Ace kicker. Heads-up the button’s open is wide: lots of unpaired broadways and some Jx/pocket pairs. My hand is ahead of most unpaired Ax/Kx/Qx combos and behind Jx and bigger pairs/sets, so I want to extract value while keeping the pot reasonable. I’ll make a small value/protective bet (~1/2 pot = 300) to charge Ace/K/Q highs and deny free cards. If Elon check‑raises big I’ll fold; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (continue only with improvement or a safe line).

Facing a small flop bet of 300 into a 900 pot (getting ~3:1 odds), my K♠7♠ has King-high overcards, backdoor spade flush draw (3 spades total), and good playability in position heads-up deep. The board (6♣ J♠ 3♥) is dry-ish but hits Minni's wide BB defending range (Jx, low pairs, overcards, weak draws like 45/78). She often leads weak or for protection/value from positionless BB. Folding is too tight after raising pre-flop; raising lacks value without a pair/draw. Calling realizes equity cheaply, controls pot size, and keeps her guessing on turn where I have position and fold equity if she overbets. Consistent with exploitative play vs her aggressive history (frequent small bets, calls stations).

I have bottom pair (3s) with a strong kicker (Ac) after betting the flop and getting called. The 2d on the turn doesn't improve my hand and still leaves me behind Jx and most pocket pairs that likely called the flop. Betting again OOP would bloat the pot with a marginal holding and expose me to a check‑raise or large action I don't want to face — my flop plan was to continue only with improvement or a safe line, and I haven't improved. With pot control preferred (pot = 1,200, my stack = 8,150), I will check and see the river; I can call reasonable river bets for value or fold to large aggression, and I preserve chips for better spots.

Pot is 1200, Minni checks turn after leading small flop into a dry board (6c Js 3h 2d now), which connects poorly with her wide BB defending range (Jx, low pairs, Ax, suited connectors). My Ks7s is a strong bluff-catcher/king-high with position, blocking top pair and overcards. History shows Minni aggressive but folds to continued pressure when missed (e.g., hands 12,60,86). Her flop lead was thin/protective; turn check likely weakness or medium strength without nuts. Half-pot bet (600) gets value from Jx/low pairs/worse high cards she might call light (stationy calls seen in 14,35,73), folds pure air/draws, and denies equity to gutshots (45/A5). Checking realizes equity but misses thin value and risks her improving/betting river. Deep vs her 8150 effective, but this sizing <10% stack risk while building pot profitably. Consistent with preflop aggression and flop call.

Preflop I defended and c-bet the flop with Ac3s to charge air and weaker Ax hands. I checked the turn to keep the pot under control with a marginal bottom pair/top kicker. Elon now bets 600 into a 1,200 pot (1800 total) — I’m getting 3:1 pot odds (need ~25% equity). Against his flop‑call/call‑down range (lots of Jx, some 6x, medium pairs, A‑highs and occasional floats/bluffs) my 3s with the Ace kicker has enough showdown equity to justify a call. Raising OOP with this hand is thin: I’ll often be called by better hands (Jx, pairs) and bloat the pot with a marginal holding, while folding concedes >25% equity for only a small investment. I’ll call 600 to see the river, preserve stack (8150 -> 7550 if called), and re‑evaluate: shove/raise only if I improve to two pair/trips or face a river check and can extract value; fold to large river aggression if I don’t improve.

I have bottom pair (3s) with a strong Ace kicker on a 6c Js 3h 2d Td board. Line review: I bet the flop and was called (range includes Jx, 6x, some pairs, floats); I checked the turn and called Elon’s 600 — he has shown willingness to value-bet the turn. The river checked through, pot is 2400 and there is no bet to face. With only a small pair I’m behind Jx/Tx and larger pairs and only ahead of missed A-highs and pure bluffs. Betting here would risk folding out worse hands (thin value) and can get raised by hands that currently beat me; checking preserves stack and takes the cheapest route to showdown. I’ll check and see showdown rather than bloat the pot with a marginal holding.

River Td completes some backdoor diamond draws and pairs tens, but doesn't drastically change the board texture (still no flush, open-ended straight draws unlikely). My line (PFR, flop call vs lead, turn c-bet after check, now river bet) reps strength like Jx, Tx, or better pairs/set mining from preflop range. Ks7s blocks strong Jx combos (KJ, KT) and is ahead of her bluff-catchers, float calls, and missed draws from wide BB defense. Minni's history shows stationy calls on rivers (e.g., #14, #35, #73, #103, #108) with medium strength, and she checked turn/river suggesting no nuts (would bet for value/protection). Pot 2400, her 7550 eff stack; half-pot bet 1200 gets thin value from weaker pairs/Tx/Jx without T/dominates her calling range, folds air. Checking risks her having showdown value or free cards (unlikely), and forgoes EV+ thin value in position late tournament (112/150, building stack). Balanced sizing avoids overbet jam risk.

I hold Ac-3s — bottom pair with a strong Ace kicker on 6c Js 3h 2d Td. Elon opened wide from the button, called my flop c-bet and then barreled turn and river; his line is weighted toward Jx/Tx and some medium pairs but also contains missed A-high floats and occasional bluffs. The pot is 3600 and he bet 1200 on the river, so I’m getting about 3:1 (need ~25% equity). My 3 with the Ace kicker has enough showdown equity versus his calling/barrel range to justify a call; raising OOP with a marginal pair is thin and folding concedes >25% equity for a small chip saving. I will call 1200 to see showdown and preserve stack for better spots if I’m beaten.

Heads-up on the button with Q5o is a hand I'd open rather than fold or just limp. The pot is 150 and it only costs me 50 to call, but raising to 200 (2BB) is the standard heads‑up play to take the initiative, generate fold equity, and take advantage of position postflop. Q5o does well in wide heads‑up raising ranges — if Elon folds I pick up the pot immediately; if called I keep position and can c‑bet or control the pot depending on the flop texture. If Elon 3‑bets I can fold without risking too much since stacks are deep and the raise is small relative to stacks. Raising maximizes my expected value here compared with passive calling. I'll raise to the minimum (200) to balance aggression and risk management.

Heads-up in BB facing a standard button raise to 200 (2BB). My hand 3s5c is offsuit trash with minimal playability, poor equity against Minni's wide button opening range (~40-50% hands), and I'll be out of position postflop. Pot odds are ~3:1 but not enough given low implied odds and reverse implied odds with such a weak hand. Folding preserves stack for better spots with 37 hands left.

Heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB with Qc9c, a suited queen-high connector with good playability and post-flop potential. Position post-flop is a huge advantage. Standard play is to raise for value, steal blinds, or build a pot with initiative. Minni defends wide based on history, but Q9s has good equity vs calling range. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is standard sizing to take control. Folding or limping is too passive; this hand plays well post-flop in position.

Elon (button/SB) opened to 300. From the BB I have T♥9♠ — a strong, playable heads‑up hand with straight and two‑overcard/paired potential. The pot is 400 and I need to add 200 more (calling to 300 total) — pot odds ≈ 3:1, so I need ~25% equity. Versus a wide HU open range T9o has sufficient equity and plays well postflop. I prefer to call rather than 3‑bet light: stacks are deep and I don’t want to inflate an OOP pot with a marginal hand. Plan: call to 300, see the flop, continue on favorable textures (connectors, backdoor draws, top pair) and fold or proceed cautiously to heavy aggression when I miss.

Heads‑up on a dry, paired flop (3s 3h 7d) with T♥9♠ — I have no pair, just two overcards and 6 outs to pair by river (~24% equity). Elon opens very wide preflop, so his flop range includes lots of bluffs and weak pairs but also the occasional 3/7 or set. Out of position with deep stacks, I want pot control and to realize my equity rather than inflate the pot with a marginal hand. Checking keeps weaker bluffs in and allows me to call a standard c‑bet; for reference, a half‑pot c‑bet (~300) gives ~4:1 pot odds (need ~20% equity), so I can call such bets and fold to heavy pressure that represents sets. Plan: check and reevaluate the turn — call small/standard c‑bets, fold to large polarized raises, and become aggressive only if I pair or see a favorable turn.

Preflop, we raised Qc9c from the button/SB, a strong playable hand with position and suited connectivity, and Minni defended wide from the BB as per her historical tendencies (frequent calls pre and postflop). Flop is 3s3h7d, an extremely dry, paired low board with minimal draws (rainbow except paired treys). This heavily favors our preflop raising range: overpairs (QQ+, 88-JJ likely ahead), Ax, KQ, broadway cards all crush villain's capping range (weak pairs, suited junk, low connectors). Q9c has queen-high blocker (blocks Qx value), suited backdoor flush/straight potential, and decent equity (~40-50% vs her check range). She checks (standard weak), so we c-bet for thin value against weaker Qx/Jx/87/T8-type hands she floats with, protection against any live Ax turning gutshots, and to deny equity/realize fold equity. Her stack is ~18bb effective (10950 / 600 pot), so sizing 2/3 pot (400) applies pressure without overbetting, maintaining fold equity while getting called by worse. Checking relinquishes initiative on a board we dominate; betting maximizes EV by charging stations/calling stations like Minni (evident in prior hands where she calls light but folds to continued aggression). Long-term tournament goal: exploit position and range advantage to grow stack.

Call 400. I have T♥9♠ on 3s 3h 7d — no pair but 6 clean outs (3 tens + 3 nines), ~24–25% to hit by the river. The break‑even equity vs this bet is 400/(1000+400) ≈ 28.6%, so pure raw equity is slightly below the mathematical call threshold. However Elon opened wide from the button and is c‑betting a relatively small size into a 1,000 pot, so his range contains a lot of bluffs/weak overcards that I can beat at showdown or force off later streets. Calling keeps the pot controllable OOP, realizes my equity, and lets me reevaluate on the turn: if I pair or pick up additional equity I can apply pressure; if I miss and he fires big (polarized sizing) I’ll fold. I don’t want to inflate the pot by check‑raising or raising OOP with a marginal hand and deep stacks, so a straightforward call is the best +EV line here.

Check. On the turn I have T♥9♠ on 3♠‑3♥‑7♦‑5♥: no pair and only 6 outs to a pair on the river (≈6/46 ≈ 13%), so my raw equity with one card to come is very low. I called the flop to realize equity and keep the pot manageable; out of position I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal holding or risk facing a large raise. Checking controls the pot, keeps bluffs in Elon’s range, and lets me see the river for free or face a bet I can fold to cheaply. Plan: check and reevaluate the river — call small river bets when odds justify it or when I pick up a pair, fold to large pressure, and only bluff/raise if I improve or get a very favorable read.

Turn board brings the 5h, completing some wheel straight draws (A46, 46x) and heart flush draws, while also providing gutshots to 46/68. The board remains relatively dry but more drawy now (two hearts). Preflop as raiser with Qc9c (suited queen-high connector, position), flop c-bet thin value on paired dry board got called by Minni, whose history shows she's a calling station (frequent light calls pre/postflop, e.g., Hands 14,35,73,78,99) but folds to sustained aggression at times. My range remains ahead: overpairs (QQ+,88+), Ax/Kx broadway dominate her capping/defending range (7x, weak Qx/Jx, Ax floats, low pairs/draws). Qc9c blocks Qx, has backdoor club flush/straight potential, and queen-high has showdown value vs bluffs/missed draws. Checking relinquishes initiative, allows her to realize equity cheaply or bluff river; betting charges draws (hearts ~20% equity to flush), worse (Jx/87/Tx), and extracts value while denying equity. Pot 1400 (~14bb), her ~105bb effective (but tournament pressure late), SPR ~7.5: sizing ~2/3 pot (900) builds pot, maintains pressure without committing off, gets value from her station tendencies, fold equity vs air/marginal. Maximizes EV in position vs exploitable opponent; checking too passive given range advantage and history.

I have T♥9♠ on 3♠-3♥-7♦-5♥: no pair and only 6 clean outs (three Tens + three Nines). With one card to come my raw equity to improve to a pair is ~6/46 ≈ 13%. Elon bet 900 into a pot that became 2,300 (pot after his bet), so a call of 900 requires ~900/(2,300+900) ≈ 28.1% equity to break even. My ~13% is far below the price, so a plain call is -EV. 

Range/read: Elon opened wide preflop, but he continuation-bet the paired flop and now barrels the turn with a sizable bet after being called — a line consistent with value (7x/77/sets) and only a limited bluff frequency. Raising as a bluff OOP with only two overcards is risky: it risks a large portion of my deep stack for fold equity that I’m unlikely to get enough of against his range, and I have poor showdown equity if called. 

Tournament/stack considerations: I’m deep (≈105 BB) and can wait for a better spot; preserving stack and folding marginal holdings here is the prudent +EV play. 

Decision: fold.

Heads-up on the button with J5o. This is a hand that belongs in my open-raise range in heads-up: it has reasonable showdown value and plays well in position. I want initiative and fold equity rather than just completing for 50, and a 3x BB-sized open is standard and keeps the pot manageable against a much deeper opponent. I will raise to 300 total (committing 300 including my SB) — if Elon 3-bets big I can fold this marginal hand, if he calls I can use my positional advantage to c-bet appropriate flops and pressure weaker holdings.

Td2d is a speculative suited hand but very weak with low cards and poor playability out of position versus a button raise. Minni's range is wide but still crushes this hand on average. Pot odds are about 3:1 (calling 200 into 400+200=600), requiring ~25% equity, but T2s realizes far less against her likely range due to domination and reverse implied odds. Folding preserves my large stack advantage (29k vs her 10k) for better spots in this late stage (hand 115/150).

Pocket Jacks is an extremely strong hand heads-up, especially from the button with position. Standard play is to raise for value to build the pot and take control. Minni's history shows she calls raises frequently but JJ crushes most of her calling range. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is a standard size, balancing value and fold equity while maintaining pot control postflop.

Heads-up vs the button who opened to 300. I’m the big blind with 5♥7♥ and stacks are deep (~105bb). 5♥7♥ is a playable, suited connector with good multi-street equity and implied-odds potential against a wide open range. However I’ll be out of position postflop (button acts last), so making a thin 3-bet or a tiny min-raise is suboptimal — a larger 3-bet would commit me OOP with a marginal hand. Pot odds are favorable: I need ~33% equity to call (200 to win the 400 pot). Plan is to call and see a flop; if I hit a pair/flush/straight draw I can continue aggressively, otherwise I’ll proceed cautiously and fold to heavy pressure. Call (total commitment = 300).

Heads-up I'm out of position with 5♥7♥ on Q♦ 8♦ 3♥. I have no pair and only a very flimsy backdoor heart draw (needs two running hearts) plus weak pair/floating potential. Elon opened wide from the button so he'll c-bet frequently, but calling OOP with nothing is negative EV in deep stacks unless I have a real draw or plan to float small bets — I don't. Leading or bluffing here is unnecessary and riskier out of position. Plan: check and fold to any meaningful contended bet; if he checks back I get a free turn to pick up a pair or a real draw and can reassess.

Pocket Jacks is an overpair on a Q-8-3 rainbow-ish board with a diamond flush draw. This is a strong hand that beats most of Minni's checking range (Ax, lower pairs, bluffs, draws). She checked, indicating weakness. C-bet for value against worse hands like 99-TT, AJ, KQ sometimes, and to protect against diamond draws, straight draws (e.g., J10, 97). Pot is 600, betting 2/3 pot (400) denies equity to draws and builds the pot while keeping fold equity against her occasional strong hands. Her history shows she calls flops often but folds to continued aggression if missed draws. Deep stack allows flexibility, but value/protection now is standard heads-up.

I checked the flop OOP with 5♥7♥ on 3♥ Q♦ 8♦ and Elon c-bet 400 into a 1000 pot. I have no pair and only a backdoor heart (needs runner‑runner) plus weak pair/straight futures; my realistic winning equity vs a typical button c-bet range is well under the break‑even threshold. Calling 400 would require roughly 400/(1000+400+400) ≈ 22% equity to be profitable; while I have ~24% to make a pair by the river, many of those pairs will still be behind and my effective equity is lower. Raising OOP with this hand is too risky. Fold and conserve chips for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button with A♠3♣ — a strong, playable ace in position. I’ve already posted the 50 SB; raising to 300 (≈3x BB) takes the initiative, creates fold equity against Elon’s wide BB defending range, and builds a pot I can play postflop with positional advantage. Table dynamics show Elon calls and opens wide often, so this sizing is consistent and will either win the pot preflop or let me play a capped range in position. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to a polarizing shove/very large 3‑bet; call smaller 3‑bets to leverage position).

Preflop in BB facing a standard 3x open from BTN/SB by Minni, who is aggressive and opens wide based on history (frequent raises with marginal hands). K7o is a decent speculative hand heads-up with deep stacks (295 BB effective vs opponent's 100 BB). Equity vs typical BTN 40-50% range is around 42-45%, and postflop playability is okay with king high, suited? No offsuit but still defend wide OOP in BB. Pot odds 3:1 (200 to call into 400), closing equity achieved. Folding too tight; 3-betting polarized range better with stronger hands. Calling to realize equity, control pot OOP, and exploit Minni's tendency to overplay postflop or c-bet wide.

OOP on a drawy flop (5h Js 6c) with K7o: king-high no pair, no straight draw, backdoor heart flush draw at best. Board hits villain's wide button range with Jx, 65s-type straights, draws (34,47,78). Leading out (donk) with air is -EV and invites raises. Check to control pot, induce bluffs/c-bets from aggressive Minni (who cbets wide per history), realize showdown value if checked behind rarely, or fold equity post-turn. Preflop call was for multiway potential but heads-up now; folding pre was too tight, but postflop discipline key to avoid spewing chips long-term in 150-hand tourney.

Heads-up in position with A♠3♣ on J♠-5♥-6♣: I have the best Ace-blocker in my range and a hand that fares well as a continuation-bet candidate. The flop is fairly dry (no real flush draws, only backdoor straight possibilities), and Elon’s check after calling preflop signals weakness or pot-control. Betting about half the pot applies pressure, folds out many hands that currently beat Ace-high (small pairs and unpaired broadways without equity), and builds the pot when I improve. I size to 350 into the 600 pot — large enough to have real fold equity but small enough to avoid bloating the pot against a big stack; if Elon calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (continue with A / blank turns, proceed cautiously to check-fold to a big check-raise).

Heads-up against aggressive Minni on button who opens wide and c-bets frequently based on extensive history. Preflop call with K7o was standard in BB vs BTN. Flop J-6-5 rainbow hits her range (Jx, pairs, draws) but she cbets small (37% pot), consistent with wide c-betting. King-high blocks strong Jx (KJ, KT, etc.), equity vs polarized c-bet range ~38-42% (blockers help). Excellent pot odds (350 into 950, ~2.7:1, need 27%). OOP but multi-street potential with deep effective stacks (her 97BB); calling realizes equity, controls pot size, sets up cheap folds to her likely continuation if missed, or value if ahead vs air. Raising bluff risky OOP vs caller who fights back; folding too tight preserves no equity loss on marginal spot. Call to exploit her aggression long-term in late tournament stages.

Heads-up on turn OOP with Ks7h (king-high, offsuit, no pair, no live draws; backdoor heart flush possible but irrelevant). Board 5h-Js-6c-Qd is coordinated, improving straight draws (e.g., 78, 47, 34 now gutshots or better? Actually pairs board texture: adds Qx pairs, broadways like KQ, AQ, QTs, JQ). Minni's button range wide (per 100+ hands: frequent opens, small cbets ~35-40% pot, calls raises with medium strength), polarized post-flop: value (Jx, sets, two-pair), bluffs/draws (A-high, gutshots). Flop call standard with 35% equity + pot odds vs c-bet; turn Qd bad (-10-15% equity drop, now ~25-30% vs range as blocks KJ/KQ but crushed by Jx/Qx). Pot 1300, SPR ~7.5 effective (her 9700), but OOP no fold equity for bet/raise bluff (she defends well per history). Betting risks raising into monsters or getting called wide; small bet induces floats. Check preserves stack: pot control, realize thin SDV vs air (she checks weak/missed draws ~25-30% time), fold to bet (positive EV as invested little relative to stack in late tourney). Avoid spew; folding pre/flop tighter better long-term, but realize post-flop discipline key to grind edges over 150 hands.

I’m heads‑up in position with A♠3♣. I raised preflop and c‑bet the flop; Elon called. Turn Q♦ checked to me — that line shows he’s not super strong and gives me an opportunity to continue representing a made hand. With the Ace blocker I reduce AQ combos and my hand has reasonable blocking value to bluff-turn. Betting about half pot (650 into 1300) continues my story, folds out many Jx/small pair hands that called the flop, and doesn’t overcommit my ~9.7k stack if called. If Elon check‑raises large I’ll concede the hand; if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate on the river and only barrel again with a real improvement or a very favorable blank. Bet 650.

Heads-up vs aggressive button Minni (wide opens, frequent small c-bets per 100+ hand history). Preflop call with K7o standard BB defense vs BTN raise. Flop call reasonable with king-high blockers (KJ/KT), ~35-40% equity vs polarized c-bet range, great pot odds. Turn Qd worsens board significantly: adds Qx (KQ, AQ, QJ, QTs), broadway combos, pairs strong hands; my equity drops to ~20-25% vs her continuing range (Jx dominant, overpairs, two-pair+, missed draws less likely after second bet). Pot odds ~3:1 (650 into 2600, need 20%) marginal, but OOP on river with SPR ~6.5 (her 9050 eff), no fold equity, tough to realize equity vs her value-heavy turn betting range (smaller flop bet continuation suggests not pure air). Raising bluff suicidal OOP vs defender who calls stations (history shows calling down light). Late tourney (hand 117/150), preserve deep stack (290BB) for better spots; folding air post-two streets realizes long-term EV by avoiding spew. Discipline over hope.

Heads-up on the button (SB position), I hold A♥ 3♠, which is an ace-high hand playable in a wide range preflop. With position postflop and a dominant high card against a typical BB defending range, raising is standard to build the pot, deny equity, and take initiative. Minni defends frequently based on history but folds to pressure often. Standard open size of 3x BB (300 total) balances value and fold equity without overcommitting stacks (effective ~10k). Limping risks giving her a free raise; folding is too tight.

Heads-up the button’s open to 300 represents a very wide range. 5♠7♣ is a marginal but defendable hand in the big blind — calling is cheap (200 more) and keeps the pot manageable while preserving my deep-stack postflop options. I’ll call 200 more (300 total committed) and play cautiously out of position: fold to large continuation-bets unless I pair or pick up strong equity, but continue aggressively when I connect. I avoid a 3-bet here because this hand performs poorly vs a 3-bet and there’s no need to bloat the pot out of position with a marginal holding.

Check. My 5♠7♣ completely missed the A‑8‑2 flop (no pair, only very weak backdoor outs), and Elon’s button open is a very wide range that will c‑bet frequently. Checking keeps the pot small and preserves stack flexibility out of position — I’ll call a small continuation bet to realize equity but fold to significant pressure. Concretely: if Elon fires a small c‑bet (~≤200, ~1/3 pot) I’ll call and evaluate the turn; if he bets medium or large (≈300–400+ or ~1/2–2/3 pot and up) I’ll fold given I’m almost always behind to his value range. No check‑raise or bluff‑raising here — pot control and survival with a weak holding is the priority.

Preflop raiser with position on a checked flop. Hold top pair (Ah pairs Ad) with a weak 3s kicker on a dry Ad-8c-2s board. Board is ace-high with no strong draws (one spade, no straight draws obvious). Minni's BB calling range includes weaker Ax (A2-A7, suited aces), pocket pairs below 88, and some suited connectors. As preflop aggressor, continuation bet for thin value against worse Ax pairs, protection against overcards or draws, and to deny equity to floats. She checks, suggesting weakness or trapping, but history shows she defends wide pre but folds to postflop aggression often. Half-pot sizing (400 into 600) balances value from worse hands and fold equity without overbetting with kicker vulnerability. Checking risks giving free cards to draws/improvers and loses value. Effective stacks deep enough (her 10.7k), but pot-building starts here.

I defended preflop as planned, but the A‑8‑2 flop misses my 5♠7♣. Elon’s c‑bet of 400 into a 1000 pot is a medium/strong sizing; calling 400 to win 1400 requires ~28.6% equity. I have no pair and essentially no realistic backdoor — a 5‑card flush is impossible with only one spade in my hand and two cards to come, and any straight would require runner‑runner. My actual equity is far below the required threshold and Elon’s button range includes lots of Ax/8x/top pairs/value hands that justify this sizing. I’ll fold to preserve my stack and wait for a better heads‑up spot.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the SB/Button) I should be opening a wide range. 6♠9♦ is a playable hand in position — it has enough postflop playability to raise rather than limp. I’ll make a standard 2xBB open to 200 total to seize initiative, pressure the big blind, and keep the pot size manageable. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll fold this marginal holding; if he calls I’ll use position to c‑bet favorable flops or pot‑control on dry boards. This line maximizes fold equity while preserving my stack vs big 3‑bets.

Heads-up preflop in the big blind facing a button minraise to 200. My hand Qh2s is offsuit queen-high with a terrible kicker and poor connectivity. Even heads-up where button ranges are wide (~50% of hands), Q2o has mediocre equity (~35-40%) against that range but terrible postflop playability out of position. Pot odds are good (3:1), but calling commits us to a bloated pot OOP with a hand that rarely improves to top pair or better. 3-betting as a bluff is possible given Minni's history of folding to aggression in some spots, but Q2o blocks few strong hands in her calling range and performs poorly if called. Raising would be to ~600-900, but with my large stack advantage (29k vs her 10.5k), better to preserve chips for premium spots or dominant hands later in the tournament (hand 119/150). Folding is standard and disciplined here to avoid tough postflop situations and maximize EV long-term.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with deep effective stacks (Minni at 107 BB, me covered). T6o is a playable hand from this spot: decent high card, straight potential, live cards, and excellent postflop position. Standard to raise wide here vs BB. Minni plays a bit loose/passive from history (frequent calls, some limps), so good fold equity preflop and initiative postflop. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is a balanced size: not minraise (too small/weak), not too big OOP penalty. Builds pot when ahead, steals blinds frequently in late tournament.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with Q♦7♣ — a marginal but defendable heads‑up hand. It’s only 200 more to call (300 total) and Elon’s shown a wide opening frequency, so I’ll defend to realize equity rather than 3‑bet out of position with a weak holding. Calling keeps the pot controllable and lets me evaluate the flop: I’ll play aggressively if I hit (top pair/strong draws) and fold or proceed cautiously OOP on heavily‑favorable boards for him. Call 300 (100 already in, +200 to match).

I have top pair (Q♦ with Q♣ on board) but a weak 7 kicker. Heads‑up vs a wide open button, many worse hands (Tx, lower pairs, Jx, some Kx) will call a value bet and the flop is relatively dry aside from backdoor diamonds. I want to charge those worse hands and deny free cards to any overcards/backdoor draws, so I’ll bet for value and protection. A ~2/3 pot c‑bet (~400 into 600) extracts value, keeps range advantage, and still allows me to fold to a large check‑raise if Elon shows strength. If called I’ll reassess on the turn with pot control and kicker considerations.

Top pair with Ten (Tc) on Td4dQc board, weak 6 kicker, but position postflop vs Minni's likely wide calling range from BB. Board has diamond flush draw and straight draws (J9, 53), so her 400 bet into 600 (now pot 1000) could be wide: Qx, Tx, draws, smaller pairs, bluffs. Folding top pair is too tight; raising risks getting coolered by Qx/Ax/JT or floating with overcards. Calling 400 (13% pot) is low risk, controls pot size, realizes equity, denies draws cheaply, and keeps position for turn aggression if check-fold or thin value spots. Her stack 10100 (~50BB effective? Wait 101BB but pot building), deep enough for multi-street. History shows Minni passive/calling station, pays off value often (e.g., hands 14,35,46,73). Call to pot control and extract later.

The turn A♦ is a scare card — it both overtakes my Q and completes a lot of Elon's turn-range (Ax, and many diamond combos). While I still have top pair (Q♦) plus a 4‑card diamond (9 outs to a flush), my kicker is weak and the board texture (T♦ 4♦ Q♣ A♦) favors callers/raisors from Elon. I opened the flop for value and protection and he called, so his range includes Ax, Tx, Qx with better kickers, and diamond draws that now made a flush. I prefer pot control here: checking avoids bloating the pot with a vulnerable top‑pair hand, keeps bluffs in his range, and preserves my equity to realize on the river. Plan: check — call a modest bet (small sizing) to see a river with my draw + showdown value, but fold to a large turn raise indicating strong made hands (A or a made flush).

Heads-up on button vs Minni in BB. Holding top pair (Tc pairing Td) with weak 6 kicker on a board of Td4dQcAd. Pot 1400, effective stack ~101 BB for her. FLOP: Called her 400 bet into 600 pot for pot control with position and her calling station tendencies (frequent calls postflop in history, e.g., hands 14,35,46,66,73). TURN Ad: Scary card completing diamond flush draws, pairing no board cards but improving Ax/DD. She checks after leading flop, which could be weakness (missed draw, Qx weak kicker, smaller pairs, air wanting showdown) or trap (flush/set). Her range for flopping leading bet includes Qx, Tx, gutshots (J9s,53s), diamond FD (Axdd, Kdd, etc.), pairs. Ad hits some FDs but bricks others; check could polarize to weak value or bluffs. Checking pot controls and gets showdown vs worse, but risks her improving river or free cards to draws/gutshots (e.g., 3,Kd,J). Thin value bet exploits her passive calling station style: she calls down light (multiple hands shown). Bet 800 (~57% pot) sizes for value from weaker Tx/Qx/Ax no FD, protection vs draws (Jd,3x, Kd), fold equity vs air. Position allows check-back if raised. Late tournament (120/150), build pot while ahead in equity vs her wide range. SPR post-bet ~8:1 viable for river play.

Call 800. Elon’s turn bet is small relative to the pot, so folding a hand with both showdown value and a big draw is too tight. I have top pair (Q♦) with a weak kicker, but I also have a 4‑card diamond draw (9 outs) and still beat many of his thin Tx and bluffs that called the flop. Pot odds: 800 to win ~3000 (~26.7% break‑even) — my combined showdown + draw equity versus his wide calling/betting range comfortably justifies a call. Raising OOP would be thin and commit too many chips against likely Ax or made diamond hands; plan is to call, see the river, and fold to a large river overbet if nothing improves, or extract value if the diamond completes or he checks.

The river 6♦ completes my diamond — with Q♦ in hand I now have a five‑card diamond flush (A♦T♦Q♦6♦4♦). This is a very strong heads‑up hand but not the absolute nut: the only realistic hands that beat me are K♦ combos. Elon’s line (button open pre, call on the flop, modest bet on the turn) is consistent with a wide range containing Tx, Qx, some A‑x, and some diamond draws; K♦ combos are limited. Betting for value is correct here — checking surrenders the initiative and lets him realize equity for free or allows him to bluff missed hands cheaply. I want a size that extracts calls from worse pairs, Tx, lower diamond holdings and some bluffs without folding them out. I’ll bet roughly half the pot (1500 into 3000). If he raises a reasonable amount I’ll call most reasonable raises with my flush; only an extreme polarized overbet that strongly represents K♦ would make me seriously consider folding.

River 6d on Td4dQcAd6d board pairs my 6, improving to two pair (tens and sixes) with Tc6s. Pot 3000 before her 1500 bet (now 4500), offering ~3:1 odds (25% equity needed to call). Her range for flopping a lead (Tx, Qx, diamond draws, pairs/gutshots), checking turn Ad (weak hands or traps), calling my 800 bet, then leading river: polarized to completed flushes (any diamond), thin value (Qx, Ax weak), or bluffs (missed draws betting for fold equity/thin value). Two pair crushes non-flush value (beats Tx no 6, Qx no diamond, unpaired Ax), has showdown vs bluffs. History shows Minni calls light but also bets rivers (e.g., hands 14,46,73,101), suggesting not ultra-tight value-only. No raise (vulnerable to flush shoves, pot-committing her 7800 stack). Fold too tight given odds and strength vs wide range (~40-50% equity est.). Call realizes equity, exploits passive tendencies late HU (120/150 hands, maximize stack by winning medium pots). Effective stacks allow (her 7800 covers, me deep).

A♠9♠ heads-up on the button is a strong, very playable hand: I have the nut Ace blocker, suitedness, and positional advantage. The pot is 150 and it only costs me 50 to call — folding is too passive. I'll raise to 300 total (about 3× the BB / a standard open size) to take the initiative, apply pressure to Elon's wide BB defending range, and build a pot with a hand that plays well post‑flop. The size is small relative to my 13,750 stack (low risk) but large enough to earn folds and get value from worse A‑x and broadway hands. Plan: continuation bet most A‑high or dry boards, control the pot on coordinated/wet boards, call a moderate sized 3‑bet to play post‑flop because of position and the suited Ace, but fold to extremely large 3‑bets/4‑bets unless odds and reads justify continuing.

Heads-up pre-flop in BB vs BTN/SB raise to 300 (200 to call into 400 pot, ~3:1 direct odds). My 5d9d is suited connectors with flush and straight potential, solid defending hand against a wide button opening range. Minni has been aggressive with frequent pre-flop raises from history, often with marginal holdings, suggesting a polarized but wide range including many suited/gapped hands I dominate or play well against post-flop. Deep stacks (261bb/135bb) favor seeing flops with playable hands rather than folding OOP. Calling preserves stack, realizes equity, and allows post-flop flexibility. 3-bet not ideal as 59dd isn't strong enough for value and bluff sizing OOP is tricky; fold too tight heads-up (~40-50% defend range standard). Pot odds good with implied odds on flush/straight draws.

Heads-up on flop, OOP in BB after calling button raise with 5d9d suited connectors. Board 8s-Td-Ts is paired tens with spade flush draw possible. My hand has zero equity: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot to 67 no, 97 for straight but no), backdoor diamond flush, backdoor spade flush irrelevant. Pure air with minimal showdown value. Minni's button range wide but flop hits strong hands like TT, T8s, 88, overpairs AJ+, flush draws (hearts irrelevant, spades like AQs JTs etc.). Standard OOP: check-fold to any reasonable bet, as pot odds poor without draws. Bluff bet risky OOP on paired board where caller range strong; pot 600, sizing would need to be small but still gets called by value. Check preserves stack, realizes any fold equity if she checks (possible with missed draws/overpairs slowplaying unlikely), free turn card rare but optimal. Deep effective stacks (her 135bb) but no reason to continue w/o equity. Prior history shows Minni aggressive but we fold trash post-flop.

I was the preflop raiser and Elon checked to me on a T♦ T♠ 8♠ board. I hold A♠9♠ — Ace-blocker plus the nut-flush draw and an overcard to tens — so I have both decent equity and good turn/river playability. As the preflop aggressor this is a textbook spot to continue for fold equity and to charge weaker made hands and draws. I’ll size about 2/3 pot (400 into 600): large enough to fold out many unpaired hands and to get paid by worse A‑x or single spade/straight draws, but not overcommit when I currently only have Ace‑high. Plan: if he folds I take it; if he calls I barrel spades/A turns for value and re-evaluate against check‑raises (fold to strong check‑raise without improvement, value-bet big when I complete the flush or pair the Ace).

On the flop of 8s-Td-Ts, heads-up OOP after defending BB with 5d9d suited connectors vs button raise. Hand has zero showdown value or draws: no pair, no straight draw (9-high misses everything relevant), backdoor diamond flush draw at best but irrelevant.
