Her 400 bet into ~600 pot (now pot 1000, 400 to call) is standard continuation sizing that gets called by her value and gets folds from air. Calling commits ~40% pot with no equity, poor implied odds deep but no hand to realize them; risks larger turn bets. Bluff-raising OOP on paired flush-draw board is disastrous as her range is strong and she'll continue with value. History shows Minni bets flops aggressively but folds to resistance often; however, folding preserves deep stack (259bb) for better spots. Standard check-fold equity realization complete pre-flop with playable but missed hand. Fold.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with J♥3♣ offsuit, a very weak hand with poor playability, low equity against any reasonable BB defending range, and dominated often by better jacks. Preflop, with position postflop, strong button ranges raise ~45-55% of hands, but J3o falls outside optimal open-raise range per solver standards due to the large gap, offsuit nature, and weak high card. Limping risks giving a strong BB hand free playability or facing raises without initiative. Folding forfeits the SB but avoids difficult postflop spots OOP for BB postflop wait no, position postflop as button. Still, EV positive to fold trash like this to preserve stack for better spots in tournament phase. With deep effective stacks (140BB), no need to gamble with marginal equity hands. Opponent history shows Minni plays aggressively, but discipline key to maximizing stack long-term.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the SB) with Q♠8♠ and deep stacks (~141 BB). In heads-up this is a clear opening hand — suited queen with decent playability and backdoor flush/straight possibilities. Calling 50 is cheap, but I want to seize the initiative, deny Elon the free flop and build a pot in position. A standard 3x-ish open to 300 (total) is size I’ve seen at this table and gives good fold equity while still leaving plenty of room to play postflop if called. If Elon calls I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and probe; if he 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and his tendencies (likely folding to big 4-bets, calling small squeezes sometimes). Action: open-raise to 300 total.

9h4h is a suited hand with some straight potential and flush draw possibilities. Facing a button min-raise from Minni, who has been aggressive in raises from late position throughout the history. In the BB, getting excellent pot odds (200 to call into 400 pot, better than 3:1). Defending wide OOP vs BTN raise is standard with deep stacks (my 25k, opp 13k). Calling allows realizing equity post-flop cheaply, where we can hit draws and have fold equity on bluffs or strong hands. Folding is too tight; 3-betting is possible as a bluff but this hand prefers set-mining/speculative play rather than polarized 3-bet range. Plan to play aggressively if we hit.

Preflop call with 9h4h was correct as a suited speculative hand OOP against button minraise, getting great odds. Flop K♦ Q♣ J♦ is a highly coordinated, top-heavy board with broadway straight possible (A-T), hitting villain's BTN raising range strongly (KQ, KJ, QJ, AK, AQ sets etc.). My hand has zero equity: no pair, no straight draw (94 doesn't connect), no flush draw (rainbow board). Minimal backdoors not enough. Acting first OOP with air on scary board, standard check-fold to villain's likely c-bet (aggressive history shows frequent postflop aggression). Donk betting is -EV: folds out better equity hands, gets called/raised by value. Pot 600, villain eff 13.8k, no need to bluff now; save bluffs for better spots. Check to realize 0 equity, induce fold later streets if checked through (unlikely). Maximizing EV over tournament.

Flop K♦ Q♣ J♦ — I have Q♠8♠: top pair but a very weak kicker on a highly connected, two-diamond board. Elon checked after calling pre, so his range is wide (Ax, Kx, Jx, suited broadways, connectors, pairs). This is a classic heads‑up continuation-bet spot: I have the initiative, many worse hands (weaker Qx, Jx, single K, various Ax, and some pair/draw combos) will either fold or pay to continue, and a bet charges straight/diamond draws. I’ll size for value and protection but not overcommit with a marginal kicker — bet ~2/3 pot to get value from worse pairs and charge draws. Plan: if he folds I take the pot; if he calls I use position to control the turn; if he check‑raises large I’ll give credit and fold (small raises I’ll call and re-evaluate).

Facing a 400 bet into 1000 pot on a K-Q-J rainbow flop with 9h4h, which has zero equity: no pair, no straight draw (94 doesn't connect to broadway cards), no flush draw (board rainbow, hearts irrelevant), and only weak backdoors. Preflop call was speculative OOP getting good odds vs button minraise, but flop misses completely. Minni's button range hits this board hard (KQ, KJ, QJ, AK, AQ, sets, etc.) and she continuation bets frequently per history (aggressive postflop player). OOP with air on coordinated, top-heavy board, check-fold is standard to avoid realizing 0 equity and prevent difficult turn/river spots. Pot odds ~3.5:1 insufficient for bluff-catch without equity or fold equity. Save chips for better spots; villain covers my needs effectively at 13k. Folding maximizes EV.

Preflop from SB with Ac6s against Minni in BB. Suited ace with good playability, deep stacks (255BB effective). Standard play is to raise for initiative, isolate, and take advantage of BB's wide defending range. Minni has shown she calls frequently with marginal hands in history. Raise to 3x BB (300 total) to build pot while keeping it reasonable. Avoid limping to prevent multiway pots or giving her position for free. Folding too weak, calling/limping too passive.

Heads-up in the big blind vs a standard open to 300 from the button: T♠7♥ is a marginal but perfectly defendable hand. Elon opens a lot and his BTN range is wide, so I get good pot odds to call — the pot is 400 and I only need to commit 200 more (total 300), so required equity is ~33%. With deep-ish stacks I prefer to see a flop with this hand rather than 3‑bet light out of position. Plan: call to 300, evaluate the flop — continue with a pair or a strong draw, fold to heavy sustained pressure when I miss. This keeps the pot controlled and preserves stack depth for later spots.

I defended preflop with T♠7♥ to see a flop — this board (4♥ 3♥ 8♦) gives me no pair and only a weak runner-runner/backdoor heart possibility. Heads-up vs a wide button range this is a hand I don’t want to build a pot with out of position. I’ll check to control the pot, realize my (small) equity for free, and re-evaluate on the turn. If Elon fires a small c-bet (≈≤½ pot) I’ll consider calling to see the turn; I’ll fold to heavy sustained pressure since I have very little showdown value.

Preflop raiser with Ac6s on a 8-4-3 board with two hearts. Board is relatively dry with possible flush draws and limited straight draws (e.g., 65, 52), but Ac blocks strong Ax and 6s blocks some gutshot straights like 75 or 52. No direct draws in my hand, but ace-high has showdown value and good playability. Minni checked, consistent with her history of frequent checking. Initiative favors a continuation bet to fold out her weaker calling range (e.g., dominated Ax, low pairs, bluffs). Betting 50% pot (300) denies equity to draws and takes down the pot often while keeping pot manageable deepstacked (effective ~140BB). Check-fold too weak, check-call invites pressure.

Call 300. I checked the flop to control the pot and Elon’s c‑bet is small (~1/3 pot), giving me roughly 25% pot odds to see the turn. T♠7♥ has no made hand on 4♥3♥8♦ and only remote runner‑runner/backdoor outs, but facing a wide BTN range and a small bet this is a reasonable, speculative call to realize equity and keep the pot manageable. Stack sizes are deep enough to avoid committing further without improvement. Plan: fold to heavy turn aggression if I don’t improve; if I pair (T or 7) or pick up a real draw on the turn I’ll proceed (check/call or raise for value), otherwise concede the pot to strong pressure.

I defended preflop and called Elon’s small flop c-bet to see the turn, but the Q♣ on the turn doesn’t help — I have no pair and no real draw. With 7♥T♠ I lost the backdoor (I would have needed both turn and river hearts), so my showdown equity is effectively zero versus a reasonable BTN continuing range. Out of position heads‑up I want to keep the pot small and avoid getting barreled off my stack with air. Since Elon checked to me on the turn, I’ll check back to control the pot and see the river cheaply. Plan: fold to any meaningful bet on the river unless I pick up a pair or the bet gives very generous pot odds; consider a small bluff only if the river card and opponent’s action make it credible.

Turn board brings the Qc, pairing the board slightly but not dramatically changing the texture. Flop was 4h-3h-8d (two hearts, low pairs possible, flush/straight draws). Minni called a half-pot c-bet on flop after checking, indicating some strength like a pair (8x, 4x, 3x), heart draw, or Ax. My Ac6s is ace-high with no pair or draw, but Ac blocks strong Ax hands. Pot is 1200, effective stacks ~138BB deep. Minni checks again, consistent with her history of passive play and frequent calling but not aggressive. Betting risks folding out worse (e.g., weaker Ax, draws) while getting called by better (pairs, Qx). Value betting thin with ace-high suboptimal against her sticky range. Bluffing possible but pot control better; realize equity at showdown where ace-high often good vs her calling range. Check preserves stack, avoids bloating pot OOP post-river. Folding irrelevant since checked to. Plan to check river unless strong improvement.

Board: 4♥ 3♥ 8♦ Q♣ 9♥ — my hand Ten♠ / Seven♥. I missed: no pair and only T-high with one heart (board has three hearts). Elon's line (wide BTN open, small flop c-bet, check on turn) is consistent with a lot of hands that beat me (Qx, 9x, 8x, pocket pairs) or medium-strength hands that will call a small bet on the river. With pot = 1,200 and my stack big enough to wait for cleaner spots, preserving chips is the better EV play. I’ll check and take it to showdown; I’ll fold to any meaningful river raise or bet from Elon.

River brings 9h, completing the heart flush draw and adding a 9 to the board (possible pairs or straights like T7 gutshot earlier unlikely). Board: 4h-3h-8d-Qc-9h. My Ac6s is pure ace-high, no pair, no flush. I c-bet flop (dryish low board), Minni called (could be pair 8/4/3, heart draw, Ax, overs); checked turn (Qc pairs board, neutral); now river check to me. Ace-high (blocks AA/AK/AQ) likely good vs her sticky BB calling range post-flop (not premium, as she'd raise pre sometimes). Checking realizes equity at showdown, avoids bloating pot OOP with marginal hand, preserves stack for better spots late tournament (hand 124/150, 2 players left). No fold needed. Pot control optimal.

Heads-up on the button with K♣4♦ — this is a hand worth opening. I’ve already posted SB 50 and only need to call 50, but calling lets the BB see a cheap flop and surrenders fold equity. Elon has shown he defends and opens a lot preflop, but a standard 3x BB open (to 300 total) here is the right exploitative play: it’s a common table sizing, gives good fold equity, denies the BB correct odds, and keeps the initiative with position. If Elon folds I pick up the pot; if he calls I play postflop in position and c-bet selectively; if he 3-bets big I’ll re-evaluate (often folding K4o to heavy 3-bets). Action: a standard heads-up raise to 300.

Heads-up play in BB vs aggressive SB/Button 3bb raise (to 300). Hand Td8s is offsuit, mediocre connectivity, poor postflop playability OOP. Better to wait for stronger hands to maximize stack over remaining hands.

Preflop heads-up on the button with AhQs offsuit, a strong premium hand in position against the BB. Heads-up vs Elon (button/SB) opened to 300. I have T8o — a playable, connected hand with decent equity vs a wide heads-up opening range. I have top pair (T♥) but a weak kicker (8♠) and I'm out of position. Preflop raised with AQo in position heads-up, strong hand. Raising/folding irrelevant here.

I have top pair (T♥) with a weak 8 kicker and I'm out of position. I have top pair (T♥) with a weak 8 kicker and I’m out of position. Elon opened wide preflop and c‑bet small on the flop, which indicates a wide range (lots of Kx, broadways, bluffs). Heads-up on button with AhQs after preflop raise and flop c-bet called by Minni, who checks turn again. I have top pair (T♥) with a very weak 8 kicker and I’m out of position. I have top pair (T) with a very weak 8 kicker and I'm out of position. River board Td7cKs2s5d completes no strong draws (spade flush draw missed, no obvious straight, diamond flush unlikely), blank card pairing nothing dangerous. Checking down suboptimal as we deny value from ~40% of her range we beat. Late tournament (126/150), aggression builds stack.

I have top pair (T♥) with a very weak 8 kicker and I’m out of position. Elon opened wide preflop and has used very small-to-medium sizing on the flop and turn, which indicates a wide continuing range containing Kx, some worse tens, and a fair number of missed broadway/Ax bluffs. Raising OOP with a weak kicker would only get called (or reraised) by better hands and fold out the worse hands I want value from, so it’s thin + low EV. Folding surrenders good pot odds against a wide line. Therefore I will call to realize equity and go to showdown (fold only to a large overbet/shove).

Heads-up on the button with deep stacks — 8c5c is a strong open-raising candidate because it has suit, postflop playability and blocks some of opponent’s broadway holdings. Limping/calling 50 gives Elon a cheap price and lets him see flops with initiative; raising gains fold equity and keeps me in position. I’ll open to a standard 3x (~300) to apply pressure, keep my range wide and small enough to play postflop comfortably. Plan: c-bet on dry favorable flops, check/float on coordinated boards, and fold to heavy 3-bet aggression depending on sizing and reads.

Heads-up against Minni's button raise to 300 (200 to call into 400 pot, ~3:1 odds). My J7o is playable vs. a wide button opening range, with 25%+ equity needed which it achieves due to jack blocker, backdoor straight/flush potential, and live cards. Deep stacks allow realizing equity post-flop despite OOP. Folding is too tight; 3-bet sizing would be large (~900+), better as a bluff with better blockers. Call and play cautiously post-flop, looking to improve or fold to aggression.

Flop: 7s Ks Qs. Heads-up OOP with top pair (7s) weak kicker Jh7c. Board is extremely wet: flush draw complete with KsQs rainbow except all spades, open-ended straight draws (JT), combo draws heavy. Minni's button raise range is wide but hits this board hard (Kx, Qx, spades, broadways). My J blocks some JT straight draws, but kicker is live only vs lower. Deep stacks (22550 vs 16850), pot 600. First to act: betting risks folding out worse (Ax, overpairs) or getting raised off equity by better (KQ, KK); donk bet non-standard here. Check standard for pot control, showdown value, induce bluffs from missed draws/overpairs. Plan: check-call small/medium bets (equity ~30-40% vs polarized range), fold to large bets/overbets unless improves. Improves to two pair/straight unlikely without specific cards. Goal: maximize stack long-term, avoid spew OOP.

Don’t c‑bet. This flop (Ks Qs 7s) is monochrome and heavily favors the caller’s range — lots of spades, K/Q single pair hands and strong broadway combos. My hand (8c5c) has no spade, no pair and very little equity, so a continuation bet would be a thin bluff with poor blockers and I’ll get called or raised by many better hands. With deep stacks and heads‑up dynamics I prefer pot control and to realize equity on non‑spade turns; if Elon leads a small sizing I can consider a call as a float, but I’ll fold to large/pressure bets. Check to control the size of the pot and look for a safe turn.

Turn Kd pairs the board kings, improving my hand to two pair: kings (Ks Kd) and sevens (7s 7c), with Jh kicker. Strong made hand heads-up. Minni's button raise pre-flop is wide; flop check-behind after my check suggests she lacks a flush, sets, or KQ (would typically bet on scary spade board for value/protection). OOP with value hand, betting builds pot from worse (KQ? rare check, but AQ/KQ possible thin), extracts from pairs/draws, denies equity to gutshots (e.g., JT/T9 now open-ended?). Pot 600, bet 2/3 pot (400) standard size for thin value + protection. Deep stacks (22k eff 16k), can fold to credible raise (monsters). Checking risks giving free card or facing bet where I call OOP suboptimally; betting takes initiative, maximizes EV long-term in tournament.

Elon fires a small probe bet (400 into a 1000 pot) on a very spade‑heavy board. I have 8c5c — no spades, no pair, only ~6 clean outs to make a single pair (~13% to hit the river). Pure card equity alone doesn’t justify a call (facing 400 into 1000 I need roughly ~22% equity), but the sizing is small and consistent with a wide range of probes/bluffs in heads‑up play. Calling keeps the pot under control, preserves positional advantage on the river, and lets me exploit bluffs or pick up the pot on a safe runout; raising here with nothing would be thin and will be called by many better hands. Plan: call the 400 now; on the river I’ll re‑evaluate — if Elon barrels a large bet into a non‑spade blank I can consider a bluff/raise line, but I’ll fold to substantial pressure on spade/king/queen rivers.

River 2h is a complete blank, no flush completes (spades already possible), no new draws, doesn't help likely holdings. My two pair (kings and sevens) remains strong, ahead of worse Kx (KJo, KTo), Qx, mid pairs that called turn. OOP on river, pot 1400, stacks deep (eff ~16k). Value betting thin extracts from worse (weaker kickers/pairs); size 2/3 pot (900) gets calls from ~1/3 of range (e.g., KQ unlikely, but KT/QT/Ax), denies showdown to bluffs, builds stack in late tournament. Checking risks free showdown or small hero calls OOP; bet maximizes EV, prepared to call credible raises (monsters rare in her capped range). Long-term stack growth prioritizes thin value here.

Board pairs on the turn and is extremely spade‑heavy (7s Ks Qs / Kd / 2h). I have 8c5c — no pair, no spade, no meaningful showdown equity; I can only win if Elon is bluffing. Bluff‑raising to the 1800 minimum is also thin: I have no relevant blockers and would be putting a lot of chips in front of many hands that will call. With deep stacks but a very poor hand and little reason to expect a profitable bluff catch, the correct play is to fold and preserve stack for better spots.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 92o, a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and playability post-flop. While button ranges are wide in HU play, 92o is marginal at best and dominated easily. Pot is small (150), to-call 50 for a limp, but limping SB leads to awkward dynamics. Minni defends wide based on history. With deep stacks (235BB effective) and only 22 hands left, preserve stack for premium spots to maximize EV and avoid tough post-flop decisions with trash. Folding is the disciplined play.

Heads-up on the button with K♦T♥ — a strong, playable hand that does well with position. I want to seize initiative, build the pot when I'm likely ahead of the BB's defending range, and avoid letting Elon see flops cheaply. Table tendencies show 300 is a common open size here, so a raise to 300 (≈3×BB) is a standard, non-committal aggressive play: it preserves fold equity, keeps SPR reasonable given our stacks, and gives us position postflop. If Elon 3-bets large I can fold and protect my stack; if he calls I can play positionally against a wide range.

Heads-up vs Minni on the button/SB who raised to 300. My hand 6d7s is a suited connector with good playability, multiway potential isn't an issue here, and deep stacks (23k effective). Getting great pot odds: calling 200 more into 400 pot (~3:1 implied, but direct 2:1). Standard defense range includes this; folding is too tight, 3-betting OOP risks playing OOP postflop with a speculative hand. Minni raises wide from SB, so equity realization postflop favors calling to see flop.

Flop board 6s 9d 4d gives me second pair (6s with 7 kicker) on a draw-heavy board with diamond flush draw prominent and possible straight draws (5-8, 7-8, 5x). Pot 600, Minni checked OOP after raising preflop from SB/Button (wide range). Deep stacks (effective ~16k). Size 2/3 pot (400) balances value/protection without overbetting speculative hand strength. Checking risks free cards to strong draws or giving up pot OOP advantage.

Elon called my preflop raise and now makes a small c‑bet (400 into a 1000 pot) on a dry 6♠-9♦-4♦ flop. I hold K♦T♥ — two overcards to the 9 and a backdoor diamond — which has reasonable equity vs a wide continuation range and also blocks some diamond/king combinations. A min‑raise to 800 is a strong, low‑risk semi‑bluff: it applies immediate pressure to many of his marginal c‑bet hands, preserves fold equity, and builds the pot when I’m often ahead or at least flip‑ish. The size is small relative to our stacks so I can fold to significant 3‑bet/shore‑shove while still forcing lots of turns to fold. If he calls I’ll proceed positionally (barrel favorable turn cards, check/control on blanks); if he shows big aggression I will fold. Raising to 800 maximizes immediate fold equity while keeping future options flexible.

Heads-up vs aggressive Minni (Button/SB) who check-raised my flop bet IP. Board 6s9d4d is draw-heavy (diamond FD prominent, gutshots like 57, 85). My 6d7s = bottom pair + weak kicker + backdoor FD (6d). I bet flop for thin value/protection vs her wide checking range (Ax, weaker pairs, floats, draws). Her check-raise polarizes to strong value (99/66/44/96s/94s/9x strong), FD combos (KdQd/JdTd/87d suited etc), some bluffs (A-high overs, pure gutshots). Pot 1800, call 400 (~19% pot odds, need ~18% equity). IP deep (~15k eff), bottom pair realizes well vs polarized range (often live or ahead bluffs/draws), turn playability good (can fold to villains if improves badly). Folding spews after rep strength; raising risks bloating pot OOP response with marginal hand (sets/draws crush). Call to pot control, realize equity IP, induce further bluffs if weak.

Heads-up OOP on turn with bottom pair (6s) + 7 kicker after calling flop check-raise. Board 6s9d4d3d is extremely wet: three diamonds complete any two-diamond flush combos (e.g., AdXd, KdQd, JdTd, suited connectors like 75d, 52d), possible straights (345 for wheel with 5x, or 567 with 5), and overpairs/sets still crush. Pot 2200, stacks deep (~15k effective). My hand has showdown value vs bluffs/draws from her polarized flop check-raise range (value: 99/66/44/96s/94s; bluffs: A-high, gutshots like T5/J5/85; draws: flush/straight). Turn 3d worsens it by completing flushes and adding gutshots, reducing my equity vs value but keeping live vs incomplete draws/bluffs. Betting polarizes further, invites raises from flushes/sets/straights (no fold equity vs her aggression), and bloats pot OOP with thin value. Checking pot-controls, induces bluffs from missed draws (e.g., spade flush draws, pure air), allows realization of equity IP on river if checked back, and keeps pot manageable (~2k vs 15k stacks). Folding spews; raising suicidal OOP with marginal hand. Standard check OOP after defensive call on scary board.

I raised the flop as a semi‑bluff with K♦T♥, but the turn 3♦ completes three diamonds on the board. I only have one diamond and no pair — I’m on a single‑card flush draw (9 outs, ~19.6%) plus backdoor pair outs — while Elon’s check can be a completed flush, a pair, or a trapping line. With stacks deep and the pot already 2,200, I prefer pot control and to realize my equity cheaply rather than risking a large bet into a range that now includes made flushes. Checking lets me see the river for free, keep maneuverability (call/bluff river), and avoid getting stacked by a strong made hand.

River 4c pairs the 4d, improving me to two pair (6s and 4s with 7 kicker) on a paired board (pair of 4s). Board: 6s 9d 4d 3d 4c is draw-heavy but river blank for flushes/straights beyond prior completes. Pot 2200, OOP first to act, deep effective stacks (~15k Minni). Minni's flop check-raise range (after my c-bet): polarized to value (sets 99/66/44, two pair 94/69/64, strong Ax) or bluffs/draws (diamond FDs, gutshots like T5/A5/J5); called my call OOP, then checked turn after scary 3d (flush complete), suggesting missed draws/non-nut flushes/slowplay value. Betting ~55% pot (1200) extracts value from her weaker pairs/A-high/missed draws/floats in calling range (history shows Minni calls rivers frequently), denies equity to any lingering draws, represents strength post-check-check turn. Checking risks free showdown (her 9x/4x/Ax beats) or small bets I call; her aggression warrants protection. Fold equity vs polarized bluffs; if raised, re-eval (snap-fold to 4x/sets unlikely but possible). Maximizes EV OOP with disguised strong hand.

Pot is 3,400 and Elon leads the river for 1,200 — I need ~26% equity to justify a call (1,200 / (3,400+1,200)). I missed my one-card diamond on the river and hold only K‑high, so I’m behind to most clean value hands (pairs, trips, or any made flush). However Elon’s line (small c‑bet on flop, call my flop raise, both checked the turn, then a relatively small 1,200 river stab) is consistent with a thin value/bluff mix — many missed single‑diamond draws or pure air hands can be betting this size. I also block a number of strong diamond combos by holding K♦, and calling 1,200 is a small percentage of my 15,400 stack (~7.8%), so the risk is limited while the pot odds are attractive. Raising as a bluff is too risky with virtually no showdown value and will be called by better hands; folding here would concede too much equity against a mixed river range. I will call to capture bluffs and weaker value bets.

Heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB with 7c2s, a trash hand with no connectivity, low cards, offsuit, poor playability post-flop, and low showdown value. Posted SB 50 already, pot 150, to call 50 more. While heads-up SB ranges are wide (40-60% raises typical), 72o is in the bottom tier of hands to fold. Opponent Minni plays aggressively but folding preserves stack for better spots with only 20 hands left. No need to risk with this unplayable hand.

Heads-up on the button with 4♦6♠. I’ve already posted the SB (50); pot is 150 and it costs me 50 to see a flop. This is a cheap defend — calling 50 (100 total committed) gives me 25% break-even equity and I’m in position postflop, so I can fold to pressure or barrel when I pick up equity. I won’t inflate the pot with a marginal, offsuit hand by raising. Plan: see the flop; continue with a pair, decent draw or favorable texture; check/fold to large aggression if I miss.

Heads-up in BB vs SB limp (effective call). 89o is a strong speculative hand heads-up with good playability, straight potential, and position advantage postflop if called. Pot is small (200), stacks deep (effective 14k). Raising builds the pot, applies pressure on Minni's wide limping range, and takes initiative. SB limps often indicate weak hands; they fold frequently to raises. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is standard sizing here late in tournament to steal or set up postflop. Checking risks giving free card OOP, better to raise for value/protection.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with 4♦6♠ vs Elon’s raise to 300. This is a marginal, offsuit holding so I don’t want to inflate the pot by raising. Calling the 200 more (300 total committed) is cheap — I get ~33% break-even odds and I have position postflop to maneuver. Plan: see the flop and continue only with a pair, good draw, or a favorable board; fold to heavy turn/river aggression if I miss. Preserve stack and use position to realize equity rather than spew chips now.

Flop board: 7c6cQd gives me an open-ended straight draw (8 outs: 4x5, 4xT) for ~32% equity, plus backdoor diamond flush potential with 8d. OOP as BB, pot 600, Minni called my preflop raise from SB limp (wide range: pairs, suited connectors, Ax, broadways). Board hits her range (Q, suited connectors for draws, clubs) but favors my playability. C-betting standard for initiative, value from worse draws/pairs (QJ,77,66, overpairs), protection vs her floats/draws, deny equity. Sizing 2/3 pot (400) balances value/protection without overbetting into her calling station tendencies seen in history (frequent calls stationy postflop). Deep stacks, 20 hands left, build pot with equity. Check risks giving free card to her draws or check-raise trap.

I have 6♠4♦ on 7♣ 6♣ Q♦ — second pair (6s) with a weak kicker, and I’m in position. Elon’s small c-bet (400 into a 1000 pot) gives me good pot odds: I need roughly 400/(1000+400) ≈ 28.6% equity to justify a call. Heads-up his continuation range is wide (top pairs, overcards, club draws, and some air), so my pair of sixes has reasonable equity and can realize more in position. Raising to 800 is possible to charge club draws, but with my weak kicker and his ability to (re)raise, I don’t want to inflate the pot unnecessarily. I’ll call to keep the pot controllable, use position to probe the turn, and fold to heavy action on a club or clear-showdown-better card unless I improve. Call 400 more (700 total committed).

Turn Jc completes the club flush draw and adds straight possibilities (e.g., 8T, KT for straights), making this a scary coordinated board. My 89h still holds a strong open-ended straight draw (8 outs: 4x5s, 4x10s for nuts straights), ~32% equity vs. a calling range, plus backdoor diamond equity, but no made hand and vulnerable to flushes, two pairs (QJ, JJ), sets. OOP after flop c-bet called into a pot of 1400, with Minni's SB limp-call range including Qx, mid pairs, clubs, suited connectors/draws that many improved. History shows Minni stationy (frequent calls to rivers, rarely folds post-flop aggression), so betting risks value-owning with worse or getting raised off equity by made hands. Checking induces bluffs from missed draws (e.g., incomplete clubs, overcards), realizes equity cheaply OOP, pots small for river jam if checked through or small bet, preserves deep stack (25k) vs. her shorter 13.5k with 19 hands left in tournament. No fold equity strong enough to bet profitably here; better to check and play post-flop dynamics.

I have second pair (6♠) with a weak 4 kicker on 7♣-6♣-Q♦-J♣. Elon checked the turn after barreling the flop and I called — his check looks like pot-control/weakness rather than a massive made flush (which would require two clubs in hand). In position I prefer to take a controlled stab to get value from worse hands (weaker pairs, Jx, some Kx/Tx) and deny equity to backdoor holdings, while keeping the pot manageable if I face resistance. I’ll make a modest bet rather than check back and give him a free river — bet ~600 into the 1400 pot (~40% pot). If he raises, I can fold this weak-kicker second pair; if he folds I take it down, if he calls I go to river and re-evaluate.

Turn board Jc completes the club flush (7c6cJc) and adds straight draws (T8, 85, K10 etc.), very coordinated and scary. Holding 89h for open-ended straight draw (8 outs to nuts straight: 4x5, 4xT, ~32% equity vs calling range of Qx, clubs, pairs, draws), plus backdoor diamonds. Pot 2000, OOP after check vs Minni's 600 bet (~40% pot, small sizing suggesting thin value/weak flush/A-high clubs/Qx or semi-bluff). Her SB limp-call range hits flop often but polarized post-call: made hands (QJ,77/66 sets, clubs) or draws that improved/missed. History: Minni calling station (folds ~30% to river aggression but vulnerable pre/post), but late tournament (19 hands left, her 13k eff vs my 25k), need to maximize EV. Folding terrible (pot odds 600 to 2600 = 4.3:1, equity >>19%). Call realizes equity cheaply OOP but vulnerable to river value/obv lines. Raising optimal: value from worse (weak flushes, Qx, overpairs, missed clubs), fold equity vs bluffs/draws (~25% FE estimated), deny her equity, build pot for river jam/shove with hit/miss (implied odds huge). Sizing to 2000 total (3.3x her bet) balances, commits ~15% her stack, pot to 4000 if called; she folds weak often enough, calls nutted, I have odds to call jam (~30% equity vs nuts). Aggressive pro line maximizes stack long-term.

I have 6♠4♦ — second pair with a very weak kicker on 7♣-6♣-Q♦-J♣. I bet a controlled 600 on the turn and Elon check‑raised me to 2000. The board has three clubs, and his line (bet flop, check turn, then a large check‑raise) is highly polarized toward strong value hands (two clubs for a flush, sets 66/77, Qx/Jx turned into two‑pair, combos like QJ) with relatively few bluffs. To call 1,400 into a 4,000 pot gives only ~26% pot odds, and my equity versus that value‑heavy range is well below that — plus my kicker is poor and I have no club. Calling or raising here commits a large portion of my stack in a spot where I’m likely behind. Fold and preserve stack for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button (SB), I have position post-flop. Ts4c is a playable hand in a wide button opening range, which should be around 50-60% in HU deepstack play. It has a ten blocker, backdoor flush and straight potential. Minni defends wide from BB but folds often enough to make stealing profitable. Raising standard 3x BB (300 total) applies pressure and takes initiative. Folding is too tight; limping is suboptimal as it allows easy check-raises.

Elon opened to 300 from the button (SB). In heads-up the BB should defend widely — 7♦6♦ is a strong speculative hand with good equity and excellent implied odds against a likely wide opening range. Pot is ~400, calling 200 more (300 total committed) gives ~33% pot odds; suited connectors have better than that vs a wide range and can make straights/flushes/two-pair. 3-betting out of position with 7♦6♦ is suboptimal — it bloats the pot with a marginal hand and I have less fold equity OOP unless I make a large sizing. I’ll call to realize equity, keep the pot manageable, and play postflop for value or fold as the board dictates.

Check. With 7♦6♦ on 5♠-8♦-J♥ I have an open-ended straight draw (4s or 9s — 8 outs, ~31–32% to improve to river) plus a backdoor diamond possibility. I’m out of position and Elon opened wide heads‑up, so I have decent equity and good implied odds — no need to bloat the pot OOP with a semi-bluff that has limited fold equity. Plan: check and call reasonable bets to realize equity; if I hit my straight (or a very favorable turn) I’ll extract value, but I’ll fold to an overly large raise that makes a call incorrect.

Flop: 5s8dJh rainbow board, dryish with possible 67, 9T gutshots, but no strong draws. My Ts4c has no pair, no straight draw, backdoor spade flush, and T overcard blocking some JTs/OTB. Pre-flop I raised wide from BTN/SB, Minni called from BB with wide defending range including many floats, weak Ax, offsuit broadways, suited junk. She checked, showing weakness. High fold equity here as continuation bet standard in position HU (~60-70% freq on dry boards). Bet small ~40% pot to maintain balance, deny equity to her floats/draws, and take it down often while keeping pot small if called. Avoids bloating pot OOP with air. Pot 600, bet 250 semi-bluff.

Elon (BTN) c‑bet small into a pot of 850; I must call 250. With 7♦6♦ on 5♠‑8♦‑J♥ I have an open‑ended straight draw (8 outs → ~31–32% to improve to the river) plus a tiny backdoor diamond/paired‑outs equity. If I call 250 the pot after my call will be ~1100, so my required equity is ~250/1100 ≈ 22.7%, which is well below my actual equity — calling is +EV. I’m out of position, stacks are deep, and a flop 3‑bet/semi‑bluff would bloat the pot with a drawing hand that prefers to realize equity; raising here gives limited extra fold equity and can commit me unnecessarily. Plan: call to keep the pot manageable, see the turn, and extract value if I hit (or apply controlled aggression if a favorable turn appears). If I miss and face heavy turn pressure I’ll fold; if villain slows I can consider a river bluff or showdown value line.

I check. On 5♠-8♦-J♥-3♦ with 7♦6♦ I have a strong double-draw: an open‑ender (4s or 9s) plus a four‑card diamond flush draw. Unique outs = 15 (8 straight outs + 9 diamond outs − 2 overlap) → 15/46 ≈ 32.6% to improve on the river. Pot is ~1,100 and stacks are deep (I have 12,400, Elon 26,500). Betting OOP as a semi‑bluff here is unattractive — it bloats the pot when I’m likely to be called by many worse hands (Jx, 8x, A/K) and exposes me to a shove/raise that’s hard to continue against. Checking keeps the pot controlled and lets me realize equity. If Elon bets the turn I’ll call small-to-medium bets (math: calling is +EV up to roughly b ≈ 530 into 1,100 since b/(1,100+b) ≈ 32.6%); I’ll fold to oversized pressure. If he checks back I get a free river to try to hit and can extract value when I improve. So check and play the river based on his sizing/line.

Turn 3d is a relatively blank card for villain's likely check-call flop range (weak pairs like 5x/8x/Jx low, floats, gutshots like 67/9T, Ax). It doesn't complete strong draws (no flush really, minor straight possibilities but unlikely). Villain checks again, confirming weakness. In position HU deepstack, continue with small c-bet ~40% pot for thin value/protection against any remaining equity while maintaining high fold equity vs her floats/weak holdings. Ts blocks JTs/T9s type hands. Pot control important with no showdown value, but betting extracts folds and builds pot if villain miraculously has it. If called, can check river and fold to bet. Checking risks giving free cards but pot small anyway. Betting maximizes EV by stealing frequently.

Call. Math and ICM: pot is 1,550 and Elon has bet 450; calling 450 makes the total pot 2,000 so my break‑even equity is 450/2,000 = 22.5%. I have 7♦6♦ on 5♠-8♦-J♥-3♦ with both a four‑card diamond draw (9 diamond outs) and an open‑ended straight draw to 4 or 9 (8 straight outs) with 2 cards overlapping (4♦ and 9♦). Unique outs = 9 + 8 − 2 = 15 → 15/46 ≈ 32.6% to improve on the river, well above the ~22.5% required. Elon has been opening and c‑betting fairly wide and using small/medium sizes, so calling to realize equity OOP is +EV. Raising OOP with a draw would bloat the pot, give Elon easy shove/3‑bet leverage, and commit more with only draw equity; folding would be too tight given the pot odds. Plan: call 450, see the river — if I hit a straight or flush I extract value; if I miss I’ll fold to very large overbets but can call small/medium river bets that offer correct odds.

Elon checked the river to me. I missed both the straight and the diamond flush — 7♦6♦ on 5♠-8♦-J♥-3♦-2♥ has no showdown value. I had planned to call a river bet (450) given the pot odds if he bet, but since he checked I should not risk chips bluffing into a single opponent who has shown a willingness to use small-to-medium bet sizes and can call with any pair or even some worse. Checking gets to showdown for free and preserves my stack; if he truly has air I still win, and if he has a pair I avoid unnecessary losses. Choose the pot-control line and check.

River 2h is a blank that doesn't complete any strong draws or improve many hands in villain's check-call flop/turn range (primarily weak pairs like 5x/8x/Jx weak, Ax highs, missed gutshots/floats). Board remains dry: possible straights are unlikely (e.g., no clear 4-6 for wheel or 6-7-9-10 for broadway fits). I have position, history of small c-bets flop/turn representing a balanced range including value (sets, two-pair, top pair good, combo draws that hit) and bluffs. Villain's double check-call suggests capped range without premium value, as she'd lead or raise stronger hands earlier. Checking back risks her having showdown value and chopping pot or winning small; betting exploits her weakness, applies max pressure with high fold equity (~60-70% in HU spot), and balances my range. Sizing ~65% pot (1300) is aggressive yet pot-controlling, gets calls from weaker holdings if she has them while folding out better than showdown hands like small pairs/A-high. Deep effective stacks (hers 11.9k) allow flexibility; if called/shoved, we have ~9:1 to call odds but polarized range favors fold. Consistent aggression builds image over remaining 18 hands to maximize stack.

I have missed every draw — 7♦6♦ on 5♠‑8♦‑J♥‑3♦‑2♥ has no showdown value. Elon’s line (preflop raise, small c‑bets on flop/turn, now a 1,300 river bet into a 3,300 pot) looks value‑heavy (Jx/8x/5x/pocket pairs) and he is unlikely to be bluffing often enough. The break‑even equity to call is ~1,300/(3,300+1,300) ≈ 28%, which my hand cannot realistically achieve at showdown. Raising as a bluff is too thin (poor blockers, big risk if called or raised). Fold and preserve the stack for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button with premium pocket Queens (QQ). I’ve already posted the SB (pot 150, 50 to call). Against a deep-stacked BB who defends wide, QQ is almost always best — I want value and initiative while avoiding giving free flops. Standard, table-consistent sizing is to open to ~3x the BB (300). This builds the pot, keeps worse hands and broadways in, and leaves plenty of room postflop with 11,900 effective. Calling 50 is too passive; min-raising only to 200 is okay but smaller than the common sizing and invites more marginal play. I’ll raise to 300 and plan to c-bet most flops for value and protection.

AdKd is a premium hand, suited and dominating most of Minni's button raising range. Facing a standard button raise of 300 into 400 pot, calling leaves me out of position postflop, which is suboptimal. 3-betting isolates and builds the pot with the best hand preflop. Minni has shown aggression but also folds to 3-bets in history (e.g., hands where raises got through). Sizing to 900 (3x the raise) is standard, applying pressure while keeping her stacks in play (her 11k covers it). Late in tournament (hand 133/150), need to accumulate chips heads-up.

I opened to 300 on the button with QQ and Elon 3‑bet to 900. Heads‑up QQ is a premium — I want initiative and value while keeping the action heads‑up. Calling 900 and seeing a flop in position is reasonable, but I prefer to 4‑bet here to isolate Elon’s 3‑bet range (AK, AQ, JJ/TT, some weaker broadways) and to charge his lighter hands. I’ll 4‑bet to a size large enough to deny cheap flops but small enough to preserve fold equity and not commit my stack (I have ~116bb). Raising to 3,600 total achieves that: it extracts value from worse pairs and strong broadways, discourages light 5‑bets, and leaves room to maneuver postflop in position. Plan: if Elon folds I take the pot; if he calls I’ll use my position and the overpair to apply pressure on most flops; if he 5‑bets big I’ll re‑evaluate (QQ is close to a call against many shove ranges but I won’t auto‑call a narrow all‑in).

AdKd is still one of the best hands heads-up. Minni's button open was wide given history, my 3-bet was standard, and her 4-bet to 3600 (43% of her 8350 stack) is polarized: value like AA/KK/QQ/AK or bluffs with blockers (Axs, KQs suited). Folding premium is too tight. Calling 2700 OOP into 7200 pot is suboptimal positionally with large pot, as she can shove any flop and put me to tough decisions. 4-bet shoving my 27150 applies max pressure; she can only call 8350 more, giving huge fold equity vs her bluffs/light value (which history suggests she has), gets value from worse AK, and has ~30%+ equity vs AA/KK. Late in tournament (133/150, heads-up), accumulating by making her fold equity-advantaged hands maximizes stack EV.

Elon has shoved to 27,150 over my 4‑bet to 3,600, leaving me to call 8,350 to put my remaining stack in. The pot is ~30,750, so calling 8,350 gets ~21.4% break‑even equity (8,350 / (30,750+8,350)). QQ is a very strong heads‑up hand and will have well over ~21% equity against a realistic polarized shove range (AA/AK/KK and occasional bluffs). Versus AA only I’m a bit behind (~18%) and would fold, but Elon’s line (small 3‑bet then massive shove) is likely polarized and contains enough AK/KK and worse that QQ is +EV given the pot odds. Folding surrenders a large pot we built and is too tight here. I’ll call and commit my remaining chips (total committed this hand = 11,950).

Preflop, heads-up on the button/SB vs BB. Pot is 150, to call 50. My hand J4o is playable from this position in a heads-up dynamic with deep stacks. Minni has been calling raises frequently from history, but we have position postflop and can realize equity. Standard play is to raise a wide range (50-70%+) to steal blinds or build pot OOP disadvantage for opponent. Raising to 300 is sized appropriately, about 3x the BB, consistent with prior opening sizes. Folding too tight near endgame; limping invites trouble. Goal is max stack, so apply pressure.

I defend the big blind and call the extra 200 (300 total). Heads-up I need to play a wide range, and K3o has enough showdown value against a frequent 3x open. Elon opened to 300 with 15800 behind; I’m out of position postflop, stacks are deep, and K3o is a marginal 3-bet bluff — I’d rather keep the pot small and see a flop. Postflop plan: continue aggressively if I pair my king or pick up strong draws; otherwise fold to significant pressure.

Check. I have K♣3♦ — a small (middle) pair of 3s with a K kicker and one diamond on a A♦ 3♥ J♦ board. Heads‑up Elon's open is wide so I'm ahead of a fair number of bluffs and broadway overcards, but I'm behind any Ax or Jx and vulnerable to big turn/river action. Out of position with deep stacks I want pot control: induce a c‑bet and realize showdown value rather than build a bloated pot with a marginal hand. Plan: check-call a reasonable single barrel to see a turn (call small‑to‑medium bets), but fold to large sizing/raises that commit a big portion of stacks.

Heads-up on the button with position postflop. Flop Ad 3h Jd gives me middle pair (Jh) with a weak 4s kicker and backdoor spade flush draw. Board has two diamonds, so flush draws and straight draws (e.g., KQ, AT) are possible. Minni checked, representing possible weakness. Pot is 600; betting ~2/3 pot (400) charges draws, gets value from Ax worse than A-high, worse Js, or bluffs, while protecting against overcards or draws. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws that can outdraw me. With deep stacks (15.8k effective) and tournament nearing end (134/150), building pot with showdown value and applying pressure aligns with maximizing stack. Minni calls frequently in history, so expect some folds or calls we can beat.

Heads‑up vs an open/continuation range that's wide, my K♣3♦ is a marginal but reasonable hand to continue with — I have middle pair (trip? No, pair of 3s) with a decent K kicker and a backdoor diamond possibility, and I'm ahead of a lot of pure overcards and bluffs but behind any A‑ or J‑x. Elon’s flop action is a relatively small c‑bet into a shallow pot; I prefer pot control with deep stacks and want to realize showdown value rather than build a bloated pot out of position. Calling the 400 keeps worse hands and bluffs in, gives me the right to fold to large turn aggression, and preserves the option to value‑raise only if I improve (pair my K or make trips) or pick up a strong draw. Plan after calling: fold to big turn barrels that commit a large portion of my stack, but see one more street cheaply and re‑evaluate on the turn. Action: call.

Board: A♦ 3♥ J♦ 6♦. I hold K♣3♦ — a pair of 3s with a K kicker plus a four‑to‑a‑diamond flush (one card to complete, ~9 outs). Elon opened wide pre and c‑bet the flop (smallish) then checked the turn, showing some hesitancy/pot control. Heads‑up this is a classic pot‑control spot: I’m ahead of many pure overcards and bluffs but behind any A‑ or J‑x. With deep stacks and out of position I want to realize equity and avoid building a big pot with a marginal made hand. Betting here would get value from very few worse hands and fold out hands I beat; it also creates risk of a check‑raise by stronger hands. Checking keeps worse hands and bluffs in, preserves my ~9‑outs flush equity, and lets me re‑evaluate the river. Plan: check back the turn; call small/medium river bets with my pair+draw, fold to large polarized river pressure unless I complete or improve. Action: check.

Heads-up on the button with position. Board Ad 3h Jd 6d features three diamonds, making it flush-heavy and scary for many hands, but I hold second pair (Jh for Jacks) with a 4s kicker and backdoor spade potential. Pot is 1400 after Minni's flop call and turn check. Minni has checked twice, showing potential weakness post-flop despite calling pre-flop and flop, consistent with her history of calling stations but folding to continued pressure in spots. Betting continues my range story from pre-flop raise and flop bet, charging any remaining draws (non-nut diamond draws beat us), worse Ax (like A2-A5, A-low), or weaker pairs. Checking risks free rivers that complete straights or improve draws. With deep effective stacks (~15k), tournament late stage (134/150), a half-pot bet of 900 extracts value from calling ranges we dominate while keeping fold equity against air/draws. Maximizes EV by building pot with showdown value and position. Minni's history shows frequent calls but folds to aggression in multi-street spots.

I have K♣3♦ on A♦ 3♥ J♦ 6♦: middle pair (treys) with a K kicker plus a four‑card diamond draw. That gives me ~9 diamond outs + 2 remaining treys (≈11 outs) — roughly a 24% chance to improve on the river and overall showdown equity vs a wide, Ace/J‑weighted barreling range ≈30%+. Elon opened wide preflop and c‑bet the flop small; his turn bet of 900 is a medium sizing and offers ~28% pot odds (900 to win ≈3200). Calling is +EV: it keeps worse hands and bluffs in, realizes my equity, and preserves my ability to fold to large polarized river pressure if I miss. Raising OOP with a marginal made hand + draw is unnecessary and can get me exploited. Plan: call 900, re‑evaluate on the river — value/raise if I complete, fold to heavy polarized bet if I do not improve.

Final board A♦ 3♥ J♦ 6♦ 6♠. I hold K♣3♦ — just one pair (treys) with a King kicker; my diamond draw missed. Elon opened wide preflop, c‑bet small on the flop and bet a medium 900 on the turn (I called). He checks the river, which can be either weakness or a trap, but given his line a lot of his value hands (A‑x, J‑x, any 6 that makes trips, and many pocket pairs) still beat me. There are relatively few worse hands that will call a river bet, so betting here is thin value and bluffing is risky (I can get check‑raised or called by better hands). With a bigger stack and only one pair, the prudent play is to take showdown cheaply. Action: check to see his cards; if he fires a small bet I can consider a call for pot odds, but I will fold to large polarized river pressure.

River board pairs the 6s (Ad 3h Jd 6d 6s), bricking most straight/flush draws that were live on turn (diamond flush already complete, spade flush unlikely without prior spades). Holds pair of Jacks (second pair/top non-Ace pair) with 4s kicker. Minni checked four streets OOP after calling preflop raise + flop/turn bets (pot now 3200), consistent with her history as a calling station with medium strength (likely weak Ax like A2-A5/A7o, weaker Jx like JT/J9, 6x for trips like 67/A6/J6 two pair, or bluff-catchers). Traps with monsters (sets, full houses, nut flush) unlikely OOP passive line. Position allows value bet ~60% pot (2000) extracting from dominated hands (~30-50% of her calling range per history: weak A/J/6 we beat) that call frequently while top of range (Aces, two pair+, nut flush) can raise/shove, to which we fold. Minimal fold equity but high EV vs her sticky style (e.g., hands 14,20,35,73,78,99 calls multi-street into beats). Checking misses massive value OOP showdowns we win; bet maximizes stack heads-up late (134/150, 14.5k eff). Consistent with range (AJ+/sets/bluffs barreling).

I hold K♣3♦. The river paired the 6, so my best hand is two pair (6s and 3s). Elon opened wide preflop, c‑bet the flop and barreled the turn — now he bets 2,000 into a 5,200 pot on the river. That sizing is relatively small (≈38% pot), so calling 2,000 requires ~28% equity (2,000 / (5,200 + 2,000) ≈ 27.8%).

Which hands beat me: any 6 (trips/quads), any A‑x or J‑x (two pair A6 or J6), most higher pocket pairs and diamond flushes. Which hands I beat or can get value from: missed diamond draws, Kx/Qx/TX bluffs, and some weaker pairs. Given his line I expect a mix of value (A/J/6/flush) and bluffs; his small river sizing keeps a fair number of bluffs and worse hands in. Raising OOP is poor — it will get called/raised by many better hands and fold out only the very weakest hands, so it’s thin and exploitative in the wrong direction. Folding here surrenders ~5,200 in equity when I have reasonable pot‑odds and showdown value.

Decision: call the 2,000. I’ll only fold to large shove/raise (if he repopulates heavily), but calling to realize my equity and see showdown is the +EV line.

Heads-up on the button with K♦8♠ — a hand that's well worth opening preflop. I have position postflop and Elon in the big blind tends to defend widely but also barrels a lot; raising takes the initiative, prices him to fold weaker holdings, and lets me play small pots in position. With both stacks ~200+ BB I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand, so a standard 3x BB open is appropriate. Plan: if called, use position to c-bet when I connect (K or good-board texture) or control the pot on dry boards; fold to large 3-bets unless I have a strong read. Raise to 300 to take the lead and keep options postflop.

Q2o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability postflop. While heads-up BB defends wide against button opens, this hand is too dominated by better queens and lacks connectivity or suitedness. Pot odds are 3:1 (calling 200 into 400 pot), but equity against Minni's wide button raising range (~40-50%) is marginal (~35-40%), not justifying a call deepstacked. Folding preserves stack for premium spots in final hands.

Heads-up on the button/SB with Ac7h, a strong hand for this spot with position and a strong ace blocker. Deep stacks (195BB effective), but near end of tournament. Standard play is to raise to isolate or build pot, as equity vs Minni's BB calling range (~60-65% vs random, better vs tight) is favorable. Minni defends wide from history but folds to aggression often preflop. Raise to 3x BB (300 total) for value/thin value and to deny equity. No limp as it invites multiway trouble; fold too weak.

Elon (button) opened to 300. I’m in the big blind with 8♥9♣ and a deep stack (20,300 ≈ 203bb). The pot is 400 and it costs me 200 more to call (total commitment 300) — roughly 3:1 pot odds, so I need ~25% equity. Heads‑up the opener will be very wide and 8‑9o has playable equity and excellent implied odds with these stacks. Out of position I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand by 3‑betting; calling keeps the pot manageable and lets me realize equity on favorable flops. I’ll call and play the flop.

I check. With 8♥9♣ on Q♠ A♦ 2♣ I have no pair and no meaningful draw, and I’m out of position. Elon opened preflop so his flop range contains lots of A‑x and Q‑x hands that already beat me; I have negligible fold equity and no value to bet. Checking controls the pot with deep stacks, lets me see how Elon acts, and keeps the option to call a small c‑bet as a float against his wide heads‑up range. Plan: if he checks back I realize equity and see a free turn; if he bets small (~1/4–1/2 pot) I’ll call to continue, but I’ll fold to a large bet since I don’t improve.

Flop board Qs Ad 2c is dry and rainbow, with minimal draws (no flush or straight draws really viable). Ac7h gives top pair top kicker (pair of Aces with strong Ace blocker and decent 7 kicker), crushing Minni's likely wide BB defending range (which includes worse Ax, Qx, pairs, bluffs). Minni checked, indicating weakness or trapping, but history shows she calls light but folds to continued aggression. Deep stacks but endgame; bet for value, protection against runner-runner, and to build pot/deny equity to floats/draws. Half-pot sizing (400 into 600) balances value from worse and thin value from Qx/Kx while keeping pot manageable if raised. Checking risks giving free cards and losing value.

Call. Elon’s flop bet (400 into 1000 ≈ 40% pot) is a small c‑bet against a wide heads‑up opening range — lots of A‑x/Q‑x and many bluffs. 8♥9♣ has no pair and only weak backdoor outs, but the pot odds (call 400 to win ~1400, need ≈29% equity) plus deep stacks and good implied odds justify a float. I don’t want to 3‑bet out of position with a marginal hand (would bloat the pot and I lack fold equity), and folding surrenders too much vs a wide range. Plan: call to see the turn, fold to a large turn barrel, but continue/on blank or small turn bets to try to take the pot away or realize equity on a later street.

I have 8♥9♣ on Q♠ A♦ 2♣ 3♣ — no pair and effectively no meaningful draw with one card to come (can only pair an 8 or 9). Elon checked the turn, giving me better information: his range includes both turned-down strength (checks with some A/Q and medium made hands) and pure air. With deep stacks (≈197–189bb) I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand out of position. A small bluff here would get called too often by Ax/Qx and by float/call hands, and if called I’m committed with no real equity. Checking controls the pot, preserves stack, and lets me see the river for free — I can still bluff or value-bet on the river if it favors me, or fold to aggression if I miss. Action: check behind and re-evaluate on the river.

Turn board brings the 3c, pairing nothing but adding a club flush draw and a gutshot wheel straight draw (4x). My Ac7h remains TPTK, blocking strong Ax hands like AA, AK, AQ. Minni's check-call flop range includes weaker Ax (A-rag, suited), Qx, mid pairs (77-JJ crushed), some bluffs/draws that missed flop but called. Her turn check suggests weakness or a trap, but history shows she calls light postflop but often folds to continued aggression when behind. Deep effective stacks (~189BB), but near tournament end (136/150), so value betting thin is key to maximize EV without overexposing to draws. Pot 1400, betting ~70% pot (1000) targets value from Qx, worse Ax, pairs while charging draws (clubs ~18% equity, gutshots less). Position allows controlling if raised. Checking risks free river completion (clubs hit 9 outs, scary rivers) and missing value from calling range. Bet sizing balances value/protection, denies equity, builds pot for river value if ahead.

Board Q♠ A♦ 2♣ 3♣, I hold 8♥9♣ — no pair, no real draw. Pot is 2400, Elon bets 1000; calling 1000 to win ~3400 requires ~29% equity. I only have ~6 clean outs to pair (≈12–14% to hit the river) and even if I hit I’m often losing to any A‑x, Q‑x or better pair. Elon opened wide preflop and has c‑bet/fired the turn, so his continuing range contains many value hands that won’t fold to one more street. Raising OOP as a bluff here is thin: fold equity is limited and I’m risking a large portion of stack with almost no showdown equity. With deep stacks and 14 hands left, preserving chips is correct. Fold.

Heads-up on the button (effectively the SB) with 8♣9♥ — a playable, connected hand with good postflop potential in position. Against one opponent I want initiative and to apply pressure, so open-raising is standard. I’ll raise to the minimum legal open (200 total = 2x BB) to: (1) steal the BB often, (2) play postflop in position with a hand that can hit connected flops, and (3) keep the pot manageable with deep stacks. If Elon 3-bets big I’ll likely fold; if he just defends I’ll c-bet or proceed based on the flop texture.

Heads-up with deep stacks (~200BB effective). 78o is a standard defend from BB vs SB min-raise/open. It plays well postflop with straight potential, and equity vs wide SB range (~50-60% range) is around 40-45%, plus implied odds. Folding too tight late tournament; 3betting offsuit connector risks domination without fold equity advantage over suited versions. Call keeps pot small, realizes equity.

Flop 9d-6s-5c gives me a clean open-ended straight draw (8 outs to the nuts, ~32% equity by river) plus minor backdoor flush draws. OOP vs SB min-raise range (wide: Ax, Kx, suited connectors, broadway), which includes many overpairs, top pairs, weaker draws, floats, and air that will bet frequently. Checking OOP with strong draw induces bets from her range, allowing check-raise for value/protection against draws or thin value, or check-call smaller bets to realize equity cheaply. Pot is small (400), stacks deep (~200BB), so implied odds are huge if I hit. Donk leading is suboptimal OOP as it polarizes my range, invites raises from her strong hands/draws, and check-folds from air; better to let her bet and control pot size. Late tournament heads-up, preserve stack while playing strong equity spots aggressively post-check.

I have top pair (9♥) with an 8 kicker on a fairly dry, low flop (9♦-6♠-5♣). Heads-up in position this is a clear value/protection spot: I want to get value from worse nines and charge any straight/overcard draws while keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks. Elon checked, so betting lets me build the pot on my terms and deny free cards. I’ll size to ~60% of the pot (250 chips) — large enough to charge draws and get value, but small enough to avoid committing with a vulnerable kicker. Plan: if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (barrel on blank/weak cards, check against heavy action or scary cards); if he check-raises big I’ll give credit and likely fold, while a small raise I can consider calling to realize equity (I have a gutshot to a 7).

Heads-up deep (~200BB), OOP on draw-heavy flop 9d6s5c with perfect OESD (8 outs, ~32% equity). Villain's SB min-raise range bets wide here (overpairs, Tx+/9x, draws like 78/47/34/A4, bluffs/floats). Small 250 bet (~half-pot) into 400 screams balanced range including weak hands/draws we crush and mediums we have equity/FV against. Pot odds excellent (250 into 650 for ~27% immediate, plus massive implied), but passive call keeps pot small and lets villain realize equity cheaply with overcards/draws. Check-raise exploits: semi-bluffs fold out worse equity (gutshots, floats), value from weak pairs/draws OOP villain overcalls with, builds pot OOP for when we hit (huge stacks), denies equity to runner-runner threats. Size to ~3x bet (750 total) balances vs her floats/caps range at sets/Tx. Late tourney, apply pressure to chip leader tendencies from history (Minni often calls small but folds to aggression). Folding/calling suboptimal with nuts potential.

I have top pair (9) with an 8 kicker on a fairly dry 9♦-6♠-5♣ flop. I c-bet 250 and Elon’s large check-raise to 750 is polarizing — it can show sets (66/55), two-pair/9x combos, or the rare made straight (7-8), but it can also be an aggressive bluff. With deep stacks and position I don’t want to fold a hand that is still often best, nor do I want to bloat the pot by three-betting with a vulnerable kicker. Calling 500 more (750 total committed) keeps worse 9s and many weaker hands/draws in, gives me reasonable pot odds (500 into a 1400 pot → need ~26% equity), and lets me re-evaluate on the turn. Plan: see the turn and proceed — if it’s a blank I’ll continue for value/protection; if Elon barrels heavy on a turn that connects with his strong-check-raise range (paired card or non-blank), I’ll give it up; if I hit the straight (a 7) I’ll look to extract maximum value.

Heads-up late in tournament (hand 137/150), deep effective stacks (~190BB). Flop check-raise with OESD (78 on 965) polarized range and got called by villain's SB min-raise range, which includes pairs (Tx/9x/65 etc.), weaker draws (74/87/JT floats), sets. Turn Td bricks my straight draw (still 8 outs to nuts: 4x/Jx ~32% equity to river) but completes diamond flush draw, improving some of her range (AdXd, 8d7d etc.). OOP first to act into 1900 pot: betting essential to deny equity to her remaining draws (diamond flush, gutshots like J7/87/74), extract value from capped range (overpairs, weak pairs/T-top without diamond), protect against runner-runner. Check risks free card to her outs or bet where calling OOP worse pot odds/implied reduced. Bet ~2/3 pot (1300) balances thin value (beats her calls/folds worse), semi-bluff potential vs floats; history shows Minni calls small but sensitive to aggression postflop. Builds pot for river if hit nuts, maximizes EV with huge implied odds.

I have top pair (9♥) with a vulnerable 8 kicker on a 9♦-6♠-5♣-T♦ board. Elon’s flop check‑raise was polarizing and weighted toward strong value hands (sets 66/55, made straights like 7‑8, and strong 9x) but also can include bluffs. His turn bet of 1,300 into the ~3,200 pot gives me ~22% required equity to call (1300/(3200+1300+1300) ≈ 22%). Folding is too passive — I still beat bluffs and many weaker 9s, and calling is a relatively small % of my stack. Raising is unattractive: my kicker is weak and a raise would commit a lot of chips with a hand that fares poorly against the most likely value hands in his line (sets/straights/strong 9s). I’ll call 1,300 to keep his bluffs and worse pairs in, realize equity (including the small straight outs), and use position to re‑evaluate on the river: fold to a large river shove or heavy value bet unless I improve or get a clear showdown/value spot if he checks.

River 3d completes diamond flushes (AdXd, KdXd etc. from her wide SB minraise range) and some gutshots (24o/s for 23456 straight), but primarily bricks my previous straight draw rep while keeping my 56789 straight very strong (beats all pairs/top pair/Tx/9x/65/54 two-pair/draws that called flop CR+turn, loses to nuts 6789T ~4 combos 67s/o/T7s/o minus suited overlaps, and any diamond flush). Heads-up late (137/150), deep ~170BB eff, pot 4500 OOP first. History: Minni calls stations small/medium bets postflop (e.g., hands 133 all-in calls, 78 river calls, 120 river calls) but folds to larger aggression sometimes (131 turn raise fold). My line (flop CR OESD semi-bluff/value, turn cont bet value/protect) polarizes to nuts/draws/value; check risks check-behind from her medium strength (Tx/9x no diamond) or bet/raise from flushes/nuts. Value bet extracts from worse (Tx/9x/sets without diamond/T7/67 that improve poorly), denies equity (implied none OOP), builds stack vs caller. Size ~2/3 pot (3000 total) balances thin value/semi-bluff vs her capped calling range (too small gets called by air/draws we crush, too big folds value); pot odds for her ~40% needs ~Tx+/draws/flushes she overcalls with per history. Maximizes EV long-term stack growth.

Elon’s line (check‑raise on the flop, turn bet, and another bet on the river) is heavily value‑oriented — sets (66/55), two‑pair (65/T9), made straights (7‑8) and strong 9x hands are exactly the sorts of hands that follow this sequence. The river 3♦ also completes some diamond two‑card hands, increasing the number of very strong holdings in his continuing range. I must call 3,000 to win a final pot of ~13,500, so I need about 22% equity. A lone pair of nines with an 8‑kicker is unlikely to have that frequency of equity against Elon’s polarized, value‑heavy line; his bluff frequency looks too low to justify a call, and raising would be committing with a hand that rarely gets folds from better hands and loses to the majority of his value range. Preserve the stack and fold.

Heads-up on the button/SB with deep stacks (225bb effective). Tc4c is a suited connector with good postflop playability, including straight and flush potential. Preflop from SB in heads-up, open wide to steal blinds or build pot with initiative. Standard raise sizing around 3x BB (300) given history of 300 opens. Postflop positional dynamics favor button despite acting first on flop if raised pre. Limping invites multiway pots OOP; raising is higher EV.

Heads‑up in the big blind with 9♣7♣ facing a button open to 300. Pot is 400 and I need 200 more to call (total 300). With deep stacks (~173bb) and the button opening very wide, 9‑7 suited has good multi‑street playability (flush + straight possibilities) and decent equity. Calling keeps the pot smaller and lets me realize equity postflop; 3‑betting OOP (min‑3bet to 500) would bloat the pot when I don’t have position and I don’t want to be forced off many flops. Pot odds are about 200 into 600 (≈33% break‑even), which is reasonable vs a wide opening range. Plan: see the flop, continue with strong draws/top pairs, float/raise selectively on favorable textures, and fold to heavy turn/river pressure when clearly behind. Therefore I will call the raise.

Flop: 8♣ K♦ 9♠ with 9♣7♣ in the big blind. I have a marginal made hand — a pair of 9s with a weak 7‑kicker and only a remote runner‑runner club draw. Elon was the preflop raiser and checked the flop; heads‑up raisers c‑bet very often, so his check more likely represents missed equity or pot‑control rather than a clear value monster. With deep stacks and OOP, I want to control the pot size with a hand that is easily outflanked by Kx, two‑pair, or sets and that doesn’t improve often. Betting here would either fold out a lot of hands I already beat (reducing my showdown value) or get called by hands that are ahead and can put me in tough spots on later streets. Plan: check and see a turn cheaply, call reasonable turn bets to realize equity, fold to severe aggression or re‑evaluate if I pick up improvement (9,7,club, or runner‑runner).

Heads-up deep stacks (170-220BB), OOP on flop with Tc4c after raising preflop. Flop 8cKd9s hits my suited connector somewhat: 9-outer club flush draw (Tc4c + 8c), live Ten overcard, and minor backdoor straight/flush potential. Board is coordinated (98 possible str8 draws) but king-high favors my initiative/range. Minni checks BB calling range (wide due to odds), likely weak/marginal; c-bet semi-bluff to deny equity, take pot ~50% time, or get called/raised by worse. Small sizing ~50-60% pot exploits check, maintains fold equity vs villain's history of folding flops (e.g., many checks then folds to bets). Check risks giving free card to OESD/flush draws or check-raise. Bet 350 balances value (overpairs/draws), bluffs.

I have 9♣7♣ on K♦ 9♠ 8♣: a made pair of nines with a weak 7‑kicker and only a remote runner‑runner club draw. Pot is 950, opponent bets 350; calling 350 (pot after call 1300) requires ≈27% equity to be profitable. As the preflop raiser Elon's c‑bet range is wide heads‑up, and my pair is ahead of many of his missed hands/air and has decent multi‑street equity, while still behind Kx, two‑pair and sets. With deep stacks and out of position, raising here would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and often get me in uncomfortable spots, so I prefer to keep the pot controllable and realize my equity. Action: call 350 and re‑evaluate on the turn — fold to strong turn aggression when clearly behind, or continue/raise only if I improve or the turn texture is favorable.

Board: 8♣ K♦ 9♠ 7♥. My hand 9♣7♣ = two pair (9s & 7s). Heads‑up this is a very strong holding — it beats most single‑pair hands and many bluffs, but it is behind obvious Kx two‑pair combos (K9/K7/K8) and behind sets/trips (99/77/88). Elon is the preflop raiser with a wide range and he bet the flop small; my call kept his weaker pairs and many draws in. On the turn I should extract thin value and deny a free river to the small number of runner/straight draws that could catch up. Betting also prices weaker 9x/A9/7x type hands into calling rather than giving them a free card. I don’t want to blo﻿at the pot OOP with a huge sizing or invite a big check‑raise, so I’ll bet around 1/2 pot to get called by worse and to fold some equity from hands that currently beat me. Plan: bet half pot (650). If Elon calls I’ll re‑evaluate river for value or pot control; if he check‑raises large I’ll give credit to a very strong value range and fold/re‑evaluate based on sizing and pot odds.

Heads-up deep stacks (~170bb effective) on turn after check-call flop. Board 8cKd9s7h is draw-heavy (club/spade flush draws, 5-6/10-J str8 draws now completing 56 str8). My Tc4c: nut club flush draw (9 outs ~36% equity), live T overcard, backdoor str8d. Her check-call flop then small lead 650 (~1/3 pot into 1950) reps wide: weak Kx (KJ/KT/K9), draw (clubs/spades/56/OESD), or slowplayed value (KQ/KQ sets unlikely). History: Minni calls light pre/post (e.g., Hands 101-137 often calls then folds to turns/rivers), vulnerable to aggression. Positional advantage, pot odds 1950:650 (~3:1) great for call, but raise semi-bluff maximizes EV: high fold equity vs thin value/draws (takes pot ~40% time), value from worse draws/pairs, builds for river jam if check-misses. Minraise 1300 weak; size to 2000 (~3x, ~75% pot) pressures, balances range. Avoids overplay but exploits her passive tendencies.

I have 9♣7♣ on 8♣ K♦ 9♠ 7♥ — two pair (9s & 7s). I bet a thin 1/2‑pot on the turn to extract value and to deny free rivers; Elon has check‑raised big to 2000. That line is value‑heavy (K9/K7/K8, sets 99/77/88, KK, and the occasional made straight), but it also includes some semi‑bluffs and overcalls from a wide HU preflop range. Pot is 3950 and I must put in 1350 more; calling requires ≈25% equity (1350 / (3950+1350) ≈ 25.5%). Two pair is a strong hand heads‑up and often has >25% equity versus a mixed check‑raise range, plus I have 4 immediate outs (two 9s, two 7s) to hit a full house on the river. Raising OOP would bloat the pot with a marginal holding and shove would be unnecessary given the small fraction of my stack at risk. I’ll call the 1350 (total turn contribution 2000), see the river, and re‑evaluate: fold to an extremely large river overbet if the card pairs K/8 or completes obvious straights and sizing indicates a value range; otherwise extract value or check‑control as appropriate.

River: 8c Kd 9s 7h 9h — my 9c7c is a full house (9s full of 7s). This is a very strong hand heads‑up; only a small subset of Elon’s turn check‑raise range (pocket KK and K9 combinations using the remaining 9) can beat me. Elon’s large turn check‑raise is value‑heavy, but his passive river check can represent either a showdown‑minded Kx or a very strong hand. Checking behind surrenders thin value against Kx and worse hands that will call; betting a modest size extracts that thin value while keeping the pot controlled versus the small number of hands that beat me. I’ll bet for value ~2,000 into ~5,300 to target top pairs/two‑pairs and get value from worse Kx, while still managing risk if Elon shows extreme strength.

River pairs the 9s, completing some straight draws (e.g., 10-J, 5-6-10 for straights missed but now board pairs). My Tc4c bricked nut club FD completely (no pair, straight, or flush). Zero showdown value; pure air. Her line: check-call flop (weak/marginal), small donk turn 650 (~30% pot), calls my 2000 raise (~3x), then donks 2000 river (~27% pot) into 5300+ turn action. This reps strength: trips (99, K9), two-pair (K9, 97s), or Kx that holds vs my range. Calling turn raise commits her somewhat (~1350 call OOP), unlikely pure bluff/draw here after improving board. History shows Minni calls light but folds to heavy aggression; however, persisting post-turn raise suggests value. Pot odds 2000 to win 9300 (~4.65:1, 17.7% needed) poor vs her capped range. No fold equity missed; preserve stack late tournament (138/150, ~12 hands left, 199bb deep but ICM pressure mounting). Fold best EV.

Heads-up on the button with JJ is a straightforward value-raise. Pot is 150 and calling 50 is passive; I want to build the pot and isolate Elon’s wide BB calling range while preserving positional advantage postflop. With deep stacks (~200bb) I’ll size to 3x the BB to extract value and keep weaker hands and suited connectors in. Plan: if Elon calls I’ll play postflop in position; if he 3-bets small-to-medium I’ll continue/call to realize equity, but will respect a very large 3-bet and fold/re-evaluate to avoid getting into a dominated coinflip. Raise to 300 total.

Preflop in BB facing a button min-raise to 300 (additional 250 from SB post). Pot is 400, calling 200 more for total pot of 600. 9h5h is a suited connector with good playability, straight and flush potential. Against a wide button opening range, it has around 40%+ equity heads-up and realizes well postflop despite OOP. Pot odds are excellent (2:1 direct, plus implied). Folding is too tight in BB defense strategy; 3-betting is suboptimal as it plays poorly postflop and lacks fold equity value. Calling to see flop and play in position postflop dynamically, leveraging deep stacks (198x BB) for implied odds.

Flop is Js Ks 9d, giving me middle pair with 9h5h (pair of 9s, weak 5 kicker, backdoor heart flush draw). Board is coordinated with broadway straight draws (Q10), spade flush draw possible, and overcards in villain's wide button range. OOP as BB, pot 600, deep stacks. Vs Minni's aggressive button min-raise range (wide: pairs, suited connectors, broadway, Ax suited), I have ~35-40% equity but vulnerable to domination (Kx, Jx, higher pairs), sets, etc. Betting risks getting raised off equity or called by better; thin value unlikely to get called only by worse. Checking keeps pot small, induces bluffs from missed draws/overcards (frequent in button range), realizes equity on turns that improve (hearts, runner-runner straights), and allows pot control. Plan: check-call light if bet small, check-fold to large bets. Long-term EV positive in deep HU spot by playing postflop dynamically.

I flopped trips — Jc Jd in hand with Js on board — so this is a very strong, straightforward value situation heads-up. Elon checked, so I should bet to build the pot and charge Kx/9x/pocket pairs and any backdoor draws; the only common flop combos that currently beat me are Q10 straights, which are relatively rare. I choose a ~2/3-pot bet (400 into a 600 pot) to get called by worse hands while still pricing in draws. Plan: if he calls I continue extracting on later streets while watching for Q/T runouts; if he check-raises small I’ll strongly consider reraising for value, and if he turns up unusually large aggression (size > pot) I’ll re-evaluate for the Q10 combos but will often call a single raise to see more cards given how few hands beat me here.

Faced with a 400 bet into 1000 pot (~40% pot) on Js Ks 9d board with 9h5h (middle pair, weak kicker, backdoor FH draw). Pot odds are ~2.5:1 direct, excellent with deep stacks (~195 BB effective) for implied odds on turns improving to two pair, trips, or runner-runner straight/flush. Equity vs Minni's button c-bet range (wide: Kx/Jx value, QTs/Q10s/T10s draws, missed Ax/bluffs) is ~40%, often ahead of draws/bluffs and live vs dominated pairs. Raising risks isolation by better hands (KJ, KT, sets) or fold equity loss OOP; folding too tight given playability and history of Minni's aggression (frequent bets from prior hands, e.g., #129-138 shows continued probe-betting). Calling keeps pot controlled OOP, induces further bluffs on blank turns, realizes equity dynamically. Plan: call, evaluate turn (fold to aggression if missed, bet/call selectively if improves/misses her range). Aligns with deep HU strategy to play small pots with marginal made hands.
