Turn 2s completes the spade flush draw (Ks Js 2s) and is somewhat blank otherwise, adding action potential. Holding 9h5h for middle pair (third pair on board) with a terrible 5 kicker, no straight/flush draw. OOP first to act into 1400 pot vs aggressive button Minni (wide preflop range: broadways, suited connectors, Ax/spades, pairs; flop c-bet range polarized with value Kx/Jx/sets and bluffs/draws). Equity ~35-45% vs her range but vulnerable to domination (K9, J9, 99, KT/KQ straights possible), flushes, better pairs. Deep stacks (~190BB) but tournament late (139/150), no ICM pressure yet. Betting thin value risks calls/raises from superior hands/draws, poor fold equity on scary board. Check preserves pot control, induces bluffs from missed Q10/T10 draws/A-high/overcards (common in range), allows cheap realization of showdown value or selective calls to small bets, folds to aggression from value. Consistent with prior pot control strategy OOP with marginal made hand; dynamic postflop play maximizes EV long-term.

I have trips (Jc Jd) on Js Ks 9d 2s — a very strong value hand heads-up, but the turn brought a third spade, so two-spade hands now make a flush and Q‑10 straights already beat me. Elon checked to me, so I should both extract value from Kx/9x/pocket pairs that called the flop and protect against giving a free river to spade combos. Betting ~1000 (≈70% of the 1400 pot) accomplishes that: it charges one-pair hands and thinner calls while making it costly for flush draws and denying a free spade. If he calls I’ll re-evaluate the river for further value; if he check-raises very large (pot+), I’ll heavily discount my hand and likely fold/readjust since that size strongly represents a made flush/straight; against a small check-raise I’ll call and proceed to showdown.

Facing a 1000 bet into 2400 pot on a scary turn 2s completing the spade flush draw (board: Js Ks 9d 2s) with 9h5h for middle pair, weak kicker, no draws. OOP after pot-controlling flop and turn check. Minni's button range includes flushes (spades like QsTs, JsTs, etc.), straights (KT, KQ), sets/two pairs, dominating pairs (Kx, Jx), while bluffs less likely on paired flush-completing turn after my check. Equity <30% vs polarized turn betting range (value-heavy), poor vs value, minimal fold equity if raise OOP deep. Pot odds ~2.4:1 require ~29%, but implied odds limited by vulnerability (easy to stack-off by better). History shows Minni's aggression, but folding marginal showdown value preserves stack late tournament (139/150, deep HU), aligning with prior pot control strategy to avoid tough rivers OOP. Preserve for premium spots to maximize stack.

Pocket Kings is one of the strongest starting hands in heads-up play, especially on the button/SB with position post-flop. With deep stacks (around 190BB effective) and only 10 hands left in the tournament, we want to build the pot for value while protecting against dominated hands like Ax. Minni defends the BB wide based on history, often calling raises. A standard raise to 3BB (300 total) balances aggression, induces calls from worse, and sets up post-flop play. Limping risks multiway disguised strength; folding is insane. Min-raise too small, invites action.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with T♠6♦. Pot is 400 and I must add 200 (total committed will be 300). Calling 200 into a 400 pot gives me ~33% break-even equity, which is reasonable heads‑up against a wide button opening range. T6o has decent playability heads‑up (chance to hit top pair, two pair, straights) and the stacks are deep, so preserving chips and seeing a flop is preferable to bloating the pot OOP with a light 3‑bet. I’ll call to see a flop and plan to fold to heavy aggression on dry boards where I miss, or extract value/lead if I connect.

Flop: Kc Qc 8h. I hold T♠6♦ — no pair, no club, only a remote backdoor straight. Opponent opened preflop from the button and will have a lot of Kx/Qx, broadways and club combos that connect with this board or will c-bet frequently. With essentially no showdown equity or meaningful blockers, betting here as a bluff OOP is thin and will often get called or raise‑punished. With deep stacks and a relatively small pot (600) it's best to keep the pot under control and see a turn. Plan: check and fold to any sizable c-bet (rough threshold ≈ >1/2 pot ≈ 300). I may consider calling a very small probe (≤~200) to try and realize equity, but otherwise only continue if I pair or improve on the turn or get a clear checking opportunity from the button.

Flop Qc8hKc hits me perfectly for top set with KK, the stone nuts against most of villain's calling range from BB. Board has two clubs, so flush draws possible (e.g., AcXc), and some straight draws like JT or J9, but my hand crushes overpairs, two-pair combos, and weaker Kx. Minni's check suggests weakness or trapping, but history shows she checks often with medium strength/draws. With deep stacks (~190BB), 10 hands left, and pot of 600, a 2/3 pot bet (400) builds the pot for value while charging draws heavily. Checking risks giving free cards to flush/straight draws or letting her realize equity. Overbetting risks fold equity from worse; too small invites cheap calls. Standard value bet extracts from Qx, 88, 88+, flush draws, and induces floats from air.

Flop is Kc Qc 8h and I’m holding T♠6♦ — no pair, no club, only backdoor possibilities. Pot is 1000 and Elon bets 400; calling 400 would be risking 400 to win 1400, so I need ~28.6% equity to break even. My realistic equity (6 outs to pair plus small backdoor straight/runner combos) is in the low‑20s and is likely worse versus a button opening/c-bet range full of Kx/Qx/broadways and club draws that dominate or have fold equity. The bet (400) is larger than my probe threshold (~300) and with deep stacks there’s no reason to turn this into a multistreet bluff or float OOP with such a weak hand. Fold and wait for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button (also posted the SB) with Td2c — not a strong hand, but in heads-up I should open a very wide range to seize initiative and avoid playing out of position. Pot is 150, to call 50 and min-raise is 200. I’ll raise to 300 (3 BB) — a standard, constructive size that applies pressure, is consistent with my prior opens, and gives me position postflop. Plan: fold to a sizable 3-bet (I’m not continuing with T2o vs a bulk 3-bet), but if Elon just calls I’ll c-bet dry flops, look to take the pot down, and only invest further if I improve or get good equity. With deeper stacks I keep aggression but avoid bloating the pot with a weak holding.

7c2d is the worst possible starting hand in Hold'em. Facing a standard raise from the button/SB, there is no reason to defend the big blind here. Equity against any reasonable range is abysmal, and being out of position post-flop makes it even worse. Pot odds are 200 to call into 400 (3:1), requiring ~25% equity, but 72o has far less against a typical button opening range. With deep stacks ( ~194 BB effective) and tournament near end (141/150), preserve stack for better spots. Minni has been aggressive historically, but folding trash is standard GTO play.

Heads-up on the button/SB with Qh6d preflop. Pot 150, dead money from blinds. Q6o plays well heads-up: decent high card, live cards, position advantage postflop. Standard to raise 3x BB (300 total) to steal blinds or play profitably if called. Minni calls frequently from history, but we have range/position edge. Deep stacks (193 BB effective), but near end of tournament (142/150), aggression maximizes EV by denying equity/stealing. Limp too passive, fold too tight.

Heads-up vs a SB open to 300: his range is wide, but I’m out of position and stacks are deep. Qc7h is a marginal but defendable hand heads-up — it has decent equity vs a wide opening range and I’m getting correct pot odds to call (need ~33% equity: call 200 to win a 400 pot). I don’t want to turn this into a large multi-street contest with a hand that’s often dominated, so a small 3-bet bluff is unnecessary. Plan: call to see a flop; if I hit a Queen or good draw I can continue for value/bluffs, otherwise play cautiously and fold to heavy action. Therefore call (commit 300 total, i.e. add 200).

Heads-up on a dry, rainbow 10-6-4 flop and I’m first to act OOP with Qc7h. I have only 6 outs (3 queens + 3 sevens) — roughly ~24% to make a pair by showdown — so this is a marginal hand and not one I want to build a big pot with out of position. Checking keeps the pot small, lets me see how Elon proceeds, and preserves fold equity on later streets if I pick up a queen or a favorable card. Plan: check. If Elon fires a very small c-bet (≈≤200 into the 600 pot) I can call to realize equity vs his wide range; fold to medium/large bets (≈300+), which give me poor pot odds and usually represent enough value that I’m dominated. If he checks back I can continue on the turn only if I improve or get a favorable texture.

Heads-up on flop with middle pair (6d pairs the 6s) and queen kicker after raising preflop and getting checked to. Board is 4d 6s Tc: drawy with diamond flush draw, possible straight draws (e.g., 78 for open-ender, J9/J8 gutshots), but my range hits this well (Tx, 6x, Ax broadway). Minni's check often weak or medium strength given history of calling stations but folding to aggression postflop. Pot 600, deep effective stacks (~190BB), but nearing end (142/150 hands), so value bet to protect against draws, charge, and build pot while denying equity. Position advantage allows control. 2/3 pot bet (400) balances value vs bluffs in range, exploits Minni's calling tendencies without overbetting.

Checked the dry, rainbow 10-6-4 flop OOP with Qc7h. Elon c-bets 400 into a 1000 pot; calling 400 would risk 400 to win 1400 (break-even equity ≈28.6%). I only have ~6 clean outs (~24% to pair by river) and no meaningful backdoor draws, and I’m out of position — this is a marginal hand that doesn’t deserve multi-street investment. Against a wide SB range many continuing hands (T+x, pairs, overcards) dominate me when I do improve. With deep stacks I’ll wait for a better, more profitable spot. A smaller c-bet (≤~200) I’d call to realize equity, but 400 is above my threshold, so fold.

I have 3♠7♥ — a very weak offsuit hand with poor showdown value. Preflop the cost to see a flop is only 50 into a 150 pot (pot odds ~4:1), and in heads‑up I’ll have positional advantage postflop. Raising to 200 with this holding risks putting unnecessary chips in and is easily dominated or crushed by a 3‑bet. I’ll call the additional 50 (100 total committed) to see the flop, fold to significant preflop aggression (a 3‑bet), and proceed cautiously — using position to fold or bluff as appropriate on later streets.

Preflop in BB facing a completion/limp from SB/Button (Minni), who plays aggressively but limps wide here. My Q9o is a strong defending hand with queen-high, straight potential, and good playability. Deep stacks (19.6k effective). Raising takes initiative, denies equity to her wide limping range, builds the pot when ahead, and I can play postflop profitably if called. Standard sizing ~3x pot or 3BB. Min raise 200 too small; raise to 300 to apply pressure.

3♠7♥ is a very weak offsuit hand, but heads‑up on the button I should defend a wide range. The pot is 400 and I must call 200 to match Elon’s 300 — calling to 300 total gives me good pot odds (~33% break‑even) and preserves positional advantage postflop. Raising here with junk just bloats the pot and gets me in tough spots versus 3‑bets or stronger hands; folding is too tight given the price. Plan: call to see the flop, play cautiously — check and fold to heavy action if I miss, look to c‑bet small as a bluff on favorable flops, and apply pressure or extract value only if I improve.

Flop is 8h-8c-5h, a paired board with a heart flush draw. As preflop raiser OOP with position on a wide limping/calling range from SB, my Q9o has no pair or strong draw but queen-high overcard and backdoor potential. Board hits some of villain's range (pocket pairs, 65s, hearts like A5h/KJh suited, 87s) but whiffs many overcards, Ax, broadway. Standard c-bet to deny equity to floats and weak Ax, maintain initiative, and pot control OOP. Villain calls preflop wide from SB; history shows she calls flops often but folds to continued aggression when missed. Pot 600, bet 2/3 pot (400) balances value (trips, sets rare) and bluffs, good sizing for deep stacks (19k eff). Checking risks giving free cards to draws/improvers and loses value from folds.

I have 3♠7♥ on a flop of 8♥-8♣-5♥: no pair and only a backdoor heart possibility (need both turn and river hearts — very unlikely). Elon has bet 400 into a 1000 pot; calling 400 to win 1400 means my break-even equity is ~28.6% (400 / (1000+400)). Versus a wide heads‑up c‑bet range I beat some bluffs and overcards, and Elon has shown a tendency to c‑bet and pressure thinly, so folding outright is too tight. Raising with this junk would bloat the pot and put me in a tough spot vs a value or a 3‑bet — not profitable. With position I can call, keep his bluffs in the pot, and fold to significant turn aggression unless I pair or pick up a realistic draw. Plan: call the 400 now, re-evaluate on the turn (fold to heavy action if I miss; continue only if I improve or get a good bluffing spot).

Turn brings the Jh, completing the heart flush draw (now 8h,5h,Jh) and adding straight draws like 9-10, 10-Q, improving pairs with J (KJ,QJ,TJ,AJ). Board: 8h-8c-5h-Jh is extremely villain-favorable for her SB limp-call range, which includes many hearts (A5h,KJh,87h suited), 65s-type straights, pocket pairs 66-77, and broadway like KQ,KJ. My Qs9c is queen-high (Qs blocks QJ slightly), no pair, no made draw, no gutshot—pure air with minimal showdown value OOP. Preflop raise + flop c-bet reps strength (sets, two-pair, 88, A8, 85), but her call suggests she has hit enough (medium pairs, draws) and turn likely improved floats/draws. Pot 1400, ~19k effective stacks late in tournament (hand 143/150). Betting risks: thin bluff OOP vs aggressive villain who calls stations flops/streets per history (e.g., hands 14,20,35,66,73+), gets raised by monsters/draws; low fold equity on card that hits range. Value betting impossible. Checking preserves stack, induces bluffs/misses from Ax/overcards, allows pot control OOP; if she bets small, can fold queen-high equity easily loses; if large, fold. Cannot profitably call big bets. Checking maximizes EV long-term by avoiding spew deep.

Board: 8♥ 8♣ 5♥ J♥ — my hand 3♠7♥ has no pair but I just picked up a 4‑card heart draw (7♥ + 8♥,5♥,J♥) = 9 outs to a flush (9/46 ≈ 19.6% to hit the river). Elon checked the turn after c‑betting the flop, which often indicates a marginal/pot‑control holding or a missed hand — a good spot to semi‑bluff. Betting gives me fold equity against hands like single overcards, weak Jx or small pairs, and still preserves decent equity if called. I don’t want to bloat the pot with a naked draw, so I size to about half the pot: 700 into 1400. If he folds I take the pot now; if he calls I still have ~20% to make my hand and can re‑evaluate the river (call small river bets when I hit, fold to big river shoves when I miss). Stack sizes (≈19–20k) allow a controlled semi‑bluff here, and with heads‑up dynamics this is the best way to extract immediate value or realize equity in position.

Turn board 8h-8c-5h-Jh is highly favorable to villain's SB limp-call range vs my preflop raise + flop c-bet, which reps Ax, 88/85/55/88, pairs. Her flop call likely medium strength (8x, Jx possible, hearts like A5h/KJh/76h suited, 66-77). Jh completes flushes, improves draws/pairs (KJ/QJ/TJ now pairs), adds straight outs (e.g., 67, 910). Half-pot bet 700 into 2100 after my check suggests value/thin value from hit hands (Jx+, 8x); bluffs unlikely post-check as she controls bet sizing. Qs9c is pure queen-high air, blocks QJ slightly but no pair/draw/gutshot/SDV. Pot odds ~3:1 require ~23% equity, but vs her range (88/8x/Jx/flush/sets/draws) queen-high has <15% equity OOP. Calling builds pot OOP for river where I'm crushed; raising spewy with no leverage (19k stacks). History shows villain calls stations but folds aggression on scary boards (e.g., 14,35,66,72). Late tournament (143/150), preserve stack, fold equity irrelevant. Direct fold maximizes EV.

73o is one of the worst possible starting hands with very low equity against any calling range. Heads-up on the button/SB, I act first pre-flop, gaining no positional advantage. Pot odds to call 50 into 150 are decent (~3:1), but hand has poor playability post-flop with no suited connectors or high cards. Late in tournament (hand 144/150), stacks are deep (~190BB), better to preserve stack and wait for a premium hand or stealing opportunity rather than defend blinds with trash. Folding is standard and GTO-correct here to maximize EV long-term.

Heads-up on the button with A5s is a very strong opening hand — good blocker to Aces, nut-flush potential and strong postflop playability. I want to take the initiative, build the pot and get fold equity, so I’ll open-raise rather than just call the extra 50. A standard heads-up sizing around 2.5–3x the big blind is appropriate and still leaves plenty of stack depth for postflop play. I raise to 300 total to isolate the BB, allow frequent continuation bets on favorable flops, and put pressure on Elon. If he 3-bets large I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold or proceed cautiously depending on sizing); vs a small/medium 3-bet I can call to play postflop with position and nut-flush potential.

Hand 145 of 150, heads-up vs Minni on the button who raised to 300 into a 400 pot. My hand is 7h3c, a complete trash hand with no high card value, no suitedness, no straight potential, and terrible post-flop playability. Facing a button raise, which is wide, but 73o has negligible equity against any reasonable calling range and can't profitably realize equity heads-up. Calling 200 (less than pot odds offer, but hand is unplayable) risks stack late in tournament without upside. Folding preserves chips for better spots in final hands.

Heads-up pre-flop from SB/button with J3s. Suited jack-high hand with good playability for straights and flushes. Deep stacks (188 BB effective). Standard to raise wide in this spot to steal blinds or play post-flop in position effectively, as villain defends BB wide based on history of frequent calls and small raises. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) applies pressure and builds pot OOP for villain.

Elon (button / SB) opened to 300. I’m in the big blind with As8c and have 21050 (deep stacks). Calling the extra 200 (300 total committed) is the standard defend here: As8o is a strong heads‑up hand vs a wide SB opening range, and the pot odds are attractive (call 200 to win ~400 → ~2:1). A thin 3‑bet (min raise to 500) OOP is unnecessary with a medium ace and a middling kicker — I’d rather realize equity and play postflop. Plan: call to see the flop and proceed aggressively if I hit an Ace or strong draw; control the pot or fold to heavy aggression on dry boards.

Flop Q♠ J♥ 9♥ — I have A♠8♣: just ace‑high, no pair, no meaningful draw. Preflop raiser’s range is very wide heads‑up and this board hits a large chunk of it (Qx, Jx, 9x, broadways, connectors, some two‑card heart combos). Because the raiser checked to me, this could be either a missed hand trying to control the pot or an inducement/check‑raise — either way I don’t want to build a big pot OOP with essentially air. Checking preserves my showdown equity, keeps the pot small, and lets me realise equity against bluffs or worse Ax hands. Plan: check. If Elon checks back we see the turn for free. If he bets a small sizing on the turn I’ll generally call to keep pot control and use my Ace to call down; if he fires big (large sizing or a check‑raise line) I’ll fold — Minni’s hand has poor equity vs value ranges. If the turn pairs my Ace I’ll start betting for value.

Heads-up on the button vs BB with Js3s on Qs Jh 9h flop. I flopped second pair (Jacks) with a terrible kicker (3), plus a backdoor spade flush draw (holding three spades total). The board is draw-heavy with two hearts (flush draw possible) and straight draws (e.g., KT for broadway, T8 for open-ender). Minni checked, which is standard but could represent weakness or trapping with top pair (Qx). Deep stacks (~185 BB effective), pot 600. Checking risks giving free cards to draws that beat me (hearts, straights) or allowing bluffs. Betting protects my hand, gets value from worse pairs/draws (e.g., Ax, flush draws, gutshots), and denies equity. Minni's history shows frequent calls to flops/turns even with marginal hands (e.g., Hands 101-145 frequent small calls), so a pot-sized or 2/3-pot bet (400) balances value/protection while keeping fold equity against strong hands. Avoid overbetting OOP defense range. This spot maximizes EV long-term by charging draws and building pot with showdown value.

Flop: Q♠ J♥ 9♥. I have A♠8♣ — just ace‑high, no hearts and no straight draw. Elon (the preflop raiser) c‑bet a relatively small 400 into a 1000 pot (~40% pot) so his range is wide and contains plenty of marginal hands and bluffs as well as value (Qx, Jx, some broadways). Pot math: calling 400 now makes the pot ~1800, so my break‑even equity is ~22.2%; the raw chance to pair an Ace by the river is only ~12.5%, so calling purely for the draw isn’t justified. However, my Ace has decent showdown value heads‑up and blocks many A‑x combos; calling keeps the pot manageable out of position and lets me realize equity and float against Elon's small c‑bet. Raising OOP without a pair or a draw would be reckless; folding surrenders too much versus a wide SB bet. Plan: call the 400 to see a turn, fold to significant aggression (large raise or heavy turn barrel), call small turn bets and extract value if I pair my Ace on later streets.

Elon checked the turn after a small flop c‑bet; I have A♠8♣ — only ace‑high, no flush/straight draw. Betting OOP here would be a pure bluff against a wide but largely pair‑heavy continuing range (Qx/Jx/Kx); many of those hands will call a small bet and put me in a bloated pot with second‑best hand. Checking preserves my showdown equity, keeps the pot small, and lets me see the river for free (or induce a bluff). Plan: check to the river; call small/medium river bets where my A‑high still has decent equity against missed broadways and bluffs, but fold to large overbets/shoves that represent strong made hands. This follows my earlier plan to control the pot out of position with just an Ace and realize equity against a wide SB range.

Heads-up on button vs BB with Js3s on Qs Jh 9h Kc turn board (pot 1400, stacks ~18k/20k effective, hand 146/150). Flopped pair of Js (second pair then), backdoor spade draw; called my flop c-bet 400 (check-call OOP). Turn Kc completes broadway straights (if villain had T), improves Kx or QK to two pair/top pair, but blanks most draws (hearts/spades unchanged, clubs new but low equity). Villain checks again OOP, capping range somewhat (monsters often bet/don't check twice), likely Qx/draws/floats/worse pairs. My hand has showdown value vs her wide calling range (history: frequent check-calls small/medium bets even marginal/weak, e.g., recent hands 124-145), blocks Js. Deep stacks near end, max stack goal favors thin value + protection vs river villains (e.g., heart/spade/club draws, Ax gutshots). Check risks free card to improvers/bluffs, lower EV vs station; bet induces calls from worse (Qx weak kicker/Jx/A-high draws) without folding out equity. Half-pot bet ~700 balances value/protection/deny, exploits calliness without overbetting vulnerable hand.

Elon has bet 800 into a 1400 pot (pot shows 2200). Calling 800 needs ~26.7% equity (800 / (2200+800)). I have A♠8♣ — just ace‑high, but it blocks a lot of A‑x value combos (AK/AQ/AJ) and retains decent showdown value heads‑up. Elon’s line (small flop c‑bet then a turn barrel on the K) represents a wide but value‑heavy range (Kx, Qx, Jx, some T/straight combos and occasional bluffs). Raising OOP with no pair or draw is exploitable and would bloat a pot where I’m usually behind; folding 800 is too nitty given the small cost relative to my stack. I’ll call the 800 to keep the pot under control, realize equity, and re‑evaluate on the river — fold to a clear shove/very large overbet, call modest river bets, and extract value if I pair my Ace.

Board: Q♠ J♥ 9♥ K♣ 2♠. I hold A♠8♣ — only ace‑high. Elon was the preflop aggressor and barreled flop and turn; I called both to realize equity and because my Ace blocks a lot of Ax value combinations. He now checks the river. That line is consistent with pot‑control/value hands (Kx/Qx/Jx), turned straights (T), or an attempt to induce a bluff. Betting OOP with no pair or a meaningful draw is a thin bluff here — it will get called by many of the hands I beat rarely folds the right hands, and it bloats a pot where I’m usually behind. Checking preserves my showdown equity, keeps the pot small, and forces Elon to either show bluffs or value. If he bets, I’ll call small/medium bets (good pot odds and I block some Ax) and fold to large overbets/shoves. Therefore the optimal play is to check to showdown.

River heads-up on button with Js3s vs BB Minni on Qs Jh 9h Kc 2s board (pot 3000, effective stacks ~17k). Hand is second pair Js (blocks top pair Js), terrible 3 kicker, backdoor spade draw completed minimally (board now three spades? Wait, Qs/2s + my two = but irrelevant). Villain check-called flop 400 (draw-heavy board) and turn 800 (Kc brings Kx/Tx broadway draws), checks third time OOP. Her range: Qx (weak kickers like Q9/QT), worse Jx (AJ/KJ), 9x sets unlikely, missed heart draws (AhXh/Th8h), floats/A-high. Very few straights (requires precise preflop calls like T10 rare OOP), no flush hits likely (hearts need 3h, spades unlikely double-suited call pre). History shows Minni extremely sticky OOP caller of small-medium bets even marginal/weak (e.g., Hands 124-145: multiple check-call streets with air/draws/secondaries, rarely folds small). Tournament endgame (146/150), max stack favors thin value over checkdown/check-fold. Second pair has showdown vs worse (non-Q Jx/draws bluffs), blocks her J-calling hands. Half-pot bet 1500 (~50% pot) exploits station tendency: value from Qx/Jx worse/A-high/draws, fold equity vs pure air, protection vs unlikely villainous rivers. Avoids overbet (vulnerable to raises from QK/AQ/sets), balances occasional bluffs. High EV play to build stack.

Pot is 4500 and Elon bets 1500 on the river. Calling 1500 requires ~25% equity (1500 / (4500+1500)). I hold A♠8♣ — only ace‑high — but I intentionally checked to the river to preserve showdown equity and to call small/medium probes. Elon’s line (HU preflop raise, small flop c‑bet, turn barrel on the K, small river bet) is consistent with a wide continuing range: strong one‑pair hands (Qx/Jx/Kx), some straights (Tx), two‑pair/sets, and a nontrivial number of missed broadway/air hands used as bluffs. The bet is a relatively modest sizing, so his bluff frequency should be meaningful and my Ace removes/block some Ax value combos, increasing the relative frequency of bluffs in his range. Raising OOP with no pair is reckless and folding is too nitpicky given the pot odds and my blocker. I’ll call the 1500 to realize equity and to catch bluffs — fold only to a polarizing large shove or an overbet that clearly represents value.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with K♦6♠ and deep stacks (~180bb). In heads-up play you should open very wide from the button — K6o is more than playable. Raising gives fold equity, defines the pot, and preserves position postflop. The minimum raise is 200, but I prefer a standard 3BB open to avoid looking too weak and to get better isolation (300 total). If Elon 3-bets large I’ll frequently fold K6o; vs a call or small 3-bet I can play postflop aggressively with position. Calling 50 is too passive and misses value/fold equity. Action: raise to 300 total.

Heads-up near the end of the tournament with deep stacks (~180BB effective). Minni's button raise is very wide, likely 40-50%+ of hands based on history of frequent opens and calls. Qs4h is a queen-high hand with decent playability, straight potential, and queen blocker against her range. Pot odds are favorable (200 to call into 400, getting 3:1 direct + implied). Defending wide in BB vs BTN is standard to realize equity multiway in position post-flop (acting second). Folding is too tight; raising not ideal with marginal hand and her aggression. Calling to see flop and play post-flop dynamics.

Heads-up on the flop with a monotone spade board (Ts9s3s). Holding Qs4h gives me the Q-high flush draw (second-nut draw, blocking Qs), approximately 9 clean outs to the flush (~35% equity to hit by river) plus showdown value with queen-high. OOP first to act into a 600-chip pot, deep effective stacks (~180BB). Minni's button opening range is wide (40-50%+ from history), including many spades, Ax, broadways, suited connectors. This board hits her range hard but she cbets frequently post-flop. Betting OOP risks folding out worse draws/A-high/spades while giving her fold equity with position; check preserves pot control, induces bets from her polarized range (value like sets/Axs or bluffs/draws), sets up check-raise for value/thin value or max fold equity vs. her floats. If she checks behind (possible with weak made hands/draws fearing aggression), we retain massive equity cheaply. Optimal line: check to realize equity and exploit her aggression.

Check. I completely missed this flop with K♦6♠ — no pair and only one spade on a T♠9♠3♠ board. The flop is very wet and heavily favors a wide portion of Elon’s calling range (any two spades is an immediate made flush, many single-spade hands still have strong equity, and T/9 pairs or broadway combos are common). With a pot of 600 and deep stacks (SPR ≈ 30) I don’t want to bloating the pot with essentially air. Checking controls the pot, lets me reevaluate on the turn (I have ~12–13% to pair the K by the river), and preserves the ability to call a small probe or fold to real strength. If Elon bets small I’ll consider a call to see the turn; if he fires big or check‑raises I’ll fold.

Turn 6d is a relatively blank card that doesn't complete the nut flush, major straight draws, or help many weak hands significantly, preserving my equity advantage with the Qs flush draw (9 outs, ~19% to hit flush on river) plus queen-high showdown value. Minni's flop check behind on the monotone spade flop caps her range somewhat: unlikely nutted hands (they often c-bet for value/protection), more likely weaker spades, A-high, pairs, gutshots, or air from her wide button opening range. OOP first to act into 600-chip pot with deep effective stacks (~180BB). Betting applies pressure: gets thin value from worse made hands/draws (e.g., small pairs, Axs, Kxs, J8s-type gutshots), fold equity against floats/air that fear continuation of my perceived range strength on scary board, and denies her equity on rivers that improve her. Checking risks giving a free card to her draws improving to beat me (e.g., any spade hits her flush better than mine potentially, or runner-runner straights), and while inducing river bluffs is possible, her history shows passive lines sometimes but also trapping; proactive betting exploits her capped range better near tournament end to build stack. Smallish sizing (2/3 pot) gets called wide by her checking range while maintaining fold equity. Balanced with occasional check-backs.

I have K♦6♠ on T♠9♠3♠6♦ — that’s a pair of sixes plus a live spade draw (3 spades on board + my 6♠ = one-card flush draw). Elon checked the flop and now made a small turn bet (400) — a probing/delayed c-bet that widens his range (misses, medium pairs, some bluffs, and some slowplayed/paired hands). Pot is ~1000, call is 400 — I’m getting ~28.6% pot odds (400/1400). I have ~11 clean outs to significantly improve on the river (9 spades + two remaining sixes), and I also have current showdown value against a lot of his missed hands. Raising here would bloat the pot and expose me to a reraise or surrendered equity versus made spade hands; folding would be too tight with this combo draw. Optimal line is to call 400, realize equity, and re-evaluate on the river (value-bet or raise when I improve; proceed cautiously if a higher spade appears or he shows heavy action).

River 6h pairs the sixes but brings no spade, completing my Q-high spade flush (Qs with Ts9s3s). This is a very strong hand heads-up near the tournament end, likely the best in most of Minni's capped range given her flop check-back on the monotone spade board (disqualifying many nut flushes/setmines which often c-bet) and turn call of my 400 bet (which targeted her medium strength/equity hands). Her button opening is wide (~40-50% from history), including many spades, but Qs blocks her Qx flush; she can have weaker flushes (Jx, Tx, etc.), two pair, trips 6s (unlikely), or bluff-catchers like A-high no spade. Pot 1400 with ~17k effective, OOP first to act: value betting extracts from her calling station tendencies (frequent calls post-flop in history). Sizing ~2/3 pot (900) gets called wide by worse while denying her cheap showdown and building stack before final hands. Checking risks her checking back worse or small bet jamming all-in overreach; proactive value line maximizes EV.

I have K♦6♠ and the river is T♠ 9♠ 3♠ 6♦ 6♥ — that gives me trip sixes (6♠ + 6♦ + 6♥) with a King kicker. Heads‑up this is a very strong hand. The only realistic hands that beat me on this river are full houses (pocket TT/99/33 or combos with the lone remaining 6♣ plus a paired board card) and any two‑spade hole cards that already had a flush on the flop. Quads are essentially impossible given card distribution. Elon’s line (call preflop, check flop, small turn bet, small river raise) is consistent with both medium value hands and bluffs as well as the occasional flush or full house, but the frequency of hands that actually beat trips is relatively small. Pot is 2300 and his river bet is 900, so calling 900 gives me ~28% pot odds. My trips are ahead of Elon's range by a wide margin and calling is the lowest‑variance, +EV play here: I realize my showdown equity and avoid bloating the pot unnecessarily (a raise could induce a shove from the small subset of hands that beat me). Action: call 900.

Heads-up on the button (SB) preflop with 7c4s. This is a playable hand in a wide heads-up opening range due to position, fold equity on the blinds, and some postflop potential despite being offsuit low cards. Opponent history shows frequent calling and aggression, but stealing the 150 pot preflop has high EV. Raising small to 3x BB standardizes pressure late in tournament with deep stacks (~200BB). Folding too tight misses value; limping invites trouble. Raise to build pot or take it down.

Facing Elon's open to 300 from the button while I'm in the big blind with 9♦3♥. The pot is 400 and I must call 200 more (total committed 300). 9-3 offsuit is one of the worst holdings — poorly connected, unsuited and frequently dominated by a button raiser’s opening range. I'm out of position heads-up, which compounds the problem: I'll face C-bets and difficult multi-street decisions with very little equity. Pot odds (200 to win ~400) require ~33% equity; versus a reasonable button opening range 9-3o has well under that, and a 3-bet bluff here is thin — this hand is not a good candidate to build a pot OOP. With deep stacks and nearly 150 hands done, preserving stack and waiting for a more playable spot is optimal. Fold preflop.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with J♦6♦ — suited, playable and well worth opening in position. Pot is 150 and it’s cheap to raise; with deep ~200BB stacks I want the initiative and some fold equity rather than limp. I’ll open to 3x the BB (to 300) — a standard sizing that pressures the blind and keeps postflop play in position. Postflop plan: c-bet favorable flops and continue with draws or pairs; fold (or seriously tighten) if Elon 3-bets very large preflop. Raise to 300.

Heads-up pre-flop in BB facing a button min-raise to 300 (3x BB). A4o is a standard defensive hand heads-up due to the ace blocker and decent playability. Minni's button range is wide based on history (frequent opens and calls), giving us ~38% equity vs a typical 50% button range. Deep stacks (~200BB) allow post-flop maneuvering. Folding is too tight; 3-betting risks playing OOP with marginal hand when calling realizes equity better. Call the additional 200 (total commit 300) to see flop in position-disadvantaged spot but with good blocker.

Flop board Qh-8d-5s is fairly dry with some broadway potential and gutshots (e.g., 67, 97), but no immediate flush draws. OOP with A4o (ace-high, no pair/draw), equity is marginal (~25-35% vs. Minni's wide button c-bet range of ~50% which includes Ax, broadways, pairs, suited connectors). Ac blocks her strong Ax combos. Deep stacks (~200BB), so preserve stack near tournament end (hand 149/150). Donk betting air OOP is -EV and invites raises. Check to induce bluffs/value bets from her range or realize equity cheaply if she checks back. Plan: check-call small bets (<1/3 pot) with blocker/showdown, fold to large bets; check-raise jam rare polarized spots later if warranted by history of her aggression.

Heads-up on the button with J♦6♦. I opened preflop and Elon's check on this fairly dry Q♥-8♦-5♠ flop — a good spot to take the initiative. I have no pair and only backdoor diamond potential, but in position I can apply pressure and pick up the pot against missed calling ranges (Kx, Ax, small pairs, some air). I’ll make a standard continuation bet ~55–60% pot to fold out hands that have missed and to keep control if called. Plan: take it down if he folds; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn (check-back or barrel only on a diamond/J or other favorable runout); if he raises large I’ll concede (we have very little showdown equity).

Heads-up on flop, OOP with A4o (ace-high, Ac blocker) facing a small 350 bet into 950 pot (~37% pot) from Minni's button range after her min-raise pre-flop. Board Qh-8d-5s is dry rainbow with limited draws (e.g., 97, 76 gutshots, JT), favoring her wide c-betting range (~50% freq from history of aggression). Equity ~30-40% vs that range due to blocker and showdown value. Pot odds excellent (call 350 to win 1300, ~27% needed). Raising polarizes into bluffs/value but OOP deep (~200BB) risks uncomfortable spots; folding too tight given history and small size. Call realizes equity cheaply, induces bluffs on turn/river, fits plan to check-call small bets. Near end (149/150), avoid high-variance jams with marginal hand.

Turn 4s perfectly pairs my 4h, giving me bottom pair with top kicker (Ac) and strong blocker (Ac blocks strong Ax hands like AQ/AK that hit Q or backdoor). Board Qh-8d-5s-4s is coordinated: 6-7 straights completed (4-5-6-7-8), spade flush draws live (two spades), pairs possible. Minni's button range wide (min-raise pre, small flop c-bet 37% pot suggests polarized/value thin), ~40-50% equity now with made pair vs her range. OOP deep stacks (~190BB), pot 1300. Donk betting thin value risks check-raises from her strong hands/draws and folds better equity realizations; check controls pot, induces bluffs/value from wide range (history aggressive), sets up check-raise/fold to big bets. Near final hand (149/150), preserve stack variance-minimalize with showdown value. Check, plan check-call/raise small-medium bets, fold only to huge overbets.

I have J♦6♦ — after c-betting the Q♥-8♦-5♠ flop for 350 I got called. Elon checks the turn (4♠) which does not improve me — my backdoor diamond is gone and I have no pair. Pot is only 1,300 while stacks are ~19k, so there’s little incentive to barrel a thin bluff into a player who has already shown willingness to call the flop (likely Qx, 8x, 5x, small pairs or broadways). My earlier plan was to proceed cautiously on the turn if called; with a non-helpful card I prefer pot control and to see a cheap river or induce a bet I can fold to, rather than investing more with virtually no equity. I’ll check and re-evaluate on the river (bluff only if a diamond/J or a clear read of weakness appears; fold to substantial aggression).

River Jh completes potential broadway straights (e.g., 9T for 8-9-T-J-Q), pairs J for some hands, and pairs the board's 4s indirectly but no trips. My A4o turned into bottom pair top kicker (4h paired 4s, Ac blocker), giving solid showdown value (~40-50% equity vs. Minni's polarized range of Qx/8x/pairs/draws/bluffs from small flop c-bet and turn check-behind, which often indicates medium strength or missed draws). OOP first to act into 1300 pot with deep stacks (~195BB effective), betting thin value risks check-raises from straights/better pairs/flushes and folds out worse; doesn't maximize EV given history of her calling stations. Checking extracts value from worse (e.g., missed spades, Q-high) via her frequent small bets/calls, preserves stack variance-minimal for final hands (149/150), realizes equity cheaply. Plan: check-call small-medium bets (<2/3 pot), fold to large overbets without reads of spew. Optimal for stack maximization.

I flopped this hand and fired a c‑bet that got called, then both of us checked the turn. The river (Jh) pairs my J♦ and turns my air into a medium-strength hand: one pair (jacks) with a weak 6 kicker. Elon’s line (calling flop, checking turn, checking river) looks passive — range likely contains Qx, 8x, 5x, small pairs and some missed draws. Many of those hands (8x/5x/smaller pairs and some worse jacks) will call a modest river bet; Qx and rare two‑pair hands beat me but would also beat me if I check down. With deep stacks and little downside, I want to extract value from the worse portion of his range while keeping the sizing small enough to be called. I’ll make a modest value bet (~1/3–1/2 pot) to target calls from 8x/5x/small pairs and weaker Jx. If he raises big I’ll concede (fold) since my kicker is weak and I’m behind to Qx/two‑pair. Action: bet 500 to maximize thin value while controlling risk.

River with bottom pair top kicker (4s paired, Ac top kicker blocking strong Ax/Qx). Board 5s-Qh-8d-4s-Jh completes T9 straights and adds J pairs, but Minni's line (minraise pre wide range, small flop bet polarized/thin value, turn check medium/missed draw, river small 38% pot bet) includes many worse like weaker Qx/8x/5x/Jx, unpaired Ax, bluffs (missed spades/67 draws). Equity ~45% vs range (beats bluffs/worse pairs, loses to sets/straights but those underrepresented). Pot odds excellent: 500 to win 2300 (~18% needed). Deep stacks (~190BB), hand 149/150; call realizes showdown cheaply, low variance for stack max. History shows Minni calls light/aggressive. Fold too tight, raise risks folding out worse/unneeded commitment OOP. Call.

Heads-up on the button/SB with Qs6s, a suited queen-high hand with good playability and flush potential. Deep stacks (19k effective), pot 150 with 50 already posted. Standard to raise most hands from this position to steal blinds or build pot. Minni has been aggressive historically but this hand is strong enough to proceed. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) applies pressure and has fold equity while keeping pot manageable postflop.

Heads-up vs Elon (button/SB) who opened to 300. I have A♦5♠ in the big blind. The pot is 400 and I need to call 200 more (300 total). Required equity ≈33% — A5o has enough showdown value vs a wide heads-up opening range. I’ll avoid a light 3-bet with an offsuit ace while out of position (small 3-bet is easy to 4-bet or call and puts me in tough spots deep-stacked). I’ll call to realize equity, keep the pot controlled, and look to extract value when I hit an Ace or improvement; fold to large preflop/strong postflop aggression if it occurs.

Heads-up, out of position with A♦5♠ on a relatively dry 3♣ T♣ 8♥ flop and a 600-chip pot. I have decent showdown value vs a wide button opening range but poor multi-street playability and no club backdoor. With deep stacks I want pot control and to realize equity rather than barreling OOP with just an ace-high. I’ll check to keep the pot manageable, induce bluffs, and call small-to-medium continuation bets to see a turn. If Elon fires a large overbet OOP I’ll fold (he usually bets for value and will have many tens/club draws), but if he checks back or makes a modest c-bet I’ll continue (call) and look to extract value if I pair the Ace or improve.

Flop: 3c Tc 8h. Qs6s is queen-high with backdoor spade flush draw and minimal straight potential. As preflop raiser on button, this board hits my range reasonably well (e.g., overcards, broadway), and Minni's check suggests weakness or trapping, but fold equity is high given history where she folds to c-bets often. Deep stacks, last hand of tournament, pot 600 – standard c-bet 1/2 pot for value/protection/deny equity to draws (clubs, 9J). Keeps pressure on calling station tendencies while manageable if called.

Heads‑up, out of position with A♦5♠ on 3♣ T♣ 8♥. Elon opened wide from the button and fired a modest 300 into a 600 pot — I’m getting ~33% pot odds to call (300 to win 900). I have decent showdown value vs a wide c‑betting range (many missed hands, floats, some draws) and little incentive to 3‑bet OOP with an offsuit ace and no club backdoor. With deep stacks I want to keep the pot under control and realize equity; I’ll call the 300 and re‑evaluate on the turn (fold to heavy turn overbets or raise only with a clear read/real value).

Turn brings J♣ completing a three‑club board (3♣ T♣ J♣ 8♥) and improving many parts of Elon’s opening/c‑betting range (pairs, J/T combos and two‑club combos that now have a flush). I hold A♦5♠ — decent showdown value but no club and very little multi‑street playability OOP. Pot is only 1,200 while both stacks are ~20k, so I have no reason to build the pot with an ace‑high. I’ll check to keep the pot small, realize equity, and force Elon to show strength if he wants the rest of the pot. Plan: if he checks back we go to showdown; if he fires a small‑to‑medium bet I will call to see the river (A or blank can still win), but I’ll fold to large polarizing overbets/raises that represent J/T/flush made hands. I only raise here with a very strong read on a bluff.

Heads-up on the final hand (150/150) with deep stacks (~19k effective) and pot of 1200 on the turn. Board: 3c-Tc-8h-Jc (three clubs, connected). Qs6s is queen-high with backdoor spade flush draw, no pair/draw, but blocks Q9 straight. Preflop raise, flop c-bet 300 check-called by Minni (likely weak pair, club draw, or Ax). She checks turn again into scary board (club flush draw completed, straight draws open). History shows Minni calls frequently but folds to continued aggression sometimes (e.g., recent hands). As preflop aggressor in position, betting half-pot (600) applies pressure, gets value from worse Ax/77-99/ draws while denying equity, and has fold equity against her marginal hands. Checking risks giving free card to draws/ giving up pot without showdown value fully realized. Last hand maximizes EV by betting for thin value/protection/fold equity over passive play.

Elon opened wide from the button and has continued with modest sizing (c-bet flop, now 600 into the ~1200 pot). I have A♦5♠ on 3♣ T♣ 8♥ J♣ — ace-high with no club. Clean outs to improve are small (3 aces + 3 fives = 6 outs → ~13% to hit on the river), so pure catch-and-improve odds are short versus the ~25% equity I need (call 600 to win 2400). However Elon's line and bet sizing are not hugely polar — his range contains a fair number of missed hands/bluffs and one-card protection bets that I beat. Raising OOP with just an offsuit ace and no club is too thin (poor fold equity, vulnerable to reraises and club combos). With deep stacks and the final hand of the tournament I want to realize showdown equity and avoid bloating the pot without a real hand. I'll call the 600 to see the river and then re-evaluate: fold to a large polarizing shove/raise if I miss, value-bet or call small bets if I pair my ace, and fold river aggression when appropriate.

Board is 3♣ T♣ 8♥ J♣ Q♣; I hold A♦5♠ (ace‑high, no club). Pot is 2400 and it’s checked to me on the river. With four clubs on board a large portion of Elon’s continuing range contains a club flush (which easily beats my ace‑high), and his line (c‑bet flop, bet turn, then check river) is consistent with both medium made hands and some bluffs. Betting as a bluff here is thin — I have very little fold equity and risk getting called by Qx/Tx/Jx or crushed by any club. Checking keeps the pot small and lets my ace‑high show down against hands that check back (Kx, missed unpaired hands, some bluffs). I’ll check and go to showdown rather than bluff or build the pot with a weak, non‑club ace.

River on the final hand (150/150) heads-up, pot 2400, deep effective stacks (~18k). Board: 3c-Tc-8h-Jc-Qc (four clubs, open-ended straight draws like 9x for 8-9-T-J-Q). Qs6s = top pair queens (Qs + board Qc) with 6 kicker, no club, no straight. Preflop raiser/c-bettor, got two check-calls from Minni (BB caller, wide range). Her triple-check suggests capped range: no nuts (flush would likely bet/raise on turn/river), worse pairs (88-TT-JJ, weaker Qx), ace-high, or missed draws. History: Minni calling station (frequent calls to multiple streets, e.g., hands 103,108,112,120,124,126,129,134,138,146,147) but folds some rivers to pressure. Position + history of folding to aggression supports thin value bet: gets called by worse pairs/A-high/draws, fold equity vs bluffs/marginal. Checking risks her hero-calling better or chopping pot. Optimal: bet ~2/3 pot (1600) for max value vs station while denying improves. Last hand EV-maximizes stack by extracting from worse.

Elon has bet 1600 into a 4000 pot on a river with four clubs on board. I hold A♦5♠ (no club) — a weak ace-high that loses to any club flush and to most Q/J/T pairs, but beats missed hands and some thin value/turn-protection bets. Calling 1600 to win 5600 requires ≈28.6% equity (1600/5600). Given Elon's wide BTN opening and his relatively small river sizing, his range likely contains a mix of made flushes/strong pairs and a non-trivial portion of bluffs/weaker holdings that my ace-high beats. Raising OOP with a non-club ace is too thin (poor fold equity and vulnerable to reraises); folding surrenders too much showdown equity for a modest price. Therefore I will call the 1600 to realize showdown equity and avoid bloating the pot with a marginal hand.
